// \u2500\u2500 PASS SIMULATION HELPERS \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
function getPassAvailability() {
  // Simulate random satellite availability (each pass ~40-70% chance)
  return {
    AO91:    Math.random() < 0.60,
    SO50:    Math.random() < 0.55,
    ISS:     Math.random() < 0.45,
    FUNCUBE: Math.random() < 0.50,
  };
}

function fmtElev() {
  const elevs = [8, 12, 18, 23, 31, 42, 55, 67, 74, 81, 64, 48, 32, 19, 11, 6];
  return elevs[Math.floor(Math.random() * elevs.length)];
}

// \u2500\u2500 MAIN SATELLITE MENU \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
function satelliteMenu() {
  printBlank();
  printDivider();
  print('  \u224b SATELLITE TRACKING & PASS MENU \u224b', 'header');
  printDivider();
  print('  Consulting Heavens-Above.com for your location...', 'system');
  printBlank();

  const avail = getPassAvailability();
  const now = new Date();
  const timeStr = now.getUTCHours().toString().padStart(2,'0') + ':' +
                  now.getUTCMinutes().toString().padStart(2,'0') + ' UTC';

  print(`  Current time: ${timeStr}  |  Sat QSOs logged: ${G.satQSOs}`, 'amber');
  if (G.funCubeOwned) {
    print(`  SDR: ${G.funCubePro ? 'FUNcube Dongle Pro+' : 'FUNcube Dongle'}  [FUNcube telemetry ENABLED]`, 'success');
  }
  printBlank();
  print('  \u2500\u2500 UPCOMING PASSES \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500', 'divider');
  printBlank();

  const passMins = [3, 7, 12, 18, 24, 31];
  SATELLITES.forEach((sat, idx) => {
    const up = avail[sat.id];
    const mins = passMins[idx] || Math.floor(Math.random() * 40) + 5;
    const elev = fmtElev();
    const locked = G.license < sat.licReq;
    const needsDongle = sat.dongleReq && !G.funCubeOwned;
    let statusTag = up ? `  AOS in ~${mins} min  Max elev: ${elev}\u00b0` : '  No pass this window';
    if (locked) statusTag = `  [Requires ${LICENSES[sat.licReq]}]`;
    if (needsDongle) statusTag += '  [FUNcube Dongle required]';
    const cls = locked || needsDongle ? 'danger' : up ? 'success' : 'system';
    print(`  ${sat.name.padEnd(14)} ${sat.type.padEnd(22)} ${statusTag}`, cls);
    print(`    \u2191 ${sat.uplink.padEnd(28)} \u2193 ${sat.downlink || sat.telemetry}`, 'system');
    printBlank();
  });

  print('  \u2500\u2500 SATELLITE INFO & CONTACTS \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500', 'divider');
  printBlank();

  const choices = SATELLITES.map(sat => {
    const up = avail[sat.id];
    const locked = G.license < sat.licReq;
    const needsDongle = sat.dongleReq && !G.funCubeOwned;
    let label = `${sat.name.padEnd(16)} ${sat.full}`;
    if (!up) label += '  [no pass]';
    if (locked) label += `  [need ${LICENSES[sat.licReq]}]`;
    if (needsDongle) label += '  [need FUNcube Dongle]';
    return {
      label,
      fn: () => satPassDetail(sat, up, locked, needsDongle),
    };
  });
  choices.push({ label: 'Visit SDR Shop (FUNcube Dongle etc.)', fn: sdrShop });
  choices.push({ label: 'Back to shack', fn: mainMenu });
  printChoices(choices);
}

// \u2500\u2500 SATELLITE DETAIL & ATTEMPT \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
function satPassDetail(sat, isUp, locked, needsDongle) {
  printBlank();
  printDivider();
  print(`  \u224b ${sat.full} \u224b`, 'header');
  printDivider();
  printBlank();
  print(`  Type:     ${sat.type}`, 'white');
  print(`  Uplink:   ${sat.uplink}`, 'amber');
  print(`  Downlink: ${sat.downlink || sat.telemetry}`, 'amber');
  if (sat.toneHz)    print(`  CTCSS:    ${sat.toneHz}`, 'system');
  if (sat.aprsFreq)  print(`  APRS:     ${sat.aprsFreq}`, 'system');
  if (sat.telemetry) print(`  Telemetry:${sat.telemetry}`, 'system');
  printBlank();
  print(`  ${sat.desc}`, 'white');
  printBlank();

  // Elmer tips
  print('  \u2500\u2500 OPERATING TIPS \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500', 'divider');
  const tip = sat.tips[Math.floor(Math.random() * sat.tips.length)];
  print(`  "${tip}"`, 'amber');
  printBlank();

  if (locked) {
    print(`  \u2717 Requires ${LICENSES[sat.licReq]} license or higher.`, 'danger');
    printBlank();
    printChoices([{ label: 'Back to satellite menu', fn: satelliteMenu }]);
    return;
  }

  if (needsDongle) {
    print('  \u2717 FUNcube-1 telemetry requires a FUNcube Dongle or FUNcube Dongle Pro+.', 'danger');
    print('  The standard RTL-SDR or HackRF will NOT decode FUNcube telemetry frames.', 'system');
    print('  Visit the SDR Shop to purchase the correct hardware.', 'white');
    printBlank();
    printChoices([
      { label: 'Visit SDR Shop \u2014 buy FUNcube Dongle', fn: sdrShop },
      { label: 'Back to satellite menu', fn: satelliteMenu },
    ]);
    return;
  }

  if (!isUp) {
    print('  No pass available in this window. Try again later.', 'system');
    print('  Tip: Check Heavens-Above.com for accurate pass predictions.', 'system');
    printBlank();
    printChoices([{ label: 'Back to satellite menu', fn: satelliteMenu }]);
    return;
  }

  print('  A pass is available! Do you want to attempt a QSO?', 'success');
  printBlank();
  printChoices([
    { label: `Attempt satellite contact via ${sat.name}`, fn: () => doSatPass(sat) },
    { label: 'Read more tips first', fn: () => showAllTips(sat) },
    { label: 'Back to satellite menu', fn: satelliteMenu },
  ]);
}

function showAllTips(sat) {
  printBlank();
  printDivider();
  print(`  \u224b ${sat.name} OPERATING GUIDE \u224b`, 'header');
  printDivider();
  printBlank();
  sat.tips.forEach((tip, i) => {
    print(`  ${i+1}. ${tip}`, i % 2 === 0 ? 'white' : 'system');
    printBlank();
  });
  printChoices([
    { label: `Attempt QSO via ${sat.name}`, fn: () => doSatPass(sat) },
    { label: 'Back to satellite menu', fn: satelliteMenu },
  ]);
}

// \u2500\u2500 SATELLITE PASS EXECUTION \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
function doSatPass(sat) {
  printBlank();
  printDivider();
  print(`  \u224b SATELLITE PASS \u2014 ${sat.name} \u224b`, 'header');
  printDivider();
  printBlank();

  const passMsg = sat.passMessages[Math.floor(Math.random() * sat.passMessages.length)];

  // Print pass sequence with delays
  print(`  [AOS] ${sat.full}`, 'system');
  setTimeout(() => print(`  ${passMsg}`, 'amber'), 400);

  // FUNcube-1 telemetry display
  if (sat.id === 'FUNCUBE') {
    setTimeout(() => {
      printBlank();
      print('  \u2500\u2500 FUNcube Dongle' + (G.funCubePro ? ' Pro+' : '') + ' \u2014 TELEMETRY DECODING \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500', 'header');
      print(`  FUNcube Dashboard v1.9 connected to ${G.funCubePro ? 'Pro+' : 'original'} hardware`, 'system');
      print('  Receiving 145.935 MHz BPSK 1200 baud...', 'system');
      printBlank();
      sat.telemetryData.forEach(d => {
        print(`  ${d.param.padEnd(24)} ${d.value.padEnd(10)} [${d.status.toUpperCase()}]`, 'success');
      });
      printBlank();
      print('  Telemetry frames logged to FUNcube Warehouse database.', 'system');
    }, 800);
  }

  // ISS special handling
  if (sat.id === 'ISS') {
    setTimeout(() => {
      printBlank();
      print('  You hear multiple stations calling on 145.200 MHz...', 'system');
      print('  Doppler correction applied: -2.1 kHz and counting down.', 'system');
    }, 600);
  }

  // SO-50 arming sequence
  if (sat.id === 'SO50') {
    setTimeout(() => {
      print('  Transmitting 74.4 Hz arming tone for 2 seconds...', 'system');
      setTimeout(() => {
        print('  Transponder ARMED. Switching to 67.0 Hz CTCSS.', 'success');
      }, 600);
    }, 500);
  }

  // Determine success
  const antennaBonus = G.antenna * 0.04;
  const radioBonus   = (G.radio + (G.rigBonus || 0)) * 0.03;
  const licBonus     = G.license * 0.05;
  const baseChance   = 0.45 + antennaBonus + radioBonus + licBonus;
  const success      = Math.random() < Math.min(0.92, baseChance);

  setTimeout(() => {
    printBlank();
    print('  \u2500\u2500 CONTACT ATTEMPT \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500', 'divider');

    if (sat.id === 'ISS') {
      doISSContact(sat, success);
    } else {
      doStandardSatContact(sat, success);
    }
  }, sat.id === 'FUNCUBE' ? 2200 : sat.id === 'SO50' ? 1800 : 1400);
}

function doStandardSatContact(sat, success) {
  const contact = sat.contactCalls[Math.floor(Math.random() * sat.contactCalls.length)];
  print(`  You call: "${G.callsign} calling CQ satellite via ${sat.name}..."`, 'cyan');

  setTimeout(() => {
    if (success) {
      print(`  ${contact.call} comes back: "${G.callsign} de ${contact.call},`, 'success');
      print(`  you are ${contact.report} into the bird, ${contact.loc}. QSL via ${sat.name}. 73!"`, 'success');
      printBlank();
      print(`  \u2713 SATELLITE QSO LOGGED: ${contact.call} via ${sat.name}`, 'success');
      print(`  Location: ${contact.loc}  |  Signal: ${contact.report}  |  Mode: ${sat.type}`, 'system');
      const xpGain = sat.xpReward + Math.floor(G.license * 10);
      gainXP(xpGain);
      G.qsoCount++;
      G.satQSOs++;
      G.satLog.push({ sat: sat.name, call: contact.call, loc: contact.loc, report: contact.report });      G.qsoLog.push({ call: contact.call, loc: contact.loc, mode: sat.type,        report: contact.report, band: sat.downlink, ts: new Date().toISOString(), type: 'SAT', sat: sat.name });
      G.morale = Math.min(100, G.morale + 15);
      print(`  +${xpGain} XP \u2014 satellite QSO bonus!`, 'amber');
      updateStats();
      if (G.qsoCount >= 50) checkWinCondition();
    } else {
      print(`  No response heard above the noise. The window is closing fast.`, 'danger');
      print('  Tip: Try a higher-gain antenna or wait for a higher-elevation pass.', 'system');
      G.morale = Math.max(0, G.morale - 3);
      updateStats();
    }
    setTimeout(() => {
      printBlank();
      print(`  [LOS] ${sat.name} has set below your horizon.`, 'system');
      setTimeout(satelliteMenu, 1200);
    }, 1200);
  }, 800);
}

function doISSContact(sat, success) {
  const isAPRS = Math.random() < 0.3;
  const crewContact = sat.crewCalls[Math.floor(Math.random() * sat.crewCalls.length)];
  const regularContact = sat.contactCalls[Math.floor(Math.random() * sat.contactCalls.length)];

  if (isAPRS) {
    print('  Sending APRS beacon on 145.825 MHz...', 'cyan');
    setTimeout(() => {
      if (success) {
        print('  [APRS] ' + G.callsign + '>APRS,RS0ISS*,qAR: Beacon digipeated via ISS!', 'success');
        print('  Your packet was heard from orbit. ISS APRS digipeater confirmed.', 'success');
        print('  Packet visible on aprs.fi \u2014 relayed from 400 km altitude!', 'amber');
        gainXP(sat.xpReward);
        G.qsoCount++;
        G.satQSOs++;
        G.satLog.push({ sat: 'ISS-APRS', call: 'RS0ISS', loc: 'Low Earth Orbit', report: 'DIGI' });      G.qsoLog.push({ call: 'RS0ISS', loc: 'ISS / Low Earth Orbit', mode: 'APRS',        report: 'DIGI', band: '145.825 MHz', ts: new Date().toISOString(), type: 'SAT', sat: 'ISS-APRS' });
        G.morale = Math.min(100, G.morale + 20);
        updateStats();
        if (G.qsoCount >= 50) checkWinCondition();
      } else {
        print('  APRS packet not digipeated \u2014 ISS was too low or digipeater busy.', 'danger');
        updateStats();
      }
      setTimeout(() => {
        print('  [LOS] ISS has set below your horizon.', 'system');
        setTimeout(satelliteMenu, 1200);
      }, 1200);
    }, 900);
    return;
  }

  // Voice contact
  print(`  You call: "${G.callsign} calling NA1SS, do you copy?"`, 'cyan');
  setTimeout(() => {
    if (success && Math.random() < 0.35) {
      // Crew responds!
      const msg = crewContact.msg
        .replace('{call}', G.callsign)
        .replace('{rpt}', crewContact.report);
      printBlank();
      print('  \u2605\u2605\u2605 CREW RESPONSE \u2605\u2605\u2605', 'success');
      print(`  ${crewContact.crew} (${crewContact.call}): ${msg}`, 'success');
      printBlank();
      print(`  \u2713 DIRECT CREW CONTACT: ${crewContact.call} / ${crewContact.crew}`, 'success');
      gainXP(sat.xpReward * 2);
      G.qsoCount++;
      G.satQSOs++;
      G.satLog.push({ sat: 'ISS-VOICE', call: crewContact.call, loc: 'International Space Station', report: crewContact.report });      G.qsoLog.push({ call: (crewContact||regularContact).call, loc: (crewContact||regularContact).loc, mode: 'ISS Voice', report: (crewContact||regularContact).report, band: '145.800 MHz', ts: new Date().toISOString(), type: 'SAT', sat: 'ISS' });
      G.morale = Math.min(100, G.morale + 35);
      print('  Morale +35  |  XP doubled for direct crew contact!', 'amber');
      updateStats();
      if (G.qsoCount >= 50) checkWinCondition();
    } else if (success) {
      // Another station relayed / heard
      print(`  ${regularContact.call} responds via ISS: "${G.callsign} QSL, ${regularContact.loc}. 73!"`, 'success');
      print(`  \u2713 ISS-relayed QSO: ${regularContact.call} / ${regularContact.loc}`, 'success');
      gainXP(sat.xpReward);
      G.qsoCount++;
      G.satQSOs++;
      G.satLog.push({ sat: 'ISS-VOICE', call: regularContact.call, loc: regularContact.loc, report: regularContact.report });      G.qsoLog.push({ call: (crewContact||regularContact).call, loc: (crewContact||regularContact).loc, mode: 'ISS Voice', report: (crewContact||regularContact).report, band: '145.800 MHz', ts: new Date().toISOString(), type: 'SAT', sat: 'ISS' });
      G.morale = Math.min(100, G.morale + 20);
      updateStats();
      if (G.qsoCount >= 50) checkWinCondition();
    } else {
      print('  The ISS is heard but the crew does not answer.', 'danger');
      print('  Crew contacts are rare \u2014 try again on a high-elevation pass.', 'system');
      G.morale = Math.max(0, G.morale - 2);
      updateStats();
    }
    setTimeout(() => {
      print('  [LOS] ISS has set below your horizon at 17,500 mph.', 'system');
      setTimeout(satelliteMenu, 1200);
    }, 1400);
  }, 800);
}

// \u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550
// \u2500\u2500\u2500 SDR SHOP \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
// \u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550

function sdrShop() {
  printBlank();
  printDivider();
  print('  \u224b SDR SHOP \u2014 Software Defined Radio Equipment \u224b', 'header');
  printDivider();
  print('  Specialising in satellite receive hardware.', 'system');
  print('  NOTE: Only the FUNcube Dongle (original or Pro+)', 'white');
  print('  can decode FUNcube-1 (AO-73) telemetry frames.', 'white');
  printBlank();

  if (G.funCubeOwned) {
    const dtype = G.funCubePro ? 'FUNcube Dongle Pro+' : 'FUNcube Dongle (original)';
    print(`  You own: ${dtype}  \u2713 FUNcube telemetry ENABLED`, 'success');
  }
  printBlank();
  print(`  XP available: ${G.xp}`, 'amber');
  printBlank();

  SDR_ITEMS.forEach(item => {
    const owned = G.sdrItems && G.sdrItems.includes(item.id);
    const cls = owned ? 'cyan' : 'white';
    const tag = owned ? ' [owned]' : '';
    print(`  ${item.name.padEnd(30)} ${item.cost} XP${tag}`, cls);
    print(`    ${item.desc}`, 'system');
    if (item.givesFunCube) {
      print('    \u2605 Enables FUNcube-1 / AO-73 telemetry decoding', 'success');
    }
    printBlank();
  });

  const choices = SDR_ITEMS.map(item => ({
    label: `${item.name} \u2014 ${item.cost} XP${G.sdrItems && G.sdrItems.includes(item.id) ? ' [owned]' : ''}`,
    fn: () => buySdrItem(item),
  }));
  choices.push({ label: 'Back to shack', fn: mainMenu });
  choices.push({ label: 'Go to satellite menu', fn: satelliteMenu });
  printChoices(choices);
}

function buySdrItem(item) {
  if (!G.sdrItems) G.sdrItems = [];

  if (G.sdrItems.includes(item.id)) {
    print(`  You already own the ${item.name}.`, 'cyan');
    print(`  ${item.flavor}`, 'amber');
    setTimeout(sdrShop, 900);
    return;
  }

  if (G.xp < item.cost) {
    print(`  Not enough XP! Need ${item.cost}, have ${G.xp}.`, 'danger');
    setTimeout(sdrShop, 800);
    return;
  }

  G.xp -= item.cost;
  G.sdrItems.push(item.id);
  if (item.givesFunCube) {
    G.funCubeOwned = true;
    if (item.givesPro) G.funCubePro = true;
  }
  G.rigBonus = Math.max(G.rigBonus || 0, item.rigBonus);
  if (!G.inventory.includes(item.name)) G.inventory.push(item.name);
  gainXP(0);
  updateStats();

  printBlank();
  print(`  \u2554\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2557`, 'success');
  print(`  \u2551  PURCHASED: ${item.name.substring(0,33).padEnd(33)}\u2551`, 'success');
  print(`  \u255a\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u255d`, 'success');
  printBlank();
  print(`  ${item.flavor}`, 'amber');
  print(`  ${item.note}`, 'system');
  if (item.givesFunCube) {
    printBlank();
    print('  \u2605 FUNcube-1 (AO-73) telemetry decoding UNLOCKED!', 'success');
    print('  You can now decode BPSK telemetry frames from the satellite.', 'success');
    if (item.givesPro) {
      print('  Pro+ hardware gives you improved dynamic range and lower noise.', 'success');
    }
  }
  setTimeout(sdrShop, 1800);
}


// \u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550
// \u2500\u2500\u2500 SATELLITE PASSES \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
// \u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550


// \u2500\u2500 SDR / FUNCUBE SHOP ITEMS \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500

// \u2500\u2500 PASS SIMULATION HELPERS \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500


// \u2500\u2500 MAIN SATELLITE MENU \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500

// \u2500\u2500 SATELLITE DETAIL & ATTEMPT \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500


// \u2500\u2500 SATELLITE PASS EXECUTION \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500



// \u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550
// \u2500\u2500\u2500 SDR SHOP \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500

  { id:'ta57', brand:'Soviet Wired Comms', name:'TA-57 Field Telephone',
    type:'Field Telephone', cost:12, rigBonus:1, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'TA-57 Soviet standard field telephone. Magneto hand-crank, 2-wire line, 15 km range.',
    flavor:'You crank the magneto. The line crackles. Someone answers 8 km away.',
    note:'Most common Soviet field phone. Used from 1957 through today in Russian Army.',
    xpBonus:18, moralBonus:20 },
  { id:'tai43', brand:'Soviet Wired Comms', name:'TAI-43 Field Telephone (WWII)',
    type:'Field Telephone', cost:10, rigBonus:1, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'TAI-43 WWII-era Soviet field telephone. Magneto generator, leather carry case.',
    flavor:'The TAI-43 smells of old leather and Bakelite. 1943 engineering at its finest.',
    note:'TAI = Telefon Armejskiy Induktornyi. WWII workhorse. Still used in training today.',
    xpBonus:16, moralBonus:18 },
  { id:'tap_m', brand:'Soviet Wired Comms', name:'TAP-M Telephone Apparatus',
    type:'Field Telephone', cost:14, rigBonus:1, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'TAP-M portable army telephone with improved audio and 20 km line range.',
    flavor:'Crystal-clear audio up to 20 km. The Soviets over-engineered this one beautifully.',
    note:'TAP-M modernised the TAI-43 for Cold War service. Still in post-Soviet inventory.',
    xpBonus:18, moralBonus:20 },
  { id:'p171_phone', brand:'Soviet Wired Comms', name:'P-171 Field Telephone',
    type:'Field Telephone', cost:16, rigBonus:1, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'P-171 Soviet field telephone with dual-mode local battery or magneto operation.',
    flavor:'Dual mode switches automatically. Battery or magneto — your choice in any condition.',
    note:'P-171 introduced automatic mode selection. Simpler to operate under fire.',
    xpBonus:20, moralBonus:20 },
  { id:'p174_phone', brand:'Soviet Wired Comms', name:'P-174 Field Telephone',
    type:'Field Telephone', cost:18, rigBonus:2, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'P-174 improved field telephone. Enhanced audio, extended range up to 25 km.',
    flavor:'25 km on a single pair of wire. The P-174 reaches the next regiment easily.',
    note:'P-174 used at regimental level. Heavier than company phones but far greater range.',
    xpBonus:22, moralBonus:20 },
  { id:'p276_phone', brand:'Soviet Wired Comms', name:'P-276 Field Telephone Set',
    type:'Field Telephone', cost:20, rigBonus:2, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'P-276 field telephone with built-in line testing and signalling features.',
    flavor:'Line test button confirms the wire is intact before the call even goes through.',
    note:'P-276 adds diagnostics to basic field phone. Standard at battalion HQ.',
    xpBonus:22, moralBonus:20 },
  { id:'ta88_phone', brand:'Soviet Wired Comms', name:'TA-88 Field Telephone (Late Cold War)',
    type:'Field Telephone', cost:24, rigBonus:2, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'TA-88 1988-generation field telephone. Improved weather sealing, DTMF capable.',
    flavor:'DTMF tones on a field phone. The Soviets caught up with Western tech by 1988.',
    note:'TA-88 adds dual-tone signalling for automatic exchange compatibility. IP54 sealed.',
    xpBonus:24, moralBonus:22 },
  { id:'signal_sb', brand:'Soviet Wired Comms', name:'SIGNAL-M Field Switchboard Kit',
    type:'Switchboard', cost:25, rigBonus:2, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'SIGNAL-M compact 6-line manual switchboard. Platoon to company level.',
    flavor:'Six jacks, one board, canvas bag. Platoon HQ in a backpack.',
    note:'SIGNAL-M lightest Soviet switchboard. Platoon commanders carried it personally.',
    xpBonus:22, moralBonus:20 },
  { id:'tas_m_sw', brand:'Soviet Wired Comms', name:'TAS-M Field Switchboard (10-line)',
    type:'Switchboard', cost:30, rigBonus:3, antennaBonus:0, licReq:1,
    desc:'TAS-M portable 10-line field telephone switchboard. One operator, ten subscribers.',
    flavor:'You plug in ten lines. The switchboard lights up. The battalion net is live.',
    note:'TAS-M switchboard connects company telephones to battalion HQ. Manual patch.',
    xpBonus:28, moralBonus:22 },
  { id:'p193_sw', brand:'Soviet Wired Comms', name:'P-193M Field Switchboard (20-line)',
    type:'Switchboard', cost:45, rigBonus:3, antennaBonus:0, licReq:1,
    desc:'P-193M 20-line portable switchboard. Manual and automatic modes. Trunk capable.',
    flavor:'Twenty lines, one board. You manage the whole regiment from a canvas bag.',
    note:'P-193M standard regimental switchboard. Handles both field phone and wire trunk.',
    xpBonus:32, moralBonus:24 },
  { id:'p194_sw', brand:'Soviet Wired Comms', name:'P-194 Switchboard (50-line)',
    type:'Switchboard', cost:70, rigBonus:4, antennaBonus:0, licReq:2,
    desc:'P-194 50-line field switchboard. Divisional HQ telephone exchange.',
    flavor:'The P-194 fills a table. Fifty lines. The division speaks through you.',
    note:'P-194 at divisional level. Requires two operators. Complex but comprehensive.',
    xpBonus:38, moralBonus:26 },
  { id:'pat74', brand:'Soviet Wired Comms', name:'PAT-74 Portable Automatic Telephone Exchange',
    type:'Automatic Exchange', cost:55, rigBonus:3, antennaBonus:0, licReq:1,
    desc:'PAT-74 portable automatic telephone exchange. No operator required — dial direct.',
    flavor:'Dial 3-4-7. It rings immediately. No operator. Automatic Soviet exchange.',
    note:'PAT-74 automated the battalion net. Removed the operator bottleneck.',
    xpBonus:34, moralBonus:24 },
  { id:'at3030', brand:'Soviet Wired Comms', name:'AT-3030 Automatic Exchange (30-line)',
    type:'Automatic Exchange', cost:80, rigBonus:4, antennaBonus:0, licReq:2,
    desc:'AT-3030 30-line automatic telephone exchange for regimental/divisional command.',
    flavor:'Thirty internal extensions. Direct dial. The command net goes electronic.',
    note:'AT-3030 motorised automatic switching. Step-by-step electromechanical exchange.',
    xpBonus:40, moralBonus:26 },
  { id:'p244_t', brand:'Soviet Wired Comms', name:'P-244T Wire Layer Vehicle Reel',
    type:'Wire Equipment', cost:35, rigBonus:2, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'P-244T vehicle-mounted wire laying system. Deploys telephone wire from moving vehicle.',
    flavor:'The P-244T unreels wire at 40 km/h. The phone line follows the attack.',
    note:'Wire laying under fire: the P-244T deploys 10 km of field cable per drum.',
    xpBonus:24, moralBonus:18 },
  { id:'p274m_wire', brand:'Soviet Wired Comms', name:'P-274M Field Cable (500m drum)',
    type:'Wire Equipment', cost:8, rigBonus:1, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'P-274M Soviet standard two-conductor field telephone cable. Per 500m drum.',
    flavor:'One drum, 500 metres. Unroll it, connect it, talk across the battlefield.',
    note:'P-274M is the backbone of Soviet field comms. Rugged rubber insulation.',
    xpBonus:10, moralBonus:12 },
  { id:'vch_spravka', brand:'Soviet Wired Comms', name:'VCh Government Secure Telephone',
    type:'Secure Telephone', cost:120, rigBonus:4, antennaBonus:0, licReq:2,
    desc:'VCh (Vysoko-Chastotnaya) secure government telephone. Top officials only.',
    flavor:'You lift the VCh handset. The line is scrambled at the exchange level.',
    note:'VCh network serves Soviet government and military since 1930s. Frequency multiplex scrambling.',
    xpBonus:45, moralBonus:28 },
  { id:'zas_crypto', brand:'Soviet Wired Comms', name:'ZAS Cryptographic Telephone Terminal',
    type:'Encrypted Terminal', cost:150, rigBonus:4, antennaBonus:0, licReq:3,
    desc:'ZAS (Zasekrechennaya Apparatura Svyazi) encrypted terminal. State-classified scrambler.',
    flavor:'The ZAS warbles to life. Encrypted voice — unintelligible to any interceptor.',
    note:'Multiple ZAS generations: T-219 Yachta, T-231, T-240 series. All state secret.',
    xpBonus:50, moralBonus:30 },
  { id:'fialka_m125', brand:'Soviet Wired Comms', name:'Fialka M-125 Cipher Teletype',
    type:'Cipher Machine', cost:200, rigBonus:4, antennaBonus:0, licReq:3,
    desc:'Fialka M-125 10-rotor cipher machine with integrated teleprinter. Warsaw Pact standard.',
    flavor:'Ten rotors click into position. Every keystroke encrypted by Soviet mechanical genius.',
    note:'Fialka replaced Enigma in Soviet bloc. 10 rotors, bidirectional plugboard, punched tape.',
    xpBonus:60, moralBonus:35 },
  { id:'coral_sec', brand:'Soviet Wired Comms', name:'Coral Secure Telephone System',
    type:'Secure Telephone', cost:130, rigBonus:4, antennaBonus:0, licReq:2,
    desc:'Coral Soviet secure telephone. Analog scrambling for high-command protected comms.',
    flavor:'Coral scrambles your voice at the handset. The exchange only sees encrypted audio.',
    note:'Coral system used by Soviet Navy admiralty and strategic forces for secure voice.',
    xpBonus:46, moralBonus:28 },
  { id:'pskov_sub', brand:'Soviet Wired Comms', name:'PSKOV PSTN Interface Unit',
    type:'PSTN Interface', cost:40, rigBonus:2, antennaBonus:0, licReq:1,
    desc:'PSKOV subscriber unit interfaces military field telephone network with civilian PSTN.',
    flavor:'The PSKOV bridges military wire to the civilian telephone exchange.',
    note:'PSKOV allows military HQ to call civilian numbers without radio exposure.',
    xpBonus:26, moralBonus:22 },
  { id:'p180_modem', brand:'Soviet Wired Comms', name:'P-180 Wideband Crypto Modem',
    type:'Encrypted Modem', cost:110, rigBonus:4, antennaBonus:0, licReq:2,
    desc:'P-180 wideband encrypted modem for high-speed data over field wire at 9600 bps.',
    flavor:'Data flows encrypted at 9600 bps over 4-wire field cable. Cold War data link.',
    note:'P-180 enables digital data over the field telephone network. Command net upgrade.',
    xpBonus:42, moralBonus:26 },
  { id:'p193_amp', brand:'Soviet Wired Comms', name:'P-193 Trunk Line Amplifier',
    type:'Wire Equipment', cost:22, rigBonus:2, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'P-193 line amplifier for extending field telephone range beyond 25 km.',
    flavor:'Insert the P-193 at the midpoint. The signal reaches 50 km now.',
    note:'Cascade amplifiers extend Soviet field phone range across river crossings.',
    xpBonus:18, moralBonus:16 },
  // ══ NORTH KOREAN (DPRK) WIRED COMMUNICATIONS ════════════════════════════
  { id:'kpa_ta57', brand:'DPRK Field Comms', name:'KPA TA-57 Copy (Field Telephone)',
    type:'Field Telephone', cost:10, rigBonus:1, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'North Korean KPA direct copy of Soviet TA-57. Manufactured at Ryongsong factory.',
    flavor:'Identical to the Soviet original. Made in Pyongyang. Crank it. It works.',
    note:'DPRK reverse-engineered Soviet field phones in the 1970s. Still in current KPA service.',
    xpBonus:16, moralBonus:22 },
  { id:'kpa_type88', brand:'DPRK Field Comms', name:'KPA Type-88 Field Telephone',
    type:'Field Telephone', cost:15, rigBonus:1, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'KPA Type-88 field telephone. Domestic design, magneto generator, rubber sealed.',
    flavor:'Type-88. Reliable. Crude. Exactly what a KPA soldier needs in the field.',
    note:'Type-88 = Juche year 88 (1999 CE). DPRK Juche calendar stamps all military equipment.',
    xpBonus:18, moralBonus:22 },
  { id:'kpa_sb6', brand:'DPRK Field Comms', name:'KPA 6-Line Field Switchboard',
    type:'Switchboard', cost:18, rigBonus:2, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'Korean Peoples Army 6-line field switchboard. Domestic production, Soviet-inspired design.',
    flavor:'Six lines patch through the KPA board. Analog. Manual. Reliable.',
    note:'KPA field switchboards follow Soviet design doctrine but are domestically manufactured.',
    xpBonus:20, moralBonus:22 },
  { id:'kpa_exchange', brand:'DPRK Field Comms', name:'KPA Military Automatic Exchange',
    type:'Automatic Exchange', cost:75, rigBonus:3, antennaBonus:0, licReq:2,
    desc:'North Korean military automatic telephone exchange. 30-line step-relay switching.',
    flavor:'Thirty lines. Dial 0-9 prefixes. The KPA command net goes automatic.',
    note:'DPRK uses Strowger-style electromechanical exchanges reverse-engineered from Soviet tech.',
    xpBonus:36, moralBonus:26 },
  { id:'kwangmyong', brand:'DPRK Field Comms', name:'Kwangmyong Intranet Terminal',
    type:'Intranet Terminal', cost:60, rigBonus:2, antennaBonus:0, licReq:1,
    desc:'Kwangmyong (Bright Star) national intranet terminal. Completely closed domestic network.',
    flavor:'You connect to Kwangmyong. The intranet loads. No outside world — only DPRK content.',
    note:'Kwangmyong has zero internet access. Thousands of approved domestic pages only.',
    xpBonus:30, moralBonus:28 },
  { id:'dprk_teletype', brand:'DPRK Field Comms', name:'KPA Teletype Terminal (T-100 Clone)',
    type:'Teletype', cost:35, rigBonus:2, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'Korean Peoples Army teletype terminal. Clone of Soviet T-100/T-63 teleprinter.',
    flavor:'The teletype chatters. Baudot code — the same technology since WWII.',
    note:'KPA teletype network runs Baudot ITA-2 encoding. State messages, orders, grid coordinates.',
    xpBonus:24, moralBonus:20 },
  { id:'dprk_fax', brand:'DPRK Field Comms', name:'KPA Military Fax Machine',
    type:'Fax Terminal', cost:20, rigBonus:1, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'Korean Peoples Army secure fax terminal. Thermal print, encryption module slot.',
    flavor:'The fax machine hums. A map arrives pixel by pixel over the encrypted line.',
    note:'KPA command relies heavily on fax for map overlays and written orders.',
    xpBonus:18, moralBonus:18 },
  { id:'dprk_morse', brand:'DPRK Field Comms', name:'KPA Morse Key and Sounder Set',
    type:'CW Equipment', cost:12, rigBonus:1, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'KPA Morse code key and sounder. Standard issue for signal corps operators.',
    flavor:'Dots and dashes. The KPA signal corps trains every operator on Morse first.',
    note:'DPRK maintains extensive CW capability. HF Morse backup when voice is compromised.',
    xpBonus:16, moralBonus:20 },
  { id:'dprk_pager', brand:'DPRK Field Comms', name:'DPRK Military Paging System',
    type:'Paging System', cost:25, rigBonus:1, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'North Korean military pager and base station. One-way numeric paging via HF/VHF.',
    flavor:'Your DPRK pager vibrates. A 6-digit code. Only you know what it means.',
    note:'DPRK military paging is completely closed. Codes change daily. No commercial access.',
    xpBonus:20, moralBonus:22 },
  { id:'dprk_linecrypto', brand:'DPRK Field Comms', name:'DPRK Line Encryption Device',
    type:'Encrypted Modem', cost:90, rigBonus:3, antennaBonus:0, licReq:2,
    desc:'Korean-made line encryption device for field cable and trunk telephone circuits.',
    flavor:'Plug it inline. The circuit goes dark to outsiders. KPA doctrine: encrypt everything.',
    note:'DPRK military doctrine requires encryption on all command-level telephone circuits.',
    xpBonus:36, moralBonus:26 },
  { id:'dprk_encphone', brand:'DPRK Field Comms', name:'DPRK Encrypted Telephone Terminal',
    type:'Encrypted Terminal', cost:140, rigBonus:4, antennaBonus:0, licReq:3,
    desc:'North Korean encrypted telephone for party and military command. Domestically developed.',
    flavor:'Kim system encrypted line. Only party officials and generals have access.',
    note:'DPRK developed indigenous encryption after losing Soviet technical assistance post-1991.',
    xpBonus:48, moralBonus:30 },
  { id:'dprk_type70', brand:'DPRK Field Comms', name:'DPRK Type-70 Cipher Machine',
    type:'Cipher Machine', cost:180, rigBonus:4, antennaBonus:0, licReq:3,
    desc:'North Korean Type-70 rotor cipher machine. Based on reverse-engineered Soviet/Hagelin designs.',
    flavor:'Ten rotors, domestic plugboard. The Type-70 encrypts the Supreme Commanders orders.',
    note:'Type-70 unknown in the West. Believed rotor machine — captured by South Korea never confirmed.',
    xpBonus:55, moralBonus:32 },
  { id:'dprk_redphone', brand:'DPRK Field Comms', name:'DPRK Red Phone (Supreme Command Direct Line)',
    type:'Hotline Terminal', cost:250, rigBonus:5, antennaBonus:0, licReq:3,
    desc:'DPRK direct line terminal connecting to the Supreme Commander. Red handset. Fiber+encrypted.',
    flavor:'You pick up the red handset. Silence. Then: a voice that commands 1.2 million soldiers.',
    note:'Only a handful exist. Fibre-optic with AES-class encryption. No outside intercept possible.',
    xpBonus:70, moralBonus:40 },

  // ══ UNCLE SKIP'S GREY MARKET ═══════════════════════════════════════════
  { id:'skip_ht_mod', brand:"Uncle Skip's Grey Market", name:'Modified HT (TX unlock chip)',
    type:'Grey Market HT', cost:35, rigBonus:2, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'A baofeng with the TX limits removed. All frequencies, all power. Legally grey.',
    flavor:'Uncle Skip slides it across the counter. No questions asked.',
    note:'Operates 136-174 and 400-512 MHz at full power. FCC would be unhappy.',
    xpBonus:20, moralBonus:14 },
  { id:'skip_amp', brand:"Uncle Skip's Grey Market", name:'RF Linear Amplifier (no type acceptance)',
    type:'Grey Market Amp', cost:80, rigBonus:3, antennaBonus:0, licReq:1,
    desc:'500W PEP linear amplifier. Not FCC type accepted. Not for sale... officially.',
    flavor:'Skip winks. The amp hums. 500 watts. No paperwork.',
    note:'Dramatically improves DX. Also dramatically increases FCC heat if used on amateur bands.',
    xpBonus:28, moralBonus:16 },
  { id:'skip_scanner_mod', brand:"Uncle Skip's Grey Market", name:'Scanner with Cellular Enable',
    type:'Grey Market Scanner', cost:45, rigBonus:2, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'Unblocked scanner. Receives cellular, federal, and blocked public safety bands.',
    flavor:'Every frequency. Every channel. Skip says you did not buy this here.',
    note:'Pre-APCO era cellular decoded. FCC banned sale of these in 1993.',
    xpBonus:22, moralBonus:14 },
  { id:'skip_freq_counter', brand:"Uncle Skip's Grey Market", name:'Surplus Frequency Counter (0-3 GHz)',
    type:'Test Equipment', cost:20, rigBonus:1, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'Military surplus frequency counter. 0-3 GHz. Reads out in Hz.',
    flavor:'Pulled from a decommissioned ECM pod. Calibrated well enough.',
    note:'Invaluable for identifying unknown signals. Skip got 40 of these from a base closing.',
    xpBonus:14, moralBonus:10 },
  { id:'skip_spectrum', brand:"Uncle Skip's Grey Market", name:'Surplus Spectrum Analyser (HP 8558)',
    type:'Test Equipment', cost:120, rigBonus:3, antennaBonus:0, licReq:1,
    desc:'HP 8558B spectrum analyser. 100 Hz-1.5 GHz. 10 MHz IF bandwidth. Old but accurate.',
    flavor:'The CRT glows amber. The spectrum of every signal on the bench appears before you.',
    note:'HP 8558B vintage spectrum analyser — fragile but revelatory. Skip found it at auction.',
    xpBonus:40, moralBonus:18 },
  { id:'skip_milsurp_rx', brand:"Uncle Skip's Grey Market", name:'R-390A HF Receiver (Mil Surplus)',
    type:'Vintage Receiver', cost:65, rigBonus:3, antennaBonus:3, licReq:0,
    desc:'Collins R-390A military HF receiver. 0.5-32 MHz. Legendary sensitivity. 100 lbs.',
    flavor:'The R-390A settles onto the bench. Every tube lights up. The HF bands sing.',
    note:'R-390A considered the finest HF receiver ever built. Used by NSA and military.',
    xpBonus:30, moralBonus:20 },
  { id:'skip_prc77', brand:"Uncle Skip's Grey Market", name:'AN/PRC-77 Military Portable Radio',
    type:'Military Radio', cost:55, rigBonus:3, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'Vietnam-era AN/PRC-77 military portable. 30-76 MHz FM. 4 watts. Waterproof.',
    flavor:'The PRC-77 locks onto frequency. 4 watts of FM into a jungle antenna.',
    note:'Standard US infantry radio 1968-1988. Tough as nails. Skip got a crate of them.',
    xpBonus:28, moralBonus:18 },
  { id:'skip_prc25', brand:"Uncle Skip's Grey Market", name:'AN/PRC-25 Military Portable Radio',
    type:'Military Radio', cost:45, rigBonus:2, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'Vietnam-era AN/PRC-25. Predecessor to PRC-77. 30-76 MHz FM. 1.5 watts.',
    flavor:'PRC-25 — the radio that won Vietnam... or did not. Either way, its here.',
    note:'AN/PRC-25 the most widely used radio in the Vietnam war. Replaced by PRC-77.',
    xpBonus:24, moralBonus:16 },
  { id:'skip_anprc117', brand:"Uncle Skip's Grey Market", name:'AN/PRC-117F SATCOM Radio',
    type:'Military Radio', cost:200, rigBonus:5, antennaBonus:0, licReq:2,
    desc:'AN/PRC-117F wideband SATCOM manpack. 30-512 MHz plus SATCOM. Harris Corp.',
    flavor:'SATCOM lock acquired. The network handshake takes 30 seconds. Then: the world.',
    note:'AN/PRC-117F handles VHF/UHF and MILSATCOM in one package. Serious kit.',
    xpBonus:55, moralBonus:26 },
  { id:'skip_racal', brand:"Uncle Skip's Grey Market", name:'Racal RA3701 HF Receiver',
    type:'Vintage Receiver', cost:50, rigBonus:3, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'Racal RA3701 British military HF receiver. 1-30 MHz. Exceptional selectivity.',
    flavor:'The Racal pulls signals from the noise. British engineering at its Cold War finest.',
    note:'Racal receivers used by GCHQ and British Army. Now surplus. Skip imported these.',
    xpBonus:26, moralBonus:18 },
  { id:'skip_signal_gen', brand:"Uncle Skip's Grey Market", name:'Surplus Signal Generator (HP 8640)',
    type:'Test Equipment', cost:40, rigBonus:1, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'HP 8640B signal generator. 0.5-550 MHz. -145 to +10 dBm. Calibration reference.',
    flavor:'A known-good reference signal anywhere from 500 kHz to 550 MHz. Priceless for alignment.',
    note:'HP 8640B considered the best signal gen of its era. Skip has six of them.',
    xpBonus:16, moralBonus:10 },
  { id:'skip_rtlsdr_bulk', brand:"Uncle Skip's Grey Market", name:'Bulk RTL-SDR Dongles (5-pack)',
    type:'SDR Equipment', cost:25, rigBonus:2, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'Five RTL2832U/R820T2 SDR dongles in a ziplock bag. No paperwork. $5 each.',
    flavor:'Skip dumps a bag on the counter. Five dongles. All working. Probably.',
    note:'RTL-SDR covers 24-1766 MHz. Five means you can run five independent receivers.',
    xpBonus:18, moralBonus:12 },
  { id:'skip_jammer_det', brand:"Uncle Skip's Grey Market", name:'RF Jammer Detector',
    type:'Specialist Equipment', cost:60, rigBonus:2, antennaBonus:0, licReq:1,
    desc:'Detects RF jamming sources by spectrum anomaly analysis. Logs interference events.',
    flavor:'The detector lights up. Something is jamming the 2.4 GHz band in this building.',
    note:'Legally grey — detecting jammers is fine, using them is not. Skip knows the line.',
    xpBonus:24, moralBonus:14 },
  { id:'skip_tap', brand:"Uncle Skip's Grey Market", name:'Telephone Line Monitor (TAP)',
    type:'Specialist Equipment', cost:70, rigBonus:2, antennaBonus:0, licReq:2,
    desc:'High-impedance telephone line monitor. Passively monitors voice on twisted pair.',
    flavor:'Clip it to the line. The conversation plays in your headphones. Very Cold War.',
    note:'Passive monitoring only. Skip has been very clear about not discussing its uses.',
    xpBonus:28, moralBonus:14 },
  // ══ W1OLD'S VINTAGE RADIO EMPORIUM ═══════════════════════════════════════
  { id:'w1old_hq100', brand:'W1OLD Vintage Emporium', name:'Hallicrafters HQ-100 (1957)',
    type:'Vintage HF Receiver', cost:30, rigBonus:2, antennaBonus:2, licReq:0,
    desc:'Hallicrafters HQ-100 communications receiver. 0.5-30 MHz. Tube. 1957.',
    flavor:'You switch on the HQ-100. The tubes warm up. The hum of valves fills the shack.',
    note:'Classic American HF receiver of the late 1950s. Fine selectivity for its era.',
    xpBonus:22, moralBonus:18 },
  { id:'w1old_sx28', brand:'W1OLD Vintage Emporium', name:'Hallicrafters SX-28A Superheterodyne',
    type:'Vintage HF Receiver', cost:45, rigBonus:3, antennaBonus:3, licReq:0,
    desc:'Hallicrafters SX-28A WWII-era military communications receiver. 0.55-42 MHz.',
    flavor:'The SX-28A was used by OSS agents and fleet submarines. Yours now.',
    note:'SX-28A served US Navy and Army during WWII. W1OLD found this in an estate sale.',
    xpBonus:28, moralBonus:20 },
  { id:'w1old_r4b', brand:'W1OLD Vintage Emporium', name:'Collins R-4B SSB Receiver (1965)',
    type:'Vintage HF Receiver', cost:55, rigBonus:3, antennaBonus:3, licReq:0,
    desc:'Collins R-4B mechanical filter SSB receiver. 3.4-30 MHz. Crystal selectivity.',
    flavor:'The Collins mechanical filter snaps in. The selectivity is extraordinary for 1965.',
    note:'Collins R-4B considered a golden-age reference receiver. Built like a tank.',
    xpBonus:30, moralBonus:20 },
  { id:'w1old_s38e', brand:'W1OLD Vintage Emporium', name:'Hallicrafters S-38E (Budget Classic)',
    type:'Vintage HF Receiver', cost:15, rigBonus:1, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'Hallicrafters S-38E entry-level shortwave. 0.54-30 MHz. 5 tubes. 1955.',
    flavor:'The S-38E drift is legendary. But so are the shortwave signals it pulls in.',
    note:'Millions of hams started on the S-38 series. Affordable classic.',
    xpBonus:14, moralBonus:14 },
  { id:'w1old_hk1', brand:'W1OLD Vintage Emporium', name:'Homebrew Crystal-Controlled Transmitter',
    type:'Vintage Transmitter', cost:20, rigBonus:2, antennaBonus:2, licReq:0,
    desc:'Hand-built crystal-controlled CW transmitter. 40m or 80m. 10 watts. Vintage.',
    flavor:'You close the key. 10 watts of CW leaves the homebrew rig on 7.100 MHz.',
    note:'W1OLD built this in 1954. Crystal controlled: one frequency, pure and stable.',
    xpBonus:18, moralBonus:16 },
  { id:'w1old_kt34', brand:'W1OLD Vintage Emporium', name:'Hy-Gain TH-7DX Tri-Band Beam (1970s)',
    type:'Vintage Beam Antenna', cost:50, rigBonus:3, antennaBonus:4, licReq:1,
    desc:'7-element tribander. 10/15/20m. Aluminum boom, stainless steel hardware.',
    flavor:'The old tri-bander goes up on the mast. Three bands from one antenna. Elegant.',
    note:'TH-7DX was the contest standard of the 1970s. W1OLD has had his since 1972.',
    xpBonus:30, moralBonus:18 },
  { id:'w1old_ts520', brand:'W1OLD Vintage Emporium', name:'Kenwood TS-520S HF Transceiver (1976)',
    type:'Vintage HF Transceiver', cost:40, rigBonus:3, antennaBonus:3, licReq:1,
    desc:'Kenwood TS-520S hybrid HF transceiver. 80-10m SSB/CW/AM. 100W. Tubes in PA.',
    flavor:'The TS-520S glows. Tube final warms up. 100 watts of warm HF audio.',
    note:'TS-520S legendary semi-solid-state hybrid. Tube PA in a solid-state radio.',
    xpBonus:28, moralBonus:18 },
  { id:'w1old_dx60', brand:'W1OLD Vintage Emporium', name:'Heathkit DX-60B CW Transmitter (1962)',
    type:'Vintage Transmitter', cost:25, rigBonus:2, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'Heathkit DX-60B novice CW transmitter. 80-10m. 60 watts PEP. Kit-built.',
    flavor:'You key the DX-60B. The power meter swings. Heathkit quality from a kit.',
    note:'DX-60B the most popular Heathkit novice transmitter. W1OLD built his in 1963.',
    xpBonus:20, moralBonus:16 },
  { id:'w1old_receiver51j', brand:'W1OLD Vintage Emporium', name:'Collins 51J-4 HF Receiver (1956)',
    type:'Vintage HF Receiver', cost:70, rigBonus:4, antennaBonus:4, licReq:1,
    desc:'Collins 51J-4 HF receiver. 0.5-30.5 MHz. 1 kHz resolution dial. Military grade.',
    flavor:'The 51J-4 dial lights up. Collins precision engineering from 1956 at your command.',
    note:'Collins 51J-4 used by US military and diplomatic services. Exceptional stability.',
    xpBonus:36, moralBonus:22 },
  { id:'w1old_inverted_v', brand:'W1OLD Vintage Emporium', name:'Classic Inverted-V Dipole (Wire)',
    type:'Vintage Antenna', cost:12, rigBonus:1, antennaBonus:2, licReq:0,
    desc:'Hand-cut inverted-V dipole for 40m. Old-school wire, ceramic insulators, 1960s.',
    flavor:'You pull the wire up to the apex. Ceramic insulators click at each end. Classic.',
    note:'W1OLD cut this in 1965. Still resonant. Some things do not go out of date.',
    xpBonus:10, moralBonus:10 },
  { id:'w1old_bc348', brand:'W1OLD Vintage Emporium', name:'RCA BC-348 WWII Aircraft Receiver',
    type:'Vintage HF Receiver', cost:35, rigBonus:2, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'BC-348 WWII aviation HF receiver. 1.5-18 MHz. 7 tubes. Used in B-17/B-29.',
    flavor:'The BC-348 hummed in bombers over Germany. Now it hums in your shack.',
    note:'BC-348 standard receiver in WWII US Army Air Forces heavy bombers.',
    xpBonus:24, moralBonus:18 },
  { id:'w1old_sp600', brand:'W1OLD Vintage Emporium', name:'Hammarlund SP-600JX Receiver (1950)',
    type:'Vintage HF Receiver', cost:60, rigBonus:3, antennaBonus:4, licReq:0,
    desc:'Hammarlund SP-600JX professional HF receiver. 0.54-54 MHz. 16 tubes.',
    flavor:'The SP-600 is a professional-grade instrument. It shows on every dial.',
    note:'SP-600 used by military, broadcast stations, and intelligence services. Top shelf.',
    xpBonus:32, moralBonus:20 },
  { id:'w1old_globe_scout', brand:'W1OLD Vintage Emporium', name:'Globe Scout 680A CW Transmitter (1958)',
    type:'Vintage Transmitter', cost:22, rigBonus:2, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'Globe Electronics Scout 680A CW/AM transmitter. 6-meter capable. 50 watts.',
    flavor:'The Globe Scout fires up. 50 watts from a beautiful 1958 rig.',
    note:'Globe Scout was a popular club transmitter. W1OLD has had this since 1961.',
    xpBonus:18, moralBonus:14 },
  { id:'w1old_atlas', brand:'W1OLD Vintage Emporium', name:'Atlas 210X Compact HF Transceiver (1975)',
    type:'Vintage HF Transceiver', cost:35, rigBonus:3, antennaBonus:3, licReq:1,
    desc:'Atlas 210X compact HF transceiver. 80-10m SSB/CW. 100W PEP. American made.',
    flavor:'The Atlas is small but serious. 100 watts on every HF band from a lunchbox.',
    note:'Atlas Radio Corp made quality US transceivers in the 1970s. Now collectible.',
    xpBonus:26, moralBonus:18 },
  { id:'w1old_logbook', brand:'W1OLD Vintage Emporium', name:'W1OLD Signed Logbook (1952-1989)',
    type:'Collectible', cost:10, rigBonus:0, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'W1OLD personal logbook 1952-1989. 4,200 QSOs. Every continent. Every mode.',
    flavor:'You open the logbook. 1952: Novice ticket. 1969: DXCC 100 countries confirmed.',
    note:'The history of HF amateur radio in one book. Signed by W1OLD himself. Priceless.',
    xpBonus:15, moralBonus:25 },
  // ══ SOVIET CIVILIAN WIRED COMMUNICATIONS ════════════════════════════════
  { id:'sov_kvartira', brand:'Soviet Civilian Comms', name:'Kvartira Intercommunication System',
    type:'Wired Intercom', cost:5, rigBonus:1, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'Soviet apartment block intercom. Door entry buzzer and intercom speaker. 1970s.',
    flavor:'You press the button. The buzzer sounds. A voice crackles from the door.',
    note:'Kvartira (apartment) intercom standard in all Soviet housing blocks from 1960s on.',
    xpBonus:8, moralBonus:10 },
  { id:'sov_ts819', brand:'Soviet Civilian Comms', name:'TS-819 Telephone Set (Civilian)',
    type:'Civilian Telephone', cost:6, rigBonus:1, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'TS-819 standard Soviet civilian telephone. Rotary dial. Bakelite handset. 1960s.',
    flavor:'The rotary dial clicks through 7 digits. A Soviet operator answers.',
    note:'TS-819 supplied to every Soviet household connected to the PSTN. Millions made.',
    xpBonus:8, moralBonus:10 },
  { id:'sov_ts500', brand:'Soviet Civilian Comms', name:'TS-500 Rotary Phone',
    type:'Civilian Telephone', cost:5, rigBonus:1, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'TS-500 Soviet civilian telephone. Earlier design. Robust Bakelite construction. 1950s.',
    flavor:'The TS-500 is older than the TS-819. Heavier. More satisfying to dial.',
    note:'TS-500 the postwar standard before the TS-819 replaced it. Extremely durable.',
    xpBonus:8, moralBonus:10 },
  { id:'sov_pbu_intercom', brand:'Soviet Civilian Comms', name:'PBU Factory Intercom System',
    type:'Wired Intercom', cost:12, rigBonus:1, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'PBU Soviet industrial intercom speaker network. Wall-mounted, one-way broadcast.',
    flavor:'The factory intercom crackles. Morning announcements. Production quotas. Pravda.',
    note:'Every Soviet factory, school, and institution had a PBU broadcast speaker system.',
    xpBonus:10, moralBonus:10 },
  { id:'sov_radiotochka', brand:'Soviet Civilian Comms', name:'Radiotochka (Wire Radio Receiver)',
    type:'Wire Radio Receiver', cost:8, rigBonus:1, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'Radiotochka — wired radio speaker. Receives 3 state programs over telephone wire.',
    flavor:'You tune the Radiotochka. Program 1: classical. Program 2: news. Program 3: folk.',
    note:'Radiotochka wired radio served 80 million Soviet subscribers. Still used in Russia.',
    xpBonus:12, moralBonus:14 },
  { id:'sov_fistula', brand:'Soviet Civilian Comms', name:'Three-Program Wire Radio Receiver',
    type:'Wire Radio Receiver', cost:10, rigBonus:1, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'Soviet three-program wire radio. Volume knob, program selector, paper cone speaker.',
    flavor:'Program 3 is playing. A string quartet. You turn it up. The wire hisses slightly.',
    note:'Central broadcast over twisted pair. No antenna needed. Unjammable by outside forces.',
    xpBonus:12, moralBonus:14 },
  { id:'sov_aon', brand:'Soviet Civilian Comms', name:'AON Caller ID Display Unit',
    type:'Telephone Accessory', cost:8, rigBonus:1, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'Soviet AON (Automatic Number Identification) display unit. Attachable to any phone.',
    flavor:'The incoming number appears on the LED display. Caller ID, Soviet style.',
    note:'AON units were unofficial accessories — caller ID arrived in USSR before the West.',
    xpBonus:8, moralBonus:8 },
  { id:'sov_pbx100', brand:'Soviet Civilian Comms', name:'ATSK-50 Small Automatic Exchange',
    type:'PBX System', cost:20, rigBonus:2, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'ATSK-50 Soviet small automatic telephone exchange. 50 lines. Institution-level.',
    flavor:'The step relays click as calls route. 50 extensions on a Strowger electromechanical.',
    note:'ATSK series installed in Soviet factories, schools, and collective farms as internal PBX.',
    xpBonus:16, moralBonus:12 },
  { id:'sov_govoritel', brand:'Soviet Civilian Comms', name:'Govoritel Street Loudspeaker System',
    type:'Public Address', cost:15, rigBonus:1, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'Soviet street public address loudspeaker system. Broadcasts state messages citywide.',
    flavor:'The street speakers crackle. An announcement echoes across the city square.',
    note:'Govoritel (speaker) PA networks covered every Soviet city. Emergency broadcast ready.',
    xpBonus:10, moralBonus:12 },
  { id:'sov_selskiy', brand:'Soviet Civilian Comms', name:'Selskiy (Rural) Wire Radio Network',
    type:'Wire Radio Receiver', cost:7, rigBonus:1, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'Soviet rural wire radio network receiver. Reaches collective farms without RF.',
    flavor:'Even the kolkhoz gets Programme One over copper wire. Moscow speaks to everyone.',
    note:'Selskiy wire radio extended state broadcasting to rural areas without transmitters.',
    xpBonus:10, moralBonus:12 },
  { id:'sov_dial_up', brand:'Soviet Civilian Comms', name:'Soviet Modem (Akademset 1200 bps)',
    type:'Data Modem', cost:18, rigBonus:2, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'Soviet Akademset 1200 bps acoustic coupler modem. For research network access.',
    flavor:'The acoustic coupler clamps to the handset. 1200 baud to the Akademset node.',
    note:'Soviet academic network Akademset preceded internet. Researchers used it from 1980s.',
    xpBonus:14, moralBonus:10 },
  { id:'sov_videotext', brand:'Soviet Civilian Comms', name:'Videotext Terminal (Soviet Prestel Clone)',
    type:'Data Terminal', cost:22, rigBonus:2, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'Soviet Prestel-compatible videotext terminal. 40x25 character colour display.',
    flavor:'Videotext pages load over the telephone wire. Weather. Schedules. State news.',
    note:'Soviet videotext was a Prestel clone. Deployed experimentally in Moscow 1980s.',
    xpBonus:14, moralBonus:10 },
  // ══ DPRK CIVILIAN WIRED COMMUNICATIONS ══════════════════════════════════
  { id:'dprk_intercom_apt', brand:'DPRK Civilian Comms', name:'DPRK Apartment Intercom Speaker',
    type:'Wired Intercom', cost:4, rigBonus:1, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'North Korean apartment block intercom and wire radio speaker. State broadcasts only.',
    flavor:'The speaker crackles. The Korean Central Broadcasting Station begins.',
    note:'DPRK apartment intercoms also function as wire radio receivers. Cannot be turned off.',
    xpBonus:8, moralBonus:14 },
  { id:'dprk_radiospeaker', brand:'DPRK Civilian Comms', name:'DPRK Three-Revolution Wire Speaker',
    type:'Wire Radio Receiver', cost:6, rigBonus:1, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'North Korean Three Revolution wire radio speaker. Receives KCBS over copper wire.',
    flavor:'The speaker blares the morning Revolutionary Song. There is no off switch.',
    note:'Three Revolution refers to ideological, technical, and cultural revolutions. Mandatory.',
    xpBonus:10, moralBonus:14 },
  { id:'dprk_rotarydial', brand:'DPRK Civilian Comms', name:'DPRK Rotary Telephone (Domestic)',
    type:'Civilian Telephone', cost:5, rigBonus:1, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'North Korean domestic rotary telephone. Pyongyang exchange subscribers only.',
    flavor:'You dial a number. Only Pyongyang numbers work. The country has few phones.',
    note:'Domestic telephone in DPRK restricted to approved citizens. Maybe 1 million lines.',
    xpBonus:8, moralBonus:12 },
  { id:'dprk_public_address', brand:'DPRK Civilian Comms', name:'DPRK Street PA Loudspeaker Column',
    type:'Public Address', cost:10, rigBonus:1, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'North Korean public address loudspeaker column. Morning marching songs at 6 AM.',
    flavor:'The streets fill with the Workers Party Song at 6:00 AM. Every morning. Always.',
    note:'DPRK PA systems cover all cities and towns. State propaganda 24/7.',
    xpBonus:10, moralBonus:14 },
  { id:'dprk_portable_speaker', brand:'DPRK Civilian Comms', name:'DPRK Portable Wire Radio (Moranbong)',
    type:'Wire Radio Receiver', cost:8, rigBonus:1, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'Moranbong portable wire radio speaker. Receives KCBS state broadcasting over wire.',
    flavor:'Moranbong, named for the hill in Pyongyang. State music wherever you carry it.',
    note:'Portable wire radio popular in DPRK — receive KCBS without an RF receiver.',
    xpBonus:10, moralBonus:14 },
  { id:'dprk_tv_set', brand:'DPRK Civilian Comms', name:'DPRK Mansudae Television (17 inch)',
    type:'Television', cost:15, rigBonus:1, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'North Korean Mansudae brand CRT television. Receives 3 DPRK state channels.',
    flavor:'Channel 1: Korean Central TV. Channel 2: Mansudae Channel. Channel 3: Education.',
    note:'Mansudae brand televisions are domestically manufactured. All channels state-controlled.',
    xpBonus:12, moralBonus:14 },
  { id:'dprk_computer', brand:'DPRK Civilian Comms', name:'DPRK Naenara Computer Terminal',
    type:'Data Terminal', cost:20, rigBonus:2, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'North Korean Naenara (My Country) computer terminal. Kwangmyong intranet only.',
    flavor:'The Naenara boots. Red Star OS loads. Kwangmyong is waiting.',
    note:'Naenara computers run Red Star OS (Juche Linux). No internet. No exceptions.',
    xpBonus:14, moralBonus:12 },
  { id:'dprk_alarm_clock', brand:'DPRK Civilian Comms', name:'DPRK Revolutionary Alarm Clock Radio',
    type:'Wire Radio Receiver', cost:5, rigBonus:1, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'North Korean combination alarm clock and wire radio receiver. Wakes you with KCBS.',
    flavor:'The alarm sounds. The morning news begins automatically. The Party greets the day.',
    note:'Standard DPRK household item. Alarm triggers the state radio at the same time.',
    xpBonus:8, moralBonus:12 },
  { id:'dprk_vcr', brand:'DPRK Civilian Comms', name:'DPRK Illegal VCR + Foreign Video Cache',
    type:'Contraband Electronics', cost:40, rigBonus:1, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'VCR and hidden cache of South Korean and foreign videos. Extremely illegal in DPRK.',
    flavor:'You slot in a smuggled tape. South Korean drama flickers on screen. Worth the risk.',
    note:'Foreign video smuggling is a capital offense in DPRK. This item is strictly fictional.',
    xpBonus:20, moralBonus:20 },
  { id:'dprk_usb', brand:'DPRK Civilian Comms', name:'DPRK USB Smuggle Drive',
    type:'Contraband Electronics', cost:25, rigBonus:1, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'USB drive with foreign media smuggled through the Chinese border. High black market value.',
    flavor:'K-pop on a USB stick. Passed hand to hand. Each copy risking everything.',
    note:'Foreign media on USB is a major industry in DPRK despite severe penalties.',
    xpBonus:16, moralBonus:16 },

  // ══ YAESU FT-710 ECOSYSTEM & ACCESSORIES ════════════════════════════════
  { id:'yaesu_ft710', brand:'Yaesu', name:'Yaesu FT-710 AESS',
    type:'HF/6m Base', cost:195, rigBonus:4, antennaBonus:2, licReq:2,
    desc:'Yaesu FT-710 AESS 100W HF/50MHz SDR-based transceiver. Advanced Evolved Signal System.',
    flavor:'The AESS DSP locks on. The noise floor drops. Weak signals emerge from static.',
    note:'FT-710 successor to FT-991A for HF. SDR-based signal processing, 100W HF/50MHz.',
    xpBonus:44, moralBonus:22 },
  { id:'yaesu_ft710_field', brand:'Yaesu', name:'Yaesu FT-710 FIELD',
    type:'HF/6m Portable', cost:195, rigBonus:4, antennaBonus:2, licReq:2,
    desc:'Yaesu FT-710 FIELD variant. Same AESS transceiver in field-optimised packaging.',
    flavor:'Field day config locked in. 100W HF/6m from a compact package.',
    note:'FT-710 FIELD same radio as AESS, different bundled accessories for portable ops.',
    xpBonus:44, moralBonus:22 },
  { id:'ft710_mars', brand:'Yaesu', name:'MARS MOD FT-710',
    type:'HF/6m Modified', cost:200, rigBonus:4, antennaBonus:2, licReq:2,
    desc:'FT-710 with MARS/CAP modification unlocking transmit outside amateur bands.',
    flavor:'The MARS mod unlocks MARS/CAP frequencies. You now cover 1.6-54 MHz TX.',
    note:'MARS modification for emergency communications coverage beyond amateur allocations.',
    xpBonus:46, moralBonus:22 },
  { id:'m100_yaesu', brand:'Yaesu', name:'M-100 Desktop Microphone',
    type:'Accessory', cost:18, rigBonus:0, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'Yaesu M-100 desktop microphone. Cardioid dynamic element. For FT-DX and FT series.',
    flavor:'Rich audio from the M-100 electret. DX stations comment on your audio quality.',
    note:'M-100 broadcast-style dynamic desk mic with base stand. Yaesu premium accessory.',
    xpBonus:8, moralBonus:6 },
  { id:'atbk_100', brand:'Yaesu', name:'ATBK-100 Automatic Antenna Coupler',
    type:'Accessory', cost:35, rigBonus:0, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'ATBK-100 automatic antenna coupler for FT-897/FT-857 portable operation.',
    flavor:'The ATU clicks through a matching sequence. The long wire resonates on 40m.',
    note:'ATBK-100 allows unbalanced antennas and random wires with Yaesu mobiles.',
    xpBonus:10, moralBonus:6 },
  { id:'ct39_yaesu', brand:'Yaesu', name:'CT-39 CAT Interface Cable',
    type:'Accessory', cost:8, rigBonus:0, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'CT-39 CAT interface cable for computer control of Yaesu transceivers.',
    flavor:'The CAT cable connects. Log software takes control. Frequency reads back live.',
    note:'CT-39 enables FT-8 logging, remote tuning, and digital mode software control.',
    xpBonus:4, moralBonus:4 },
  { id:'yh77sta', brand:'Yaesu', name:'YH-77STA Stereo Headphones',
    type:'Accessory', cost:22, rigBonus:0, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'Yaesu YH-77STA stereo headphones with 3.5mm plug for radio monitoring.',
    flavor:'The headphones go on. The static of HF fills both ears. Immersive.',
    note:'YH-77STA designed for long operating sessions. Comfortable padded cups.',
    xpBonus:8, moralBonus:8 },
  { id:'atas120a', brand:'Yaesu', name:'ATAS-120A Active Tuning Antenna',
    type:'Mobile HF Antenna', cost:85, rigBonus:0, antennaBonus:2, licReq:0,
    desc:'ATAS-120A motorised screwdriver HF mobile antenna. 40m-70cm automatic tuning.',
    flavor:'The ATAS motor whirs. It tunes to 20m in seconds. Mobile HF contacts follow.',
    note:'ATAS-120A auto-tunes via transceiver CAT command. No manual tuning needed mobile.',
    xpBonus:20, moralBonus:12 },
  { id:'atas25', brand:'Yaesu', name:'ATAS-25 Portable HF Antenna',
    type:'Portable HF Antenna', cost:45, rigBonus:0, antennaBonus:1, licReq:0,
    desc:'ATAS-25 compact portable HF antenna. Manual tune 40m-70cm. Backpacking ready.',
    flavor:'The ATAS-25 clips to a fence post. Compact, light, capable on six bands.',
    note:'ATAS-25 designed for HTs and portable rigs. Manual tuning with SWR readout.',
    xpBonus:12, moralBonus:8 },
  { id:'fc40_yaesu', brand:'Yaesu', name:'FC-40 Automatic Antenna Tuner',
    type:'ATU', cost:55, rigBonus:0, antennaBonus:0, licReq:1,
    desc:'FC-40 automatic antenna tuner for FT-857D and similar. 100W, wire antenna capable.',
    flavor:'The FC-40 cycles through capacitors and inductors. Match found. Power flows.',
    note:'FC-40 pairs with FT-857D for field operation with random wires up to 300 ohms.',
    xpBonus:14, moralBonus:8 },
  { id:'y_acc_1', brand:'Yaesu', name:'Y-ACC-1 Accessory Cable Set',
    type:'Accessory', cost:5, rigBonus:0, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'Y-ACC-1 accessory cable bundle for Yaesu transceivers. Audio, data, control cables.',
    flavor:'The right cable is finally in the box. Everything connects first try.',
    note:'Y-ACC-1 assortment covers AF in/out, PKT, CAT, and linear amp keying.',
    xpBonus:3, moralBonus:3 },
  { id:'fh2_yaesu', brand:'Yaesu', name:'FH-2 Remote Control Head',
    type:'Accessory', cost:28, rigBonus:0, antennaBonus:0, licReq:1,
    desc:'FH-2 wireless remote control for Yaesu FT-DX series transceivers.',
    flavor:'Control the rig from across the shack. Change frequency from the couch.',
    note:'FH-2 uses 2.4 GHz link for remote VFO control, memory access, and band select.',
    xpBonus:8, moralBonus:6 },
  { id:'mhg1_yaesu', brand:'Yaesu', name:'MHG-1 Microphone Hanger',
    type:'Accessory', cost:4, rigBonus:0, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'MHG-1 desktop microphone hanger stand for Yaesu hand microphones.',
    flavor:'The mic hangs cleanly on the MHG-1 bracket beside the rig.',
    note:'Simple but useful. Keeps mic cable tidy and accessible.',
    xpBonus:2, moralBonus:3 },
  { id:'m1_ref_mic', brand:'Yaesu', name:'M-1 Reference Microphone',
    type:'Accessory', cost:60, rigBonus:0, antennaBonus:0, licReq:1,
    desc:'Yaesu M-1 reference desktop electret microphone. Studio-quality audio for FT-DX.',
    flavor:'Broadcast clarity on HF. DX stations ask what studio you are operating from.',
    note:'M-1 professional electret desk mic. Cardioid pattern, flat response 100Hz-10kHz.',
    xpBonus:14, moralBonus:8 },
  { id:'scu_lan10', brand:'Yaesu', name:'SCU-LAN10 LAN Remote Unit',
    type:'Remote Operations', cost:45, rigBonus:0, antennaBonus:0, licReq:1,
    desc:'SCU-LAN10 LAN remote operation unit for FT-DX series. Full remote via Ethernet.',
    flavor:'The SCU-LAN10 connects. You operate the FT-DX10 from a laptop across the house.',
    note:'SCU-LAN10 enables wired LAN remote control. Audio and CAT over Ethernet.',
    xpBonus:12, moralBonus:8 },
  { id:'ssm75e', brand:'Yaesu', name:'SSM-75E Clip Microphone',
    type:'Accessory', cost:15, rigBonus:0, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'SSM-75E lapel/clip electret microphone for handheld Yaesu transceivers.',
    flavor:'Hands-free operation. The SSM-75E clips to your collar. You roam the shack.',
    note:'Useful for VHF/UHF portable where PTT is still pressed but mic hands-free.',
    xpBonus:5, moralBonus:4 },
  { id:'m70_yaesu', brand:'Yaesu', name:'M-70 Hand Microphone',
    type:'Accessory', cost:18, rigBonus:0, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'M-70 Yaesu hand microphone with UP/DOWN buttons for frequency control.',
    flavor:'Classic Yaesu hand mic feel. Buttons on the side change frequency on the fly.',
    note:'M-70 works with FT-991A, FT-710, FT-DX10 and others. Standard fit.',
    xpBonus:6, moralBonus:4 },
  { id:'m90d_yaesu', brand:'Yaesu', name:'M-90D Hand Microphone (DTMF)',
    type:'Accessory', cost:24, rigBonus:0, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'M-90D hand microphone with DTMF keypad for tone autopatch and control.',
    flavor:'DTMF tones on the mic keypad dial into the autopatch. Very 1990s. Very useful.',
    note:'M-90D adds 12-key DTMF pad to standard hand mic for repeater access.',
    xpBonus:7, moralBonus:5 },
  { id:'m90ms', brand:'Yaesu', name:'M-90MS Mobile Hand Microphone',
    type:'Accessory', cost:22, rigBonus:0, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'M-90MS mobile hand microphone for FTM series mobile radios.',
    flavor:'Solid construction, coiled cord, standard 8-pin Yaesu connector.',
    note:'Standard mobile mic for FTM-400, FTM-500DR and similar mobiles.',
    xpBonus:6, moralBonus:4 },
  { id:'smb209', brand:'Yaesu', name:'SMB-209 Speaker Microphone',
    type:'Accessory', cost:20, rigBonus:0, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'SMB-209 combo speaker-microphone for Yaesu HT radios.',
    flavor:'The speaker-mic clips to your shoulder. Hands-free monitoring all day.',
    note:'SMB-209 for VX and FT HT series. IP54 rated version available.',
    xpBonus:6, moralBonus:5 },
  { id:'nifty_ft710', brand:'Yaesu', name:'NIFTY FT-710 AESS Quick Reference',
    type:'Reference', cost:5, rigBonus:0, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'NIFTY! Mini-manual quick reference guide for the Yaesu FT-710 AESS.',
    flavor:'All 247 menu items on a laminated card. You finally understand the DSP settings.',
    note:'NIFTY quick reference cards are beloved by hams for decoding complex menu systems.',
    xpBonus:5, moralBonus:6 },
  { id:'ft710_cover', brand:'Yaesu', name:'Yaesu FT-710/SP-40 Dust Cover',
    type:'Accessory', cost:8, rigBonus:0, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'Custom-fit dust cover for FT-710 AESS with optional SP-40 speaker.',
    flavor:'The cover goes on. The shack looks tidy. W1OLD would approve.',
    note:'Fitted covers protect radio surfaces and VFO knobs from dust buildup.',
    xpBonus:3, moralBonus:4 },
  { id:'radioday_ft710', brand:'Yaesu', name:'Radio Today FT-710 Guide Book',
    type:'Reference', cost:12, rigBonus:0, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'Radio Today comprehensive guide to the Yaesu FT-710. Full feature coverage.',
    flavor:'You read chapter 4 on AESS settings. The radio makes more sense now.',
    note:'Companion guide by Radio Today Australia. Third-party books often better than manual.',
    xpBonus:6, moralBonus:7 },
  { id:'yps_ft710_usb', brand:'Yaesu', name:'YPS-FT710-USB USB Programming Set',
    type:'Accessory', cost:12, rigBonus:0, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'YPS-FT710-USB USB programming cable and ADMS software for FT-710.',
    flavor:'Drag and drop memories from ADMS to the rig. 99 memories filled in 2 minutes.',
    note:'ADMS programming software allows backup and batch edit of all FT-710 memories.',
    xpBonus:5, moralBonus:4 },
  { id:'sp40_yaesu', brand:'Yaesu', name:'SP-40 External Speaker',
    type:'Accessory', cost:38, rigBonus:0, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'Yaesu SP-40 external speaker with audio filter. Improves FT-710 audio output.',
    flavor:'The SP-40 adds bass and clarity. The FT-710 sounds like a studio monitor.',
    note:'SP-40 includes audio filter for enhanced SSB intelligibility and CW clarity.',
    xpBonus:10, moralBonus:8 },
  // ══ YAESU HF TRANSCEIVERS ═══════════════════════════════════════════════
  { id:'yaesu_ftdx101', brand:'Yaesu', name:'Yaesu FTDX101D',
    type:'HF/6m Base', cost:420, rigBonus:5, antennaBonus:3, licReq:2,
    desc:'Yaesu FTDX101D flagship 100W HF/50MHz SDR transceiver. Three roofing filters.',
    flavor:'The FTDX101D waterfall is real-time. The filters are surgical. This is the pinnacle.',
    note:'FTDX101D flagship 2019-present. Dual SDR receivers, 3kHz roofing filter, best DSP.',
    xpBonus:85, moralBonus:32 },
  { id:'yaesu_ftdx10', brand:'Yaesu', name:'Yaesu FTDX10',
    type:'HF/6m Base', cost:320, rigBonus:5, antennaBonus:3, licReq:2,
    desc:'Yaesu FTDX10 mid-range SDR HF/6m. Roofing filter, full SDR waterfall display.',
    flavor:'The roofing filter bites. Contested signals separate. The FTDX10 delivers.',
    note:'FTDX10 brings FTDX101 technology to mid-market. Current production 2021+.',
    xpBonus:75, moralBonus:30 },
  { id:'yaesu_ft450', brand:'Yaesu', name:'Yaesu FT-450D',
    type:'HF/6m Base', cost:140, rigBonus:3, antennaBonus:2, licReq:2,
    desc:'Yaesu FT-450D compact 100W HF/6m transceiver. Built-in ATU. Budget HF base.',
    flavor:'FT-450D powers up. Compact. 100W. Built-in tuner. First real HF rig feel.',
    note:'FT-450D popular starter HF base. Built-in ATU, IF DSP. Discontinued 2019.',
    xpBonus:36, moralBonus:18 },
  { id:'yaesu_ft857', brand:'Yaesu', name:'Yaesu FT-857D',
    type:'HF/VHF/UHF Mobile', cost:155, rigBonus:3, antennaBonus:2, licReq:2,
    desc:'Yaesu FT-857D all-band 100W mobile. HF through 70cm. The compact all-bander.',
    flavor:'HF, VHF, UHF from a rig the size of a paperback. The FT-857D is legendary.',
    note:'FT-857D world smallest all-band 100W transceiver. Discontinued but widely used.',
    xpBonus:38, moralBonus:18 },
  { id:'yaesu_ft897', brand:'Yaesu', name:'Yaesu FT-897D',
    type:'HF/VHF/UHF Portable', cost:165, rigBonus:3, antennaBonus:2, licReq:2,
    desc:'Yaesu FT-897D portable all-band transceiver with internal battery option.',
    flavor:'Battery in the base. Antenna on top. FT-897D runs field day without shore power.',
    note:'FT-897D the original self-contained all-band portable. Battery option is unique.',
    xpBonus:40, moralBonus:18 },
  { id:'yaesu_ft100d', brand:'Yaesu', name:'Yaesu FT-100D',
    type:'HF/VHF/UHF Compact', cost:120, rigBonus:3, antennaBonus:2, licReq:2,
    desc:'Yaesu FT-100D compact all-band transceiver. HF through 70cm. 100W HF.',
    flavor:'The FT-100D is tiny but reaches everywhere. HF through 70cm from a small box.',
    note:'FT-100D discontinued but revered. Compact design influenced later mobile designs.',
    xpBonus:32, moralBonus:16 },
  { id:'yaesu_ft991', brand:'Yaesu', name:'Yaesu FT-991',
    type:'HF/VHF/UHF Base', cost:190, rigBonus:4, antennaBonus:2, licReq:2,
    desc:'Yaesu FT-991 original version. All-band all-mode 100W HF through 70cm.',
    flavor:'The original FT-991 before the A suffix. All bands, all modes, one box.',
    note:'FT-991 (non-A) lacks the updated IF DSP of the 991A but same coverage.',
    xpBonus:42, moralBonus:20 },
  { id:'yaesu_ft3000m', brand:'Yaesu', name:'Yaesu FT-3000M Mobile',
    type:'VHF/UHF Mobile', cost:85, rigBonus:2, antennaBonus:2, licReq:1,
    desc:'Yaesu FT-3000M 50W dual-band mobile. VHF/UHF, CTCSS encode/decode.',
    flavor:'The FT-3000M mounts under the dash. Full duplex on local repeaters.',
    note:'FT-3000M vintage dual-band mobile. Predecessor to FTM series. Still solid.',
    xpBonus:22, moralBonus:14 },
  { id:'yaesu_ft8500', brand:'Yaesu', name:'Yaesu FT-8500',
    type:'VHF/UHF Dual Receive', cost:90, rigBonus:2, antennaBonus:2, licReq:1,
    desc:'Yaesu FT-8500 dual-band true dual-receive mobile. Monitor two channels simultaneously.',
    flavor:'Both VFOs active simultaneously. Nothing escapes the FT-8500.',
    note:'FT-8500 true dual-receive — both VHF and UHF active at once.',
    xpBonus:24, moralBonus:14 },
  // ══ OTHER RADIOS ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  { id:'uniden_adv25', brand:'Uniden', name:'Uniden ADV25-2 UHF Handheld (2-pack)',
    type:'HT Pack', cost:45, rigBonus:1, antennaBonus:0, licReq:0,
    desc:'Uniden ADV25-2 2W UHF 477 MHz CB handheld pair. Australian UHF CB standard.',
    flavor:'The pair powers up on Ch40. The ADV25-2 is the Aussie bush-bashing radio.',
    note:'UHF CB 477 MHz is the Australian equivalent of US FRS/GMRS. 80 channels.',
    xpBonus:14, moralBonus:10 },
  { id:'yaesu_991a_2', brand:'Yaesu', name:'Yaesu FT-991A HF/VHF/UHF',
    type:'HF/VHF/UHF Base', cost:220, rigBonus:4, antennaBonus:2, licReq:2,
    desc:'Yaesu FT-991A 100W all-band transceiver. HF through 70cm with C4FM digital.',
    flavor:'One rig, all bands. HF DX in the morning, 2m FM in the afternoon.',
    note:'FT-991A current production all-band flagship. C4FM Fusion digital built in.',
    xpBonus:44, moralBonus:22 },
  { id:'yaesu_ftm500dr_2', brand:'Yaesu', name:'Yaesu FTM-500DR Dual Band Mobile',
    type:'VHF/UHF Mobile', cost:210, rigBonus:3, antennaBonus:2, licReq:1,
    desc:'Yaesu FTM-500DR 50W dual-band mobile with C4FM, APRS, and large touchscreen.',
    flavor:'The FTM-500DR touchscreen swipes through APRS contacts. Wires-X link established.',
    note:'FTM-500DR current flagship dual-band mobile. Full APRS, Wires-X, GM mode.',
    xpBonus:40, moralBonus:20 },
  { id:'xiegu_x6100', brand:'Xiegu', name:'Xiegu X6100 HF Portable SDR',
    type:'HF Portable SDR', cost:195, rigBonus:4, antennaBonus:3, licReq:2,
    desc:'Xiegu X6100 4W HF/6m all-mode SDR portable with built-in battery and tuner.',
    flavor:'The X6100 fits in a jacket pocket. Full HF coverage, SDR waterfall, built-in battery.',
    note:'X6100 competes with IC-705. Smaller, cheaper, built-in battery. SDR-based.',
