    nation:"Russia", desc:"Russian VLF station RDL. Part of RSDN strategic navigation network.",
    sound:"Pulsed VLF at 16.4 kHz. Russian MSK navigation format distinguishable from NATO.",
    detail:"Network node in the Russian Alpha long-wave navigation system." },
];

// \u2500\u2500 MILITARY SIGNAL INTERCEPTION EVENT \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
function checkMilSignal(band) {
  if (!G.milLog) G.milLog = [];

  // Pick a signal weighted by rarity
  const avail = MIL_SIGNALS.filter(s => {
    const alreadyFound = G.milLog.some(m => m.name === s.name);
    const roll = Math.random();
    return roll < s.rarity * 0.5;
  });
  if (avail.length === 0) return;
  const sig = avail[Math.floor(Math.random() * avail.length)];

  G.milSigsHeard = (G.milSigsHeard || 0) + 1;

  printBlank();
  print('  \u25bc\u25bc\u25bc ANOMALOUS SIGNAL DETECTED \u25bc\u25bc\u25bc', 'danger');
  print('  \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500', 'divider');
  printBlank();
  print(`  SIGNAL: ${sig.name}`, 'amber');
  print(`  FREQ:   ${sig.freq}`, 'system');
  print(`  CLASS:  ${sig.cls}  |  NATION: ${sig.nation}`, 'system');
  printBlank();
  print(`  ${sig.desc}`, 'white');
  printBlank();
  print('  AUDIO SIGNATURE:', 'header');
  print(`  "${sig.sound}"`, 'amber');
  printBlank();
  print('  INTELLIGENCE NOTE:', 'header');
  print(`  ${sig.detail}`, 'system');
  printBlank();
  print(`  Signal logged to your Military Intercept Log. (#${G.milSigsHeard} total)`, 'success');

  G.milLog.push({
    name: sig.name, freq: sig.freq, cls: sig.cls,
    nation: sig.nation, ts: new Date().toISOString(),
    desc: sig.desc, sound: sig.sound,
  });
  gainXP(8);
  autoSave();
  updateStats();
}


// \u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550
// \u2500\u2500\u2500 SATELLITE MONITORING STATION \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
// \u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550

const SAT_MON_CATS = [
  'Navigation Constellations',
  'Weather & Earth Obs',
  'Amateur & CubeSat',
  'Commercial & IoT',
  'Military & Government',
  'Historic & Legacy',
];

// equipment tiers: 0=any, 1=SDR dongle, 2=dish/LHCP, 3=special
const SAT_MON = [

  // \u2550\u2550 NAVIGATION CONSTELLATIONS \u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550
  { id:'gps',       cat:'Navigation Constellations', name:'GPS (Navstar)',
    nation:'USA', freq:'L1 1575.42 MHz / L2 1227.60 MHz / L5 1176.45 MHz',
    modulation:'BPSK / QPSK DSSS', equip:0,
    desc:'US DoD Global Positioning System. 31 operational Block III satellites in MEO at 20,200 km.',
    rx:'L1 C/A BPSK(1) spreading code audible as broadband noise on 1575.42 MHz.',
    note:'24-satellite baseline, 31 operational. CONUS/global coverage. Selective Availability removed 2000.',
    xpBonus:12, moralBonus:10 },

  { id:'glonass',   cat:'Navigation Constellations', name:'GLONASS',
    nation:'Russia', freq:'L1 1598.0625-1605.375 MHz / L2 1242-1249 MHz',
    modulation:'FDMA BPSK / CDMA', equip:0,
    desc:'Russian GNSS. 24 satellites in MEO at 19,100 km. FDMA distinguishes satellites.',
    rx:'Each satellite on unique L1/L2 carrier \u2014 tuning across 1598-1605 MHz reveals individual birds.',
    note:'GLONASS-M and GLONASS-K satellites. Accuracy comparable to GPS. Required on Russian territory.',
    xpBonus:12, moralBonus:10 },

  { id:'galileo',   cat:'Navigation Constellations', name:'Galileo',
    nation:'EU', freq:'E1 1575.42 MHz / E5a 1176.45 MHz / E5b 1207.14 MHz / E6 1278.75 MHz',
    modulation:'BOC / MBOC DSSS', equip:0,
    desc:'EU civilian GNSS. 26 operational FOC satellites at 23,222 km MEO. High-accuracy open service.',
    rx:'E1 BOC(1,1) signal shares L1 band with GPS. Distinct modulation shapes visible on SDR.',
    note:'Full operational capability 2016. 1 cm accuracy with authentication in OS-NMA service.',
    xpBonus:14, moralBonus:12 },

  { id:'beidou',    cat:'Navigation Constellations', name:'BeiDou (BDS)',
    nation:'China', freq:'B1 1561.098 MHz / B1C 1575.42 MHz / B2 1207.14 MHz / B3 1268.52 MHz',
    modulation:'QPSK / BOC DSSS', equip:0,
    desc:'Chinese GNSS. BDS-3 global system: 30 MEO + 3 IGSO + 3 GEO. Global since 2020.',
    rx:'B1I QPSK(2) on 1561.098 MHz. BDS-3 also shares L1/E1 band. Strong signals from GEO birds.',
    note:'BDS-3 GEO satellites at fixed positions over Asia-Pacific region \u2014 always visible there.',
    xpBonus:14, moralBonus:12 },

  { id:'qzss',      cat:'Navigation Constellations', name:'QZSS',
    nation:'Japan', freq:'L1 1575.42 MHz / L2 1227.60 MHz / L5 1176.45 MHz / L6 1278.75 MHz',
    modulation:'BPSK / BOC DSSS', equip:0,
    desc:'Japanese Quasi-Zenith Satellite System. 4 satellites in highly elliptical orbits over Japan.',
    rx:'QZSS L1C/A and L1S augmentation signals on 1575.42 MHz. QZS-1R always near zenith in Japan.',
    note:'Augments GPS over Japan and Pacific. L6 carries CLAS cm-accuracy augmentation service.',
    xpBonus:10, moralBonus:8 },

  { id:'irnss',     cat:'Navigation Constellations', name:'NavIC / IRNSS',
    nation:'India', freq:'L5 1176.45 MHz / S-band 2492.028 MHz',
    modulation:'BPSK-R DSSS', equip:0,
    desc:'Indian Regional Navigation Satellite System. 7 satellites. Coverage: Indian subcontinent + 1500 km.',
    rx:'L5 SPS signal on 1176.45 MHz. S-band RS signal on 2492.028 MHz for government use.',
    note:'ISRO operational since 2018. Renamed NavIC. All 7 satellites in GEO/GSO orbits.',
    xpBonus:10, moralBonus:8 },

  { id:'rnss',      cat:'Navigation Constellations', name:'RNSS (Generic)',
    nation:'Various', freq:'1164-1215 / 1559-1610 MHz',
    modulation:'BPSK / BOC / QPSK DSSS', equip:0,
    desc:'General Radio Navigation Satellite Service band. All GNSS constellations share these allocations.',
    rx:'Wideband SDR across 1559-1610 MHz reveals all GNSS signals simultaneously on the waterfall.',
    note:'ITU RNSS allocations protect all navigation satellites from interference. Primary and secondary.',
    xpBonus:6, moralBonus:5 },

  // \u2550\u2550 WEATHER & EARTH OBSERVATION \u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550
  { id:'noaa_apt',  cat:'Weather & Earth Obs', name:'NOAA APT',
    nation:'USA', freq:'137.500 / 137.620 MHz',
    modulation:'FM APT 2400 Hz subcarrier', equip:0,
    desc:'NOAA POES Automatic Picture Transmission. Real-time weather imagery from NOAA-15/18/19.',
    rx:'Distinctive birdlike chirping FM signal audible on 137.5 or 137.620 MHz during passes.',
    note:'Decode with WXtoImg or Noaa-apt. A crossed dipole and RTL-SDR is all you need.',
    xpBonus:18, moralBonus:16 },

  { id:'noaa_dsb',  cat:'Weather & Earth Obs', name:'NOAA DSB',
    nation:'USA', freq:'137.350 MHz',
    modulation:'BPSK 8320 bps', equip:1,
    desc:'NOAA Direct Sounder Broadcast. Atmospheric sounding data from POES satellites.',
    rx:'BPSK data stream on 137.35 MHz. Narrower than APT, requires SDR and DSB decoder.',
    note:'Contains temperature and humidity profiles from the TOVS sounder instrument.',
    xpBonus:12, moralBonus:8 },

  { id:'noaa_hrpt', cat:'Weather & Earth Obs', name:'NOAA HRPT',
    nation:'USA', freq:'1698.0 / 1702.5 / 1707.0 MHz',
    modulation:'QPSK 665.4 kbps', equip:2,
    desc:'NOAA High Resolution Picture Transmission. Full resolution AVHRR imagery at L-band.',
    rx:'Requires LHCP dish antenna (60+ cm) and L-band LNA. QPSK signal at 665 kbps.',
    note:'Images at 1.1 km resolution vs 4 km for APT. Setup complexity rewarded by stunning results.',
    xpBonus:30, moralBonus:22 },

  { id:'noaa_itos', cat:'Weather & Earth Obs', name:'NOAA ITOS HRPT (Legacy)',
    nation:'USA', freq:'1698.0 MHz',
    modulation:'PCM/PM HRPT legacy', equip:2,
    desc:'Legacy NOAA ITOS series HRPT format. Historical format from early TIROS/ITOS satellites.',
    rx:'Older HRPT frame format. Some legacy decoders required for full interpretation.',
    note:'Historical interest. Understanding ITOS format illuminates the evolution of HRPT standards.',
    xpBonus:14, moralBonus:10 },

  { id:'noaa_apt_oddity', cat:'Weather & Earth Obs', name:'NOAA APT Oddity',
    nation:'USA', freq:'137.x MHz',
    modulation:'Anomalous APT signal', equip:0,
    desc:'Unusual anomalous signals on the NOAA APT frequencies. Interference, intermod, or unknown origin.',
    rx:'Sounds like APT but image is corrupted, patterned, or shows unexpected content.',
    note:'APT oddities reported by wx-sat community include ghost passes, interference patterns, unknown carriers.',
    xpBonus:8, moralBonus:12 },

  { id:'meteor_lrpt', cat:'Weather & Earth Obs', name:'METEOR-M LRPT',
    nation:'Russia', freq:'137.100 / 137.900 MHz',
    modulation:'QPSK LRPT 72 kbps', equip:1,
    desc:'Russian METEOR-M2 Low Rate Picture Transmission. Higher quality than APT on VHF.',
    rx:'QPSK at 72 kbps on 137.1 MHz. Decode with LRPT-Decoder or SatDump. Better resolution than APT.',
    note:'METEOR-M2-3 operational 2023. 1 km resolution colour imagery from Russian polar orbiter.',
    xpBonus:22, moralBonus:18 },

  { id:'meteor_hrpt', cat:'Weather & Earth Obs', name:'METEOR-M HRPT',
    nation:'Russia', freq:'1700 MHz',
    modulation:'QPSK 665 kbps', equip:2,
    desc:'METEOR-M High Resolution Picture Transmission at L-band. Full MSU-MR resolution.',
    rx:'Similar to NOAA HRPT. LHCP dish, L-band LNA, and wideband SDR needed.',
    note:'MSU-MR instrument: 1 km visible, 4 km IR imagery. Russian meteorological standard.',
    xpBonus:28, moralBonus:20 },

  { id:'metop_ahrpt', cat:'Weather & Earth Obs', name:'METOP AHRPT',
    nation:'EU/EUMETSAT', freq:'1701.3 MHz',
    modulation:'QPSK AHRPT 3.0 Mbps', equip:2,
    desc:'EUMETSAT METOP Advanced HRPT. 3 Mbps downlink from METOP-B/C polar orbiters.',
    rx:'3 Mbps QPSK requires a good dish and wideband receiver. High-quality AVHRR + instruments.',
    note:'METOP carries IASI infrared sounder, ASCAT scatterometer, GOME-2 ozone sensor and more.',
    xpBonus:32, moralBonus:22 },

  { id:'goes_hrit',  cat:'Weather & Earth Obs', name:'GOES HRIT',
    nation:'USA/NOAA', freq:'1694.1 MHz',
    modulation:'BPSK HRIT 927 kbps', equip:2,
    desc:'GOES-16/18 High Rate Information Transmission. Full disc imagery every 10 minutes.',
    rx:'60+ cm dish, RHCP feed, L-band LNA. Decode with goestools or SatDump.',
    note:'Full hemisphere images every 10 min. Mesoscale sectors every minute. Spectacular imagery.',
    xpBonus:35, moralBonus:25 },

  { id:'goes_grb',   cat:'Weather & Earth Obs', name:'GOES GRB',
    nation:'USA/NOAA', freq:'1686.6 MHz',
    modulation:'QPSK GRB 31.3 Mbps', equip:3,
    desc:'GOES Rebroadcast. Full-resolution ABI + GLM data at 31 Mbps. Professional ground station needed.',
    rx:'Large dish required. 31 Mbps throughput needs high-gain antenna and specialised decoder.',
    note:'The full-fat GOES data stream. Every ABI band at native resolution plus lightning mapper.',
    xpBonus:50, moralBonus:30 },

  { id:'goes_dcs',   cat:'Weather & Earth Obs', name:'GOES DCS',
    nation:'USA/NOAA', freq:'402.0-402.2 MHz uplink / L-band downlink',
    modulation:'FSK/BPSK', equip:1,
    desc:'GOES Data Collection System. Relays sensor data from ground platforms via GOES satellite.',
    rx:'DCS downlink at L-band. GOES-16 retransmits environmental sensor data from thousands of platforms.',
    note:'Hydrology stations, buoys, and weather sensors across the Americas report via DCS.',

  { id:'goes_cda',   cat:'Weather & Earth Obs', name:'GOES CDA Telemetry',
    nation:'USA/NOAA', freq:'1685.7 MHz',
    modulation:'BPSK S-band', equip:2,
    desc:'GOES Command and Data Acquisition telemetry. Satellite health and housekeeping data.',

  { id:'gk2a_lrit',  cat:'Weather & Earth Obs', name:'GK-2A LRIT',
    nation:'South Korea/KMA', freq:'1692.14 MHz',
    modulation:'BPSK LRIT 128 kbps', equip:2,
    desc:'KMA GEO-KOMPSAT-2A LRIT downlink. East Asia/Pacific geostationary weather satellite.',

  { id:'elektro_l', cat:'Weather & Earth Obs', name:'ELEKTRO-L GGAK-E',
    nation:'Russia', freq:'1691.0 MHz',
    modulation:'QPSK LRIT/HRIT', equip:2,
    desc:'Russian ELEKTRO-L geostationary weather satellite. GGAK-E geophysical monitoring instruments.',
    rx:'L-band downlink similar to Meteosat. Russian data format, SatDump decoder available.',
    note:'ELEKTRO-L2/3 cover Russia, Central Asia, Indian Ocean. 10-channel imager.',

  { id:'meteosat_wefax', cat:'Weather & Earth Obs', name:'Meteosat WEFAX',
    nation:'EU/EUMETSAT', freq:'1691.0 MHz',
    modulation:'FM WEFAX (legacy)', equip:1,
    desc:'Meteosat Weather Facsimile analogue broadcasts. Legacy service from Meteosat-7 Indian Ocean.',

  { id:'cloudsat',   cat:'Weather & Earth Obs', name:'CLOUDSAT Downlink',
    nation:'USA/NASA', freq:'8160 MHz X-band',
    modulation:'QPSK X-band 4 Mbps', equip:3,
    desc:'NASA CloudSat cloud-profiling radar satellite. 94 GHz radar instrument with X-band downlink.',

    note:'CloudSat 94 GHz radar profiles clouds vertically. Near end-of-life but still operating.',

  { id:'aqua_db',    cat:'Weather & Earth Obs', name:'Aqua Direct Broadcast',
    nation:'USA/NASA', freq:'8160 MHz X-band',
    modulation:'CCSDS X-band 15 Mbps', equip:3,
    desc:'NASA Aqua Earth observation satellite X-band direct broadcast. MODIS, AMSR-E, AIRS data.',

    note:'Aqua part of A-Train constellation. Water cycle instruments include MODIS and AMSR-2.',

  { id:'argos_adcs', cat:'Weather & Earth Obs', name:'ARGOS A-DCS',
    nation:'France/NOAA/EUMETSAT', freq:'401.65 MHz uplink',
    modulation:'PM/BPSK beacon', equip:0,
    desc:'ARGOS environmental data collection and animal tracking. Receives platform transmitters.',

  { id:'lrpt',       cat:'Weather & Earth Obs', name:'Low Rate Picture Transmission',
    nation:'Russia', freq:'137.1 / 137.9 MHz',
    modulation:'QPSK 72 kbps', equip:1,
    desc:'LRPT standard for VHF weather satellite imagery. Used by METEOR-M series.',
    rx:'QPSK at 72 kbps. Better quality than APT. Decode with SatDump or LRPT-Decoder.',
    note:'LRPT provides near-APT convenience with near-HRPT quality on VHF. The ideal amateur format.',

  { id:'hrd',        cat:'Weather & Earth Obs', name:'High Resolution Data (HRD)',
    nation:'Various', freq:'8145 / 8160 MHz X-band',
    modulation:'QPSK X-band', equip:3,
    desc:'Generic High Resolution Data streams from Earth observation satellites via X-band.',

  // \u2550\u2550 AMATEUR & CUBESAT \u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550
  { id:'funcube1',   cat:'Amateur & CubeSat', name:'FUNcube-1 / AO-73',

  { id:'cas4a',      cat:'Amateur & CubeSat', name:'CAS-4A',
    nation:'China/CAMSAT', freq:'145.835 MHz downlink / 435.180 MHz uplink',
    modulation:'Linear SSB/CW transponder', equip:0,
    desc:'CAMSAT CAS-4A linear transponder CubeSat. FM and CW telemetry beacon.',
    rx:'145.835 MHz FM telemetry beacon. Linear transponder 435/145 MHz inverting.',
    note:'CAMSAT linear transponders use same operating techniques as Western amateur birds.',
    xpBonus:18, moralBonus:12 },

  { id:'cas4b',      cat:'Amateur & CubeSat', name:'CAS-4B',
    nation:'China/CAMSAT', freq:'145.910 MHz downlink / 435.280 MHz uplink',
    modulation:'Linear SSB/CW transponder', equip:0,
    desc:'CAMSAT CAS-4B. Companion to CAS-4A. Linear transponder and CW beacon.',

    note:'Launched together with CAS-4A. Provides redundancy and additional linear transponder capacity.',

  { id:'delfic3',    cat:'Amateur & CubeSat', name:'Delfi-C3',
    nation:'Netherlands/TU Delft', freq:'145.870 MHz',
    modulation:'BPSK 1200 bps', equip:0,
    desc:'Delft University CubeSat. Solar cell experiments + amateur transponder. Launched 2008.',

  { id:'horyu4',     cat:'Amateur & CubeSat', name:'HORYU-4',
    nation:'Japan/Kyushu Tech', freq:'437.375 MHz',
    modulation:'CW + AFSK 1200 bps AX.25', equip:0,

  { id:'aist2d',     cat:'Amateur & CubeSat', name:'AIST-2D',
    nation:'Russia/Samara', freq:'435.215 MHz',
    modulation:'BPSK 9600 bps', equip:0,
    desc:'Russian Samara University AIST-2D (RS-39S). Gravity gradient boom experiment + beacon.',

    note:'AIST series from Samara Space Center. Structural dynamics and attitude control experiments.',
    xpBonus:14, moralBonus:10 },

  { id:'amsat_p3d',  cat:'Amateur & CubeSat', name:'AMSAT-P3D / AO-40',
    nation:'AMSAT International', freq:'2401.323 MHz S2 / 435.848 MHz U',

  { id:'aprizesat',  cat:'Amateur & CubeSat', name:'AprizeeSat',
    nation:'USA', freq:'437.525 MHz',
    modulation:'FSK AX.25 9600 bps', equip:0,

    nation:'Russia', freq:'145.820 MHz',

  { id:'proba12',    cat:'Amateur & CubeSat', name:'PROBA-1/2',
    nation:'EU/ESA', freq:'2202.5 MHz / 8089.6 MHz',
    modulation:'S/X-band BPSK', equip:2,
    desc:'ESA PROBA technology demonstration satellites. Space weather instruments + X-ray imager.',

  { id:'qo100',      cat:'Amateur & CubeSat', name:'QO-100 / Es\'hail-2',
    nation:'Qatar/AMSAT', freq:'10489.5-10490.0 MHz downlink / 2400.0 MHz uplink',

  { id:'icarus',     cat:'Amateur & CubeSat', name:'ICARUS',
    nation:'Russia/Germany/DLR', freq:'401.18 MHz',
    modulation:'BPSK uplink beacon', equip:0,

  // \u2550\u2550 COMMERCIAL & IoT \u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550

    nation:'UK/Inmarsat', freq:'1530-1545 MHz downlink',

// \u2500\u2500 SDR requirement check \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
function hasSdrGear(level) {
  if (level === 0) return true;
  if (level === 1) return G.sdrItems && G.sdrItems.length > 0;
  if (level === 2) return G.sdrItems && (G.sdrItems.includes('airspy_mini') || G.sdrItems.includes('hackrf') || G.funCubeOwned);
  if (level === 3) return G.sdrItems && G.sdrItems.includes('hackrf');
  return false;
}

const SDR_REQ_LABELS = ['Any antenna', 'SDR dongle', 'Dish + LNA', 'Specialised hardware'];

// \u2500\u2500 SATELLITE MONITORING MAIN MENU \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
function satMonitorMenu(catFilter, page) {
  catFilter = catFilter || null;
  page = page || 0;
  const PAGE = 8;

  printBlank();
  printDivider();
  print('  \u2263 SATELLITE MONITORING STATION \u2263', 'header');
  printDivider();
  print('  Receive-only. No license required to listen.', 'system');
  printBlank();

  const log = G.satMonLog || [];
  print(`  Satellites monitored: ${log.length}  |  SDR gear: ${G.sdrItems && G.sdrItems.length > 0 ? 'YES' : 'None (some sats locked)'}`, 'amber');
  printBlank();

  if (!catFilter) {
    // Category select
    print('  Select category:', 'white');
    printBlank();
    const catChoices = SAT_MON_CATS.map(cat => {
      const items = SAT_MON.filter(s => s.cat === cat);
      const monitored = items.filter(s => log.some(l => l.id === s.id)).length;
      return {
        label: `${cat.padEnd(26)}  ${items.length} sats${monitored ? '  (' + monitored + ' logged)' : ''}`,
        fn: () => satMonitorMenu(cat, 0),
      };
    });
    catChoices.push({ label: 'Back to satellite menu', fn: satelliteMenu });
    printChoices(catChoices);
    return;
  }

  // Satellite list in category
  const items = SAT_MON.filter(s => s.cat === catFilter);
  const start = page * PAGE;
  const slice = items.slice(start, start + PAGE);

  print(`  Category: ${catFilter}`, 'header');
  printBlank();

  slice.forEach(sat => {
    const monitored = log.some(l => l.id === sat.id);
    const canRx = hasSdrGear(sat.equip);
    const cls = monitored ? 'success' : canRx ? 'white' : 'danger';
    const tag = monitored ? ' \u2611' : canRx ? '' : ` [need ${SDR_REQ_LABELS[sat.equip]}]`;
    print(`  ${sat.name.padEnd(26)}  ${sat.nation.padEnd(18)}  ${sat.freq.substring(0,20)}${tag}`, cls);
  });
  printBlank();

  const choices = slice.map(sat => ({
    label: `${sat.name}  (${sat.nation})`,
    fn: () => satMonitorDetail(sat, catFilter, page),
  }));

  const nav = [];
  if (page > 0) nav.push({ label: '\u25c4 Previous', fn: () => satMonitorMenu(catFilter, page - 1) });
  if (start + PAGE < items.length) nav.push({ label: 'Next \u25ba', fn: () => satMonitorMenu(catFilter, page + 1) });
  nav.push({ label: 'Back to categories', fn: () => satMonitorMenu(null, 0) });

  printChoices([...choices, ...nav]);
}

// \u2500\u2500 SATELLITE DETAIL VIEW \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
function satMonitorDetail(sat, cat, page) {
  printBlank();
  printDivider();
  print(`  \u2263 ${sat.name} \u2263`, 'header');
  print(`  ${sat.nation}`, 'system');
  printDivider();
  printBlank();
  print(`  Frequency:  ${sat.freq}`, 'amber');
  print(`  Modulation: ${sat.modulation}`, 'system');
  print(`  Equipment:  ${SDR_REQ_LABELS[sat.equip]}`, sat.equip > 1 ? 'danger' : 'system');
  printBlank();
  print(`  ${sat.desc}`, 'white');
  printBlank();
  print('  RECEIVE NOTES:', 'header');
  print(`  ${sat.rx}`, 'amber');
  printBlank();
  print('  TECHNICAL NOTES:', 'header');
  print(`  ${sat.note}`, 'system');
  printBlank();

  const canRx = hasSdrGear(sat.equip);
  const alreadyDone = (G.satMonLog || []).some(l => l.id === sat.id);

  if (!canRx) {
    print(`  \u2717 Requires: ${SDR_REQ_LABELS[sat.equip]}`, 'danger');
    print('  Visit the SDR Shop to acquire the necessary equipment.', 'system');
    printBlank();
    printChoices([
      { label: 'Visit SDR Shop', fn: sdrShop },
      { label: `Back to ${cat}`, fn: () => satMonitorMenu(cat, page) },
    ]);
    return;
  }

  printChoices([
    { label: alreadyDone ? 'Monitor again (already logged)' : 'Attempt to receive this satellite', fn: () => doSatMonitor(sat, cat, page) },
    { label: `Back to ${cat}`, fn: () => satMonitorMenu(cat, page) },
  ]);
}

// \u2500\u2500 SATELLITE MONITORING ATTEMPT \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
function doSatMonitor(sat, cat, page) {
  if (!G.satMonLog) G.satMonLog = [];

  printBlank();
  printDivider();
  print(`  \u2263 MONITORING: ${sat.name} \u2263`, 'header');
  printDivider();
  printBlank();

  // Success chances based on equipment + antenna
  const baseChance = sat.equip === 0 ? 0.75
    : sat.equip === 1 ? 0.65
    : sat.equip === 2 ? 0.55
    : 0.35;
  const bonus = (G.antenna * 0.03) + (G.radio * 0.02);
  const success = Math.random() < Math.min(0.95, baseChance + bonus);

  // Category-specific receive messages
  const rxMsgs = {
    'Navigation Constellations': [
      `Locking onto ${sat.name} GNSS signal...`,
      `GNSS signal acquired at ${sat.freq}. PDOP: 1.3. Fix: 3D.`,
      `${sat.name} signal solid. Pseudorange measurement active.`,
    ],
    'Weather & Earth Obs': [
      `Satellite pass beginning. ${sat.freq} signal building...`,
      `Signal locked. ${sat.modulation} demodulator synchronised.`,
      `Data frames incoming. Imagery / data capture in progress.`,
    ],
    'Amateur & CubeSat': [
      `AOS confirmed. Telemetry beacon audible on ${sat.freq}.`,
      `${sat.name} packet decoded. Doppler correction applied.`,
      `CubeSat downlink received. Frame integrity: GOOD.`,
    ],
    'Commercial & IoT': [
      `Commercial signal on ${sat.freq} detected and locked.`,
      `${sat.name} carrier identified. Signal analysis complete.`,
      `Data stream active. Commercial format recognised.`,
    ],
    'Military & Government': [
      `Signal detected on ${sat.freq}. Military allocation confirmed.`,
      `${sat.name} signature identified in monitoring database.`,
      `Encrypted government traffic detected. Metadata only.`,
    ],
    'Historic & Legacy': [
      `Scanning ${sat.freq} for legacy signal...`,
      `Historic satellite signal detected! Very faint but present.`,
      `${sat.name} signal confirmed. A ghost from the space age.`,
    ],
  };

  const msgs = rxMsgs[sat.cat] || rxMsgs['Historic & Legacy'];

  setTimeout(() => print(`  Pointing antenna... tuning to ${sat.freq}`, 'system'), 200);
  setTimeout(() => print(`  ${msgs[0]}`, 'amber'), 700);

  setTimeout(() => {
    if (success) {
      setTimeout(() => print(`  ${msgs[1]}`, 'success'), 300);
      setTimeout(() => print(`  ${msgs[2]}`, 'success'), 800);
      setTimeout(() => {
        printBlank();
        print(`  \u2713 SIGNAL CONFIRMED: ${sat.name}`, 'success');
        print(`  ${sat.rx}`, 'system');

        const alreadyLogged = G.satMonLog.some(l => l.id === sat.id);
        if (!alreadyLogged) {
          G.satMonLog.push({ id: sat.id, name: sat.name, freq: sat.freq,
            nation: sat.nation, cat: sat.cat, ts: new Date().toISOString() });
          gainXP(sat.xpBonus);
          G.morale = Math.min(100, G.morale + sat.moralBonus);
          print(`  +${sat.xpBonus} XP | Morale +${sat.moralBonus} | Added to monitor log.`, 'amber');
        } else {
          print('  Already in log \u2014 signal quality verified again.', 'system');
          gainXP(Math.floor(sat.xpBonus / 4));
        }
        autoSave();
        updateStats();
        setTimeout(() => satMonitorMenu(cat, page), 1600);
      }, 1200);
    } else {
      print('  Signal too weak or below horizon. No reception this attempt.', 'danger');
      print(`  Tip: ${sat.rx}`, 'system');
      updateStats();
      setTimeout(() => satMonitorDetail(sat, cat, page), 1200);
    }
  }, 1400);
}


// \u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550
// \u2500\u2500\u2500 SCANNER & TERRESTRIAL SIGNAL MONITOR \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
// \u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550

const SCAN_CATS = [
  'Voice Modes',
  'Trunking Systems',
  'Digital Mobile Radio',
  'Data & Telemetry',
  'Public Safety & Dispatch',
  'Scrambling & Encryption',
  'Aviation & Marine',
];

// equip: 0=any scanner, 1=SDR dongle, 2=capable SDR+decoder software, 3=specialist
const SCAN_SIGNALS = [

  // \u2550\u2550 VOICE MODES \u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550
  { id:'nfm_voice',   cat:'Voice Modes', name:'NFM Voice (FM Narrow)',
    nation:'Universal', freq:'Various VHF/UHF',
    modulation:'NFM \u00b12.5 kHz deviation', equip:0,
    desc:'Narrowband FM voice. The standard for public safety, amateur, and commercial VHF/UHF radio.',
    rx:'Any scanner or SDR with FM demodulator. 12.5 kHz channel spacing is now standard.',
    note:'NFM replaced WFM on most professional bands post-2013 narrowbanding mandate in the USA.',
    xpBonus:6, moralBonus:5, txOk:true },

  { id:'wfm_voice',   cat:'Voice Modes', name:'WFM Voice (FM Wide)',
    nation:'Universal', freq:'88-108 MHz (broadcast) / 29-50 MHz (legacy)',
    modulation:'WFM \u00b175 kHz deviation', equip:0,
    desc:'Wideband FM. Used for commercial broadcast and legacy low-band public safety.',
    rx:'Any FM radio or SDR. 200 kHz channel spacing for broadcast. Legacy 30 MHz band land mobile.',
    note:'WFM on 29-50 MHz legacy bands still active in rural areas for some agencies.',
    xpBonus:5, moralBonus:4, txOk:true },

  { id:'dstar',       cat:'Voice Modes', name:'D-STAR',
    nation:'Japan/IARU', freq:'144/430/1200 MHz',
    modulation:'GMSK 4800 bps', equip:1,
    desc:'Digital Smart Technologies for Amateur Radio. ICOM\'s amateur digital voice/data protocol.',
    rx:'GMSK demodulator + D-STAR codec. Decode with D-STAR decoder or SDR# + DSD+ plugin.',
    note:'Connects via repeater reflectors to worldwide DV gateway network. Open protocol.',
    xpBonus:14, moralBonus:10, txOk:true },

  { id:'fusion',      cat:'Voice Modes', name:'Yaesu System Fusion / C4FM',
    nation:'Japan/Yaesu', freq:'144/430 MHz',
    modulation:'C4FM FDMA 9.6 kbps', equip:1,
    desc:'Yaesu digital voice protocol. C4FM FDMA modulation. Wires-X internet linking.',
    rx:'C4FM demodulator + AMBE codec. Decode with DSD+ or YSFR (YSF Reflector client).',
    note:'Fusion repeaters support mixed analog/digital operation. Wires-X links worldwide.',
    xpBonus:12, moralBonus:10, txOk:true },

  { id:'voice_inv',   cat:'Voice Modes', name:'Voice Inversion Scrambling',
    nation:'Universal', freq:'Various VHF/UHF/HF',
    modulation:'Analogue frequency inversion', equip:0,
    desc:'Simple analogue voice privacy. Audio spectrum flipped around a centre frequency.',
    rx:'SDR with inversion decoder, or dedicated descrambler. Many scanner apps support this.',
    note:'NOT secure \u2014 descrambled trivially. Used for privacy, not encryption. Common on older LMR.',
    xpBonus:8, moralBonus:8, txOk:true },

  // \u2550\u2550 TRUNKING SYSTEMS \u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550
  { id:'p25',         cat:'Trunking Systems', name:'Project 25 (P25 / APCO-25)',
    nation:'USA/International', freq:'136-174 / 380-512 / 764-869 MHz',
    modulation:'C4FM / CQPSK 9600 bps', equip:1,
    desc:'APCO Project 25 digital trunking standard. US public safety standard. Phase 1 and Phase 2.',
    rx:'SDR + DSD+ or Unitrunker + DSD. Control channel decoding reveals all talkgroup traffic.',
    note:'P25 Phase 2 uses H-DQPSK at 12 kbps for higher spectral efficiency. Widely deployed.',
    xpBonus:20, moralBonus:14, txOk:true },

  { id:'mpt1327',     cat:'Trunking Systems', name:'MPT 1327',
    nation:'UK/Europe', freq:'138-175 / 450-470 MHz',
    modulation:'FSK 1200 bps control + NFM voice', equip:1,
    desc:'UK Ministry of Post and Telecommunications trunked radio standard. Analogue trunking.',
    rx:'Control channel FSK at 1200 bps reveals channel assignments. Voice on assigned NFM channel.',
    note:'MPT 1327 dominated European trunking before TETRA. Still widespread in legacy systems.',
    xpBonus:14, moralBonus:10, txOk:true },

  { id:'tetra',       cat:'Trunking Systems', name:'TETRA',
    nation:'ETSI/Europe/International', freq:'380-400 / 410-430 / 450-470 / 870-876 MHz',
    modulation:'Pi/4-DQPSK TDMA 36 kbps', equip:2,
    desc:'Terrestrial Trunked Radio. European digital trunking standard. Encrypted group voice.',
    rx:'Pi/4-DQPSK demodulator + TETRA codec. Commercial TETRA decoders exist (SDRTrunk, Trunk Recorder).',
    note:'Used by police, fire, rail, utilities across Europe and 100+ countries. TEA1/2/3 encryption.',
    xpBonus:22, moralBonus:14, txOk:true },

  { id:'tetrapol',    cat:'Trunking Systems', name:'Tetrapol',
    nation:'France/International', freq:'Various VHF/UHF',
    modulation:'GMSK FDMA', equip:2,
    desc:'French trunking standard competing with TETRA. Used by French police (RUBIS network).',
    rx:'GMSK demodulation + Tetrapol codec. Less software support than TETRA. Specialist hardware.',
    note:'RUBIS (French police) and INPT networks. Proprietary Airbus Defence & Space system.',
    xpBonus:16, moralBonus:10, txOk:true },

  { id:'motorola_t2', cat:'Trunking Systems', name:'Motorola Type II (SmartNet)',
    nation:'USA/Motorola', freq:'136-174 / 406-512 / 806-870 MHz',
    modulation:'FSK 3600 bps control + analog voice', equip:1,
    desc:'Motorola SmartNet/SmartZone Type II analogue trunking. Dominant US system 1985-2000s.',
    rx:'Unitrunker decodes Type II control channel. Assign scanner to follow talkgroups.',
    note:'Type II introduced digital ID codes for talkgroup tracking. Analogue voice on working channels.',
    xpBonus:14, moralBonus:10, txOk:true },

  { id:'edacs',       cat:'Trunking Systems', name:'EDACS (Enhanced Digital Access)',
    nation:'USA/GE/Ericsson', freq:'138-174 / 406-512 MHz',
    modulation:'FSK 9600 bps control + NFM voice', equip:1,
    desc:'GE/Ericsson Enhanced Digital Access Communications System. 9600 bps trunking protocol.',
    rx:'Unitrunker + EDACS plugin decodes control channel. Trunk-following scanner needed for voice.',
    note:'EDACS EA (Extended Addressing) supports 65535 talkgroups. Rare but still deployed.',
    xpBonus:14, moralBonus:10, txOk:true },

  { id:'ltr',         cat:'Trunking Systems', name:'Logic Trunked Radio (LTR)',
    nation:'USA/EF Johnson', freq:'138-174 / 406-512 MHz',
    modulation:'FSK 300 bps control + NFM voice', equip:1,
    desc:'EF Johnson Logic Trunked Radio. Distributed control \u2014 each repeater carries its own data.',
    rx:'Scanner with LTR trunking support. LTR control data embedded in voice channel subaudible.',
    note:'No dedicated control channel \u2014 trunking data rides on every repeater simultaneously.',
    xpBonus:12, moralBonus:8, txOk:true },

  { id:'iden',        cat:'Trunking Systems', name:'iDEN (Integrated Digital Enhanced Network)',
    nation:'USA/Motorola', freq:'806-821 / 851-866 MHz',
    modulation:'M16QAM TDMA 64 kbps', equip:2,
    desc:'Motorola Integrated Digital Enhanced Network. Nextel network. PTT + cellular data.',
    rx:'M16QAM TDMA demodulator. Largely defunct after Sprint/Nextel shutdown. Residual signals.',
    note:'iDEN shut down by Sprint 2013. Some private systems still operate. PTT beloved by users.',
    xpBonus:12, moralBonus:8, txOk:true },

  { id:'opensky',     cat:'Trunking Systems', name:'OpenSky',
    nation:'USA/Harris', freq:'769-775 / 799-805 MHz',
    modulation:'VSELP TDMA proprietary', equip:2,
    desc:'Harris Corporation OpenSky proprietary digital trunking. Used by some US public safety.',
    rx:'Proprietary format \u2014 limited decoder support. Harris-specific hardware for full decode.',
    note:'OpenSky competed with P25 and TETRA in 700 MHz band. Limited market penetration.',
    xpBonus:10, moralBonus:6, txOk:true },

  { id:'nxdn',        cat:'Trunking Systems', name:'NXDN',
    nation:'Japan/ICOM+Kenwood', freq:'136-174 / 400-512 MHz',
    modulation:'4-level FSK FDMA 6.25 kHz', equip:1,
    desc:'Icom/Kenwood Next Generation Digital Narrowband. 6.25 kHz channel spacing.',
    rx:'NXDN demodulator + DSD+. NXDN trunking decodable with Unitrunker + DSD combination.',
    note:'NEXEDGE (Kenwood) and IDAS (ICOM) are NXDN implementations. Efficient 6.25 kHz channels.',
    xpBonus:14, moralBonus:10, txOk:true },

  // \u2550\u2550 DIGITAL MOBILE RADIO \u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550
  { id:'dmr',         cat:'Digital Mobile Radio', name:'Digital Mobile Radio (DMR)',
    nation:'ETSI/Universal', freq:'136-174 / 400-512 MHz',
    modulation:'4FSK TDMA 9.6 kbps per slot', equip:1,
    desc:'ETSI digital mobile radio standard. Two TDMA timeslots per 12.5 kHz channel.',
    rx:'4FSK demodulator + AMBE+ codec. DSD+, SDRTrunk, or Trunk Recorder + AMBE dongle.',
    note:'Tier II conventional. Tier III trunking. BrandMeister/DMR-MARC global networks.',
    xpBonus:18, moralBonus:12, txOk:true },

  { id:'dpmr',        cat:'Digital Mobile Radio', name:'dPMR (Digital PMR)',
    nation:'ETSI/Europe', freq:'446.1-446.2 MHz (dPMR446) + licensed bands',
    modulation:'4FSK FDMA 6.25 kHz', equip:1,
    desc:'ETSI Digital Private Mobile Radio. 6.25 kHz FDMA single-channel. Licence-free dPMR446.',
    rx:'4FSK demodulator. DSD+ supports dPMR decode. Very similar to NXDN in modulation.',
    note:'dPMR446 is licence-free in Europe (like PMR446 but digital). Low power 0.5W.',
    xpBonus:12, moralBonus:8, txOk:true },

  { id:'astro25',     cat:'Digital Mobile Radio', name:'Motorola ASTRO 25 HPD',
    nation:'USA/Motorola', freq:'136-174 / 380-512 / 764-870 MHz',
    modulation:'CQPSK / C4FM P25 Phase 2', equip:2,
    desc:'Motorola ASTRO 25 High Performance Data air interface. P25 Phase 2 implementation.',
    rx:'P25 Phase 2 demodulator. Unitrunker + DSD+ with P25 Phase 2 support.',
    note:'ASTRO 25 is Motorola\'s P25 platform. HPD adds high-speed packet data for APX radios.',
    xpBonus:18, moralBonus:12, txOk:true },

  { id:'securenet',   cat:'Digital Mobile Radio', name:'Motorola SECURENET',
    nation:'USA/Motorola', freq:'Various VHF/UHF',
    modulation:'DVP / DES encrypted analogue', equip:3,
    desc:'Motorola SECURENET encrypted voice. DVP (Digital Voice Protection) over analog channel.',
    rx:'Proprietary encryption \u2014 signal demodulable but voice unrecoverable without key.',
    note:'DVP-XL uses proprietary vocoder with hardware-key DES encryption. Requires Motorola hardware.',
    xpBonus:10, moralBonus:6, txOk:true },

  { id:'provoice',    cat:'Digital Mobile Radio', name:'ProVoice / EDACS ProVoice',
    nation:'USA/Ericsson', freq:'138-174 / 406-512 MHz',
    modulation:'EFR proprietary TDMA', equip:3,
    desc:'Ericsson/GE ProVoice digital voice on EDACS trunking channels. Proprietary vocoder.',
    rx:'Proprietary format. No public decoder available. Signal identifiable but voice unrecoverable.',
    note:'ProVoice used on EDACS systems wanting digital voice without P25. Now largely replaced.',
    xpBonus:8, moralBonus:6, txOk:true },

  // \u2550\u2550 DATA & TELEMETRY \u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550
  { id:'ais',         cat:'Data & Telemetry', name:'AIS (Automatic Identification System)',
    nation:'IMO/ITU', freq:'161.975 / 162.025 MHz',
    modulation:'GMSK FM 9600 bps HDLC', equip:0,
    desc:'Maritime Automatic Identification System. Ships broadcast position, course, speed, ID.',
    rx:'Any SDR with AIS decoder (rtl-ais, AIS-catcher). Free vessel tracking data from ships.',
    note:'Every vessel over 300 GT must carry AIS class A. Class B for smaller vessels. Free to receive.',
    xpBonus:14, moralBonus:12, txOk:true },

  { id:'ctcss',       cat:'Data & Telemetry', name:'CTCSS (Continuous Tone-Coded Squelch)',
    nation:'Universal', freq:'67.0-254.1 Hz subaudible',
    modulation:'Subaudible tone below 300 Hz', equip:0,
    desc:'PL tones. Subaudible squelch control. Prevents hearing unintended transmissions.',
    rx:'Any scanner. Enable CTCSS scan to identify tone in use on repeater or simplex channel.',
    note:'67 standard CTCSS tones from 67.0 to 254.1 Hz. DCS (Digital Code Squelch) is the digital variant.',
    xpBonus:8, moralBonus:6, txOk:true },

  { id:'mdc4800',     cat:'Data & Telemetry', name:'MDC-4800',
    nation:'USA/Motorola', freq:'Various VHF/UHF (burst)',
    modulation:'FSK 4800 bps burst', equip:0,
    desc:'Motorola MDC-4800 radio ID signalling. PTT ID, emergency alert, status, and call alert.',
    rx:'MDC decoder in many scanner apps. The brief data burst at start of each transmission.',
    note:'The familiar "boop-boop" at start of Motorola radio keying is MDC-4800 PTT ID.',
    xpBonus:8, moralBonus:6, txOk:true },

  { id:'rdlap',       cat:'Data & Telemetry', name:'RD-LAP (Radio Data Link Access Procedure)',
    nation:'USA/Motorola', freq:'806-870 MHz (SMRS/800 MHz)',
    modulation:'C4FM / GMSK 19.2 kbps', equip:1,
    desc:'Motorola mobile data protocol for MDT (Mobile Data Terminal) communications.',
    rx:'RD-LAP demodulator + protocol decoder. MDT traffic includes dispatch messages, query responses.',
    note:'RD-LAP carried licence plate queries, warrants checks, and dispatch on Motorola data networks.',
    xpBonus:12, moralBonus:8, txOk:true },

  { id:'reflex',      cat:'Data & Telemetry', name:'ReFLEX',
    nation:'USA/Motorola', freq:'929-932 MHz (US paging)',
    modulation:'FSK 4-level 25 kbps', equip:1,
    desc:'Motorola ReFLEX two-way paging protocol. Predecessor to modern push-to-talk and IoT.',
    rx:'FSK demodulator + ReFLEX decoder. Paging messages decodable in plain or encoded form.',
    note:'ReFLEX-50 (two-way) built on FLEX one-way paging. Motorola PageNet was largest operator.',
    xpBonus:10, moralBonus:6, txOk:true },

  { id:'lojack',      cat:'Data & Telemetry', name:'LoJack (Vehicle Tracking)',
    nation:'USA/UK', freq:'173.075 MHz',
    modulation:'FSK 5 bps low power', equip:0,
    desc:'LoJack stolen vehicle recovery system. Passive transceiver in vehicle activates on theft report.',
    rx:'173.075 MHz FSK burst. Very short transmission. Police cruisers have LoJack trackers.',
    note:'LoJack transmits only when law enforcement activates the stolen vehicle report.',
    xpBonus:10, moralBonus:8, txOk:true },

  { id:'uhf_vls',     cat:'Data & Telemetry', name:'UHF Vehicle Location System',
    nation:'Various', freq:'Various UHF',
    modulation:'FSK/BPSK GPS telemetry', equip:1,
    desc:'Proprietary vehicle location telemetry systems on UHF bands. Fleet tracking.',
    rx:'UHF data bursts with GPS coordinates. Proprietary protocols vary by vendor.',
    note:'Includes AVLS (Automatic Vehicle Location System) used by transit and public safety fleets.',
    xpBonus:8, moralBonus:6, txOk:true },

  { id:'ctcss_dcs',   cat:'Data & Telemetry', name:'DCS (Digital Code Squelch)',
    nation:'Universal', freq:'Subaudible data 134.4 Hz clock',
    modulation:'Digital BFSK subaudible', equip:0,
    desc:'Digital Code Squelch. 23-bit Golay encoded squelch codes. 104 standard codes.',
    rx:'Scanner with DCS support. Identify which DCS code is in use on a given channel.',
    note:'DCS complements CTCSS. Also called DPL (Digital Private Line) by Motorola.',
    xpBonus:6, moralBonus:5, txOk:true },

  // \u2550\u2550 PUBLIC SAFETY & DISPATCH \u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550
  { id:'autocab',     cat:'Public Safety & Dispatch', name:'Autocab MDT System',
    nation:'UK', freq:'Various VHF/UHF',
    modulation:'POCSAG / GPRS data link', equip:1,
    desc:'UK taxi dispatch Mobile Data Terminal system. Cab-to-office job dispatch via data radio.',
    rx:'POCSAG on VHF/UHF or GPRS cellular. Older analogue systems decodable with POCSAG decoder.',
    note:'UK-specific taxi dispatch platform. Similar to Datatec, Cordic, and other MDT systems.',
    xpBonus:8, moralBonus:6, txOk:true },

  { id:'gandalf_mdt', cat:'Public Safety & Dispatch', name:'Gandalf MDT',
    nation:'UK', freq:'Various UHF',
    modulation:'FSK MDT proprietary', equip:1,
    desc:'Gandalf Technologies Mobile Data Terminal. UK police and ambulance dispatch system.',
    rx:'FSK data link with proprietary framing. Partially decodable with FSK decoder.',
    note:'Gandalf MDT used by UK emergency services before transition to Airwave TETRA.',
    xpBonus:10, moralBonus:8, txOk:true },

  { id:'p25_trunked', cat:'Public Safety & Dispatch', name:'P25 Trunked Systems',
    nation:'USA/International', freq:'7/800 MHz public safety bands',
    modulation:'P25 Phase 1/2 trunking', equip:2,
    desc:'P25 trunking with multiple sites, voting, and simulcast. Full PSAP dispatch capability.',
    rx:'Unitrunker + DSD+ + AMBE dongle for voice. SDRTrunk is all-in-one. ScannerCast streams.',
    note:'Modern P25 systems use encryption for dispatch and tactical channels. Control channel readable.',
    xpBonus:22, moralBonus:14, txOk:true },

  { id:'stargate_hpd', cat:'Public Safety & Dispatch', name:'Motorola ASTRO 25 HPD Stargate',
    nation:'USA/Motorola', freq:'806-870 MHz',
    modulation:'P25 TDMA + packet data', equip:2,
    desc:'Motorola Stargate high-speed data service on P25 systems. LPD plates, warrants, mug shots.',
    rx:'P25 data channel decode requires ASTRO-aware decoder. Control channel reveals activity.',
    note:'HPD Stargate delivers dispatch-quality data to patrol cars. 28 kbps effective throughput.',
    xpBonus:16, moralBonus:10, txOk:true },

  // \u2550\u2550 SCRAMBLING & ENCRYPTION \u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550
  { id:'dvp',         cat:'Scrambling & Encryption', name:'DVP / DVP-XL Scrambling',
    nation:'USA/Motorola', freq:'Various VHF/UHF',
    modulation:'DVP proprietary voice scramble', equip:3,
    desc:'Motorola Digital Voice Protection scrambling. DVP and DVP-XL variants.',
    rx:'Signal receivable but voice unrecoverable. Proprietary Motorola encryption key required.',
    note:'DVP uses a key-variable algorithm. DVP-XL is a stronger variant. Both Motorola proprietary.',
    xpBonus:6, moralBonus:5, txOk:true },

  { id:'p25_des',     cat:'Scrambling & Encryption', name:'P25 DES/AES Encrypted',
    nation:'USA', freq:'Various P25 system frequencies',
    modulation:'P25 + DES-OFB / AES-256', equip:3,
    desc:'P25 digital voice with DES or AES encryption. Talkgroup traffic encrypted end-to-end.',
    rx:'Control channel readable. Traffic channels show as encrypted voice. Key required for audio.',
    note:'AES-256 P25 is unbreakable with public resources. DES is deprecated but still in use.',
    xpBonus:6, moralBonus:5, txOk:true },

  { id:'securenet_dvp', cat:'Scrambling & Encryption', name:'SECURENET / DVP Digital Voice',
    nation:'USA/Motorola', freq:'Various VHF/UHF',
    modulation:'DVP + DES encrypted', equip:3,
    desc:'Full SECURENET with encrypted digital voice. Motorola analogue-channel digital encryption.',
    rx:'Identifiable from RF signature. Content unrecoverable without Motorola keyloader and CIK.',
    note:'SECURENET uses hardware-key management. KEK (Key Encryption Key) protects traffic keys.',
    xpBonus:6, moralBonus:4, txOk:true },

  // \u2550\u2550 AVIATION & MARINE \u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550
  { id:'vdl_m2',      cat:'Aviation & Marine', name:'VDL Mode 2 (VDL-M2)',
    nation:'ICAO/International', freq:'136.900 / 136.925 MHz',
    modulation:'D8PSK 31.5 kbps VHF datalink', equip:1,
    desc:'ICAO VHF Data Link Mode 2. Digital ACARS replacement. Aircraft text messaging system.',
    rx:'D8PSK demodulator + VDL-M2 decoder. Free VDL-M2 decoder software available.',
    note:'Carries ACARS messages, ATIS, digital clearances, and ATC datalink. Global coverage.',
    xpBonus:14, moralBonus:10, txOk:true },
  // ══ SCIENTIFIC & RESEARCH RADAR ══════════════════════════════════════════
  { id:'ghadir',      cat:'Scientific & Research Radar', name:"Ghadir OTH Radar (Iran)",
    nation:'Iran/IRGC', freq:'8-40 MHz HF',
    modulation:'FSK/FMCW over-the-horizon pulse', equip:1,
    desc:"Iranian Ghadir OTH radar. Targets naval vessels in Gulf/Arabian Sea. Reported 3000 km range.",
    rx:'HF SDR wide-band sweep. Distinctive FMCW ramp signature on lower HF.',
    note:"Ghadir means 'capable' in Farsi. Believed operational since 2014.",
    xpBonus:20, moralBonus:14, txOk:true },

  { id:'kontayner',   cat:'Scientific & Research Radar', name:"29B6 'Kontayner' OTH Radar",
    nation:'Russia', freq:'3-30 MHz HF',
    modulation:'Multi-pulse OTH skywave', equip:1,
    desc:"Russian 29B6 Kontayner OTH radar. Kovylkino site, covers Europe/Middle East. High PRF bursts.",
    rx:'HF receiver, look for rhythmic wideband pulse bursts spanning 0.5-1 MHz.',
    note:'Kontayner replaced the Duga/Woodpecker. Far less disruptive to amateur bands.',
    xpBonus:20, moralBonus:14, txOk:true },

  { id:'codar_scan',  cat:'Scientific & Research Radar', name:'CODAR (Coastal Ocean Dynamics Radar)',
    nation:'USA/International', freq:'4.4-50 MHz HF',
    modulation:'FMCW chirp ocean surface scatter', equip:1,
    desc:'CODAR SeaSonde HF radar. Measures ocean surface currents. FMCW chirp with ionospheric clutter.',
    rx:'HF SDR. Characteristic FMCW chirp sweeping ~50-200 kHz bandwidth. Many coastal sites worldwide.',
    note:'CODAR data is freely available online. Used for search & rescue and tsunami warning.',
    xpBonus:16, moralBonus:10, txOk:true },

  { id:'cmor_scan',   cat:'Scientific & Research Radar', name:'CMOR (Canadian Meteor Orbit Radar)',
    nation:'Canada/UWO', freq:'17.45 / 29.85 / 38.04 MHz',
    modulation:'Pulsed meteor scatter HF radar', equip:2,
    desc:'Canadian Meteor Orbit Radar at U of Western Ontario. Detects meteor head echoes on 3 frequencies.',
    rx:'CW receiver on 17.45 or 29.85 MHz. Brief pings from meteor ionisation trails.',
    note:'CMOR tracks thousands of meteors per day. Data feeds into meteor shower forecasting.',
    xpBonus:18, moralBonus:14, txOk:true },

  { id:'digisonde_scan',cat:'Scientific & Research Radar', name:'Digisonde Ionospheric Sounder',
    nation:'International/NOAA', freq:'1-30 MHz HF (swept)',
    modulation:'Swept-frequency chirp sounder', equip:1,
    desc:'Digital ionosonde. Sweeps 1-30 MHz measuring ionosphere. NOAA/UMLCAR network worldwide.',
    rx:'HF SDR. Distinctive chirp sweeping across entire HF band every few minutes.',
    note:'Real-time ionograms at digisonde.com. Predicts MUF for amateur propagation.',
    xpBonus:14, moralBonus:10, txOk:true },

  { id:'ionosonde_scan',cat:'Scientific & Research Radar', name:'Ionosonde (Generic Chirpsounder)',
    nation:'International', freq:'2-30 MHz (swept)',
    modulation:'FMCW or pulsed ionospheric sounder', equip:0,
    desc:'Generic ionospheric chirpsounder. Sweeps entire HF spectrum measuring E/F layer height.',
    rx:'Any HF receiver. The classic chirp sound sweeping upward every 15 minutes.',
    note:'Many variants: Digisonde, Ionosondes, Lowell DPS. All produce ionograms.',
    xpBonus:10, moralBonus:8, txOk:true },

  { id:'pluto2_scan',  cat:'Scientific & Research Radar', name:'PLUTO II OTH Radar (France)',
    nation:'France', freq:'Various HF OTH',
    modulation:'OTH skywave surveillance radar', equip:2,
    desc:'French PLUTO II OTH radar. Operated by French Navy for Atlantic surveillance.',
    rx:'HF receiver. French OTH pulse patterns on lower HF frequencies.',
    note:'Successor to the original PLUTO system. Part of French maritime surveillance network.',
    xpBonus:18, moralBonus:12, txOk:true },

  { id:'podsolnukh_scan',cat:'Scientific & Research Radar', name:"Podsolnukh 'Sunflower' Surface Radar",
    nation:'Russia', freq:'3-30 MHz HF surface wave',
    modulation:'FMCW HF surface wave radar', equip:2,
    desc:'Russian Podsolnukh (Sunflower) over-the-horizon surface-wave radar. Coastal surveillance.',
    rx:'HF SDR on lower HF. Surface-wave FMCW, shorter range than skywave OTH but all-weather.',
    note:'Covers Caspian Sea and Baltic. Detects ships and low-flying aircraft to 400 km.',
    xpBonus:18, moralBonus:12, txOk:true },

  { id:'rothr_scan',   cat:'Scientific & Research Radar', name:'ROTHR (Relocatable OTH Radar)',
    nation:'USA/US Navy', freq:'5-28 MHz HF',
    modulation:'Doppler FMCW skywave HF', equip:1,
    desc:'US Navy Relocatable Over-the-Horizon Radar. Drug interdiction/air defence. 3 sites: TX, VA, PR.',
    rx:'HF receiver, 5-28 MHz. 160 kHz bandwidth FMCW burst, distinctive chirp-doppler sound.',
    note:'ROTHR primary mission is counter-drug surveillance over Caribbean/South America.',
    xpBonus:16, moralBonus:12, txOk:true },

  { id:'jorn_scan',    cat:'Scientific & Research Radar', name:'JORN (Jindalee OTH Radar, Australia)',
    nation:'Australia', freq:'5-32 MHz HF',
    modulation:'Doppler OTH skywave FMCW', equip:2,
    desc:'Australian Jindalee Operational Radar Network. 3 sites: Longreach, Laverton, Alice Springs.',
    rx:'HF SDR. Wide-bandwidth FMCW pulses on 5-32 MHz. Covers northern Australia/Indonesia.',
    note:'JORN covers 37,000 km² — one of largest surveillance areas of any radar system.',
    xpBonus:20, moralBonus:14, txOk:true },

  { id:'haarp_scan',   cat:'Scientific & Research Radar', name:'HAARP (HF Active Auroral Research)',
    nation:'USA/UAF', freq:'2.8-10 MHz (transmit) / broadband (observe)',
    modulation:'HF heating pulses / ionospheric scatter', equip:1,
    desc:'High Power Auroral Stimulation research facility, Gakona AK. 3.6 MW EIRP ionospheric heater.',
    rx:'HF receiver worldwide. HAARP schedules posted at haarp.gi.alaska.edu. Heating modulations.',
    note:'Run by Univ of Alaska Fairbanks. Open to public for scheduled experiments.',
    xpBonus:22, moralBonus:18, txOk:true },

  { id:'hipas_scan',   cat:'Scientific & Research Radar', name:'HIPAS (High Power Auroral Stimulation)',
    nation:'USA/UCLA', freq:'2.85 / 4.53 MHz',
    modulation:'HF ionospheric heater CW/mod', equip:1,
    desc:'UCLA High Power Auroral Stimulation facility near Fairbanks AK. Now decommissioned.',
    rx:'Historic signal. Operated 1986-2006 on 2.85 and 4.53 MHz. CW and modulated HF heating.',
    note:'Predecessor to HAARP. Early ELF generation experiments via ionospheric modulation.',
    xpBonus:18, moralBonus:14, txOk:true },

  { id:'oth_generic',  cat:'Scientific & Research Radar', name:'OTH Radar (Generic)',
    nation:'Various', freq:'4-30 MHz HF',
    modulation:'FMCW/pulsed OTH skywave', equip:0,
    desc:'Generic over-the-horizon radar signature. Wideband HF pulse or FMCW from unknown origin.',
    rx:'HF receiver, 4-30 MHz. Wideband rhythmic pulses or sweeping chirp. Multiple nations operate OTH.',
    note:'Can be from Russia, China, Australia, France, Iran, USA or other nations with OTH capability.',
    xpBonus:12, moralBonus:8, txOk:true },

  { id:'radar_like',   cat:'Scientific & Research Radar', name:'Radar-Like Signal (UNID)',
    nation:'Unknown', freq:'Various HF',
    modulation:'Pulsed or swept wideband signal', equip:0,
    desc:'Unidentified radar-like HF signal. Periodic pulse bursts — could be OTH, ionosonde, or sounder.',
    rx:'HF receiver. Periodic wideband bursts or sweeps. Log parameters and check ENIGMA databases.',
    note:'Report unidentified radars to UDXF or HFUnderground. Help identify mystery transmitters.',
    xpBonus:10, moralBonus:6, txOk:true },

  { id:'chinese_foghorn_scan',cat:'Scientific & Research Radar', name:"Chinese 'Foghorn' OTH Radar",
    nation:'China/PLA', freq:'4-20 MHz HF',
    modulation:'Pulsed OTH broadband burst', equip:1,
    desc:"Chinese OTH nicknamed 'Foghorn' by SWLs. Covers Pacific. Wideband pulsed bursts.",
    rx:'HF receiver, Pacific-facing orientations. Distinctive deep rhythmic foghorn-like pulses.',
    note:'Multiple Chinese OTH sites believed operational. Covers South China Sea and Pacific approaches.',
    xpBonus:18, moralBonus:12, txOk:true },

  { id:'chinese_oth_scan',cat:'Scientific & Research Radar', name:'Chinese 160kHz-Wide OTH Radar',
    nation:'China/PLA', freq:'8-25 MHz HF',
    modulation:'160 kHz bandwidth wideband pulse', equip:2,
    desc:'Chinese OTH radar variant using unusually wide 160 kHz bandwidth. Advanced beam-forming.',
    rx:'HF SDR minimum. 160 kHz wide pulse signature makes identification straightforward.',
    note:'Wideband OTH for high-resolution maritime surveillance. Highly disruptive to HF users.',
    xpBonus:20, moralBonus:14, txOk:true },

  // ══ RFI & INTERFERENCE SOURCES ═══════════════════════════════════════════
  { id:'elevator_scan', cat:'RFI & Interference Sources', name:'Elevator Motor Interference',
    nation:'Domestic/Commercial', freq:'Various HF/VHF',
    modulation:'Broadband interference from motor commutator', equip:0,
    desc:'Elevator drive motor EMI. DC motor commutator arcs produce broadband RFI during operation.',
    rx:'HF receiver with strong signal near building. Rhythmic crackling rises and falls.',
    note:'Worst offenders are old DC elevator motors. Modern VFD lifts produce SMPS noise instead.',
    xpBonus:6, moralBonus:3, txOk:true },

  { id:'errant_2m',    cat:'RFI & Interference Sources', name:'Errant Signal on 2m Ham Band',
    nation:'Unknown/Local', freq:'144-148 MHz',
    modulation:'Unknown — FM/SSB/data', equip:0,
    desc:'Unidentified signal on 2m amateur band. Could be illegal, unlicensed, or poorly filtered device.',
    rx:'2m FM/SSB receiver. Log frequency, modulation, and time. Report to local ARRL frequency coordinator.',
    note:'Illegal 2m signals often come from remote sensors, intercoms, or baby monitors drifting on-freq.',
    xpBonus:8, moralBonus:5, txOk:true },

  { id:'eth_powerline_scan',cat:'RFI & Interference Sources', name:'Ethernet Over Powerline (HomePlug)',
    nation:'Domestic', freq:'2-30 MHz HF',
    modulation:'OFDM multicarrier over mains wiring', equip:0,
    desc:'HomePlug/PLC adapters couple OFDM data onto household wiring. Severe HF RFI to 30 MHz.',
    rx:'HF receiver indoors. Comb of OFDM subcarriers every ~24 kHz, patterned noise floor rise.',
    note:'HomePlug AV uses 2-30 MHz notching out amateur bands — but notching often fails. Petition ITU.',
    xpBonus:6, moralBonus:3, txOk:true },

  { id:'rf_heat_scan', cat:'RFI & Interference Sources', name:'RF Heating & Welding Interference',
    nation:'Industrial', freq:'13.56 / 27.12 / 40.68 MHz ISM',
    modulation:'High-power CW ISM band heating', equip:0,
    desc:'Industrial RF heating and dielectric welding on ISM bands. Leaking RF causes severe QRM.',
    rx:'HF receiver near industrial area. Constant harsh buzzing on ISM bands. Very strong near source.',
    note:'ISM band RF heaters are legal but must be shielded. Non-compliant units destroy 10m/CB bands.',
    xpBonus:6, moralBonus:3, txOk:true },

  { id:'iranian_bubble_scan',cat:'RFI & Interference Sources', name:"Iranian 'Bubble' Jammer",
    nation:'Iran', freq:'6-22 MHz HF',
    modulation:'Wideband bubbling noise jammer', equip:0,
    desc:"Iranian HF jammer targeting BBC Persian, VOA Farsi, Radio Farda. Unmistakable bubbling audio.",
    rx:'HF receiver 7-22 MHz. Sounds like boiling water — distinctive gurgling roar. Strongest 9.0-12 MHz.',
    note:'Active since 1979. Used consistently against external Persian broadcasts. International outrage.',
    xpBonus:8, moralBonus:4, txOk:true },

  { id:'cuban_jam_scan',cat:'RFI & Interference Sources', name:'Cuban HF Jammer',
    nation:'Cuba', freq:'Various HF — targets 49/41/31m',
    modulation:'Broadband noise jammer / tone jammer', equip:0,
    desc:"Cuba's HF jammer targeting Radio Marti and other anti-Castro broadcasts to Cuba.",
    rx:'HF receiver on 49m/41m/31m shortwave bands. Loud white noise or tone stack over Voice of America.',
    note:'Cuban jamming has operated since 1985 targeting Radio Marti (1,000 kW). Now 24/7.',
    xpBonus:8, moralBonus:4, txOk:true },

  { id:'firedrake_scan',cat:'RFI & Interference Sources', name:"Chinese Firedrake Jammer",
    nation:'China/MOC', freq:'3-19 MHz HF',
    modulation:'Traditional Chinese music noise jammer', equip:0,
    desc:"China's jamming signal. Plays looped traditional Chinese music over Falun Gong and US broadcasts.",
    rx:'HF receiver 3-19 MHz. Music loop — instantly recognisable folk melody on top of target broadcast.',
    note:"Named 'Firedrake' (Huolong) by DXers. China jams RFA, VOA, BBC Chinese using this technique.",
    xpBonus:10, moralBonus:6, txOk:true },

  { id:'nk_noise_scan', cat:'RFI & Interference Sources', name:'North Korean Noise Jammer',
    nation:'North Korea/KCNA', freq:'Various HF',
    modulation:'Broadband noise / heterodyne jammer', equip:0,
    desc:"DPRK HF jamming targeting Radio Free Asia, VOA Korean, and Radio Free Chosun broadcasts.",
    rx:'HF receiver on 9-13 MHz Korean target frequencies. Broadband hash, sometimes heterodyne tones.',
    note:'DPRK jamming is inconsistent and reactive to specific broadcast schedules.',
    xpBonus:8, moralBonus:5, txOk:true },

  { id:'noise_hf_scan', cat:'RFI & Interference Sources', name:'Noise Over All HF Bands',
    nation:'Local/Unknown', freq:'2-30 MHz',
    modulation:'Broadband interference (SMPS/PLT/local)', equip:0,
    desc:'Generic broadband noise raising HF noise floor. Usually SMPS, PLT adapters, or local industrial.',
    rx:'HF receiver with S-meter. Raised noise floor S5+ across multiple bands simultaneously.',
    note:'Fix: ferrite chokes, common-mode filters, and locating the offending device by directionality.',
    xpBonus:4, moralBonus:2, txOk:true },

  { id:'one_beep_scan', cat:'Mystery & UNID Signals', name:"'One Beep' (UVB-76 type)",
    nation:'Russia (assumed)', freq:'4625 kHz USB',
    modulation:'Single periodic tone burst', equip:0,
    desc:"Mystery buzzer/beeper. Single short beep every few seconds on fixed frequency. Purpose unknown.",
    rx:'SSB receiver 4625 kHz. Single beep tone, quiet, periodic. Occasionally voice messages over it.',
    note:"Often confused with UVB-76 'Buzzer'. These single-beep variants may be propagation markers.",
    xpBonus:12, moralBonus:10, txOk:true },

  { id:'am_test_scan',  cat:'RFI & Interference Sources', name:'AM Test Signal (Carrier Only)',
    nation:'Various', freq:'Various',
    modulation:'Unmodulated AM carrier', equip:0,
    desc:'Unmodulated carrier — AM transmitter on air but carrying no audio. Often test or fault condition.',
    rx:'AM receiver. Strong carrier, zero modulation. Could be BC station, jammer, or reference source.',
    note:'Check scheduling databases. A dead carrier is often a transmitter left running empty.',
    xpBonus:4, moralBonus:2, txOk:true },

  // ══ MYSTERY & UNID SIGNALS ═══════════════════════════════════════════════
  { id:'f03_scan',     cat:'Mystery & UNID Signals', name:'F03 (Numbers Station)',
    nation:'Unknown/Intelligence', freq:'Various HF 4-18 MHz',
    modulation:'USB/AM FSK numbers station', equip:0,
    desc:'ENIGMA designation F03. Female voice reading number groups in English or Russian.',
    rx:'SSB receiver, scan 5-18 MHz. Scheduled transmissions. Listen for repeated number sequences.',
    note:'Numbers stations transmit OTP (one-time pad) cipher messages to field agents. Unencrypted carrier.',
    xpBonus:16, moralBonus:12, txOk:true },

  { id:'f07_scan',     cat:'Mystery & UNID Signals', name:'F07 (Numbers Station)',
    nation:'Unknown/Intelligence', freq:'Various HF',
    modulation:'RTTY/FSK numbers station', equip:0,
    desc:'ENIGMA F07. FSK automated number-group station. Believed Eastern European origin.',
    rx:'SSB/FSK decoder, scan HF bands. Machine-generated groups with sync header.',
    note:'F07 type stations often transmit at fixed times. Pattern analysis may reveal schedule.',
    xpBonus:14, moralBonus:10, txOk:true },

  { id:'p03_scan',     cat:'Mystery & UNID Signals', name:'P03 (Numbers Station)',
    nation:'Unknown/Intelligence', freq:'Various HF',
    modulation:'AM/USB number groups', equip:0,
    desc:'ENIGMA P03. Male voice Spanish-language numbers station. Regular schedules on 6-12 MHz.',
    rx:'AM/SSB receiver, Spanish broadcast bands. Listen for sets of 5-digit groups.',
    note:"Often called 'Atencion' station from its opening word. Cuban intelligence suspected.",
    xpBonus:14, moralBonus:10, txOk:true },

  { id:'p07_scan',     cat:'Mystery & UNID Signals', name:'P07 (Numbers Station)',
    nation:'Unknown/Intelligence', freq:'Various HF',
    modulation:'USB number groups with preamble', equip:0,
    desc:'ENIGMA P07. Numbers station with distinct interval signal before number-group transmission.',
    rx:'SSB receiver. Wait for interval signal (music or tone), then number groups follow.',
    note:'P07 interval signals vary by schedule. Some use morse ID, others use melody.',
    xpBonus:14, moralBonus:10, txOk:true },

  { id:'xsl_scan',     cat:'Mystery & UNID Signals', name:"Japanese 'Slot Machine' (XSL)",
    nation:'Japan/Unknown', freq:'4.17 MHz (historic)',
    modulation:'Rapid sequential tone bursts (FSK/DTMF-like)', equip:1,
    desc:"The 'Slot Machine' — rapid succession of DTMF-like tones, machine-gun cadence. Unknown purpose.",
    rx:'SSB receiver 4.17 MHz. Sounds like a rapid-fire slot machine payout — cascading tones.',
    note:'Still active as of 2024. Origin unconfirmed — Japanese Maritime Safety Agency suspected.',
    xpBonus:18, moralBonus:14, txOk:true },

  { id:'two_beeps_scan',cat:'Mystery & UNID Signals', name:'Two Beeps (UNID Beacon)',
    nation:'Unknown', freq:'Various HF',
    modulation:'Double periodic tone burst', equip:0,
    desc:'UNID beacon transmitting two short tones at regular intervals. Origin and purpose unknown.',
    rx:'SSB receiver. Two-tone ping every few seconds. Try multiple frequencies 3-10 MHz.',
    note:'May be time/propagation reference, military channel marker, or automated link-check beacon.',
    xpBonus:12, moralBonus:8, txOk:true },

  // ══ SELCALL & ALERTING ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
  { id:'ccir493_scan', cat:'Selcall & Alerting', name:'CCIR 493-4 Selcall',
    nation:'International/ITU', freq:'Various MF/HF',
    modulation:'10-tone FSK selective calling', equip:0,
    desc:'CCIR 493-4 — ITU standard 10-digit FSK selective calling system. Maritime and HF commercial use.',
    rx:'SSB receiver + Multipsk SELCALL decoder. 10 sequential tones identify called station.',
    note:'Forms basis of GMDSS DSC. Also used for NAVTEX alerting and coast radio station calling.',
    xpBonus:10, moralBonus:7, txOk:true },

  { id:'icao_selcal_scan',cat:'Selcall & Alerting', name:'ICAO Selcal (Aviation)',
    nation:'ICAO/International', freq:'HF aviation bands 2-18 MHz',
    modulation:'4-tone sequential audio code (SELCAL)', equip:0,
    desc:'ICAO Selcal — 4-tone sequential selective calling for HF aviation. Wakes crew radio automatically.',
    rx:'SSB receiver on HF aviation frequencies. 4-tone chime sequence 312-1479 Hz range.',
    note:'Each aircraft has unique 2-letter pair SELCAL code assigned by ARINC. Decoding services exist.',
    xpBonus:10, moralBonus:8, txOk:true },

  { id:'gmdss_dsc_scan',cat:'Selcall & Alerting', name:'GMDSS DSC (Digital Selective Calling)',

    desc:'Global Maritime Distress Safety System DSC. Automated digital distress/calling on 70ch and HF.',
    rx:'Marine VHF receiver + DSC decoder. Hear digital bursts on 156.525 MHz constantly.',
    note:'DSC distress alert transmits MMSI, GPS position, and nature of distress automatically.',
    xpBonus:12, moralBonus:9, txOk:true },

  { id:'multitone_paging_scan',cat:'Selcall & Alerting', name:'Multitone Paging',

    modulation:'FSK/CTCSS/tone sequential paging', equip:0,
    desc:'Multitone sequential paging system. Multiple audio tones address specific pagers or groups.',

    note:'Legacy paging tech but still used in hospitals, industry. Tone sequences identify receiver.',

  { id:'pocsag_scan',  cat:'Selcall & Alerting', name:'POCSAG Paging',
    nation:'International', freq:'Various VHF 138-174 MHz / UHF 450-470 MHz',

    rx:'FM receiver + rtl_433 or multimon-ng. 512/1200/2400 bps 2-FSK. Hospital and fire brigade pagers.',

    xpBonus:10, moralBonus:7, txOk:true },

  { id:'coquelet_scan', cat:'Selcall & Alerting', name:'Coquelet (French Selcall)',
    nation:'France', freq:'Various HF/VHF',
    modulation:'Sequential tone selcall system', equip:0,
    desc:'French Coquelet sequential tone selective calling system. Used by French diplomatic networks.',
    rx:'SSB receiver + Multipsk Coquelet decoder. Sequential audio tone burst identification.',
    note:'Coquelet-13 uses 13-tone variant. French embassy and government HF circuits.',

  { id:'mazielka_scan', cat:'Selcall & Alerting', name:'Mazielka / X06 (Russian Selcall)',
    nation:'Russia', freq:'Various HF military/diplomatic',
    modulation:'Russian sequential tone selcall', equip:1,
    desc:"Russian 'Mazielka' (X06) — sequential tone selcall for Russian military/diplomatic HF nets.",
    rx:'SSB receiver + Multipsk X06/Mazielka decoder. Tone sequences on Russian HF frequencies.',

  // ══ HF ARQ & PROTOCOL MODEMS ══════════════════════════════════════════════
  { id:'arq_e3',       cat:'HF ARQ & Protocol Modems', name:'ARQ-E / ARQ-E3',

    modulation:'Single-channel ARQ FSK 50/100 Bd', equip:1,
    desc:'ARQ-E and ARQ-E3 — single-channel ARQ variants. 50 or 100 baud FSK with error-retransmit.',

    note:'ARQ-E3 uses 3-character blocks for error checking. Precursor to more complex ARQ systems.',

    nation:'International', freq:'Various HF commercial',
    modulation:'2-ch ARQ FSK 242 Bd multiplex', equip:1,
    desc:'ARQ-M2/242 — 2-channel multiplexed ARQ at 242 Bd. European and Middle Eastern HF commercial.',

    note:'M2 means 2 data channels. 242 Bd vs 342 Bd variants offer different throughput/reliability.',

    desc:'ARQ-M2/342 — 2-channel ARQ at 342 Bd. Higher speed variant of the ARQ-M2 family.',
    rx:'SSB + Multipsk ARQ-M2/342. 450 Hz shift, 342 baud channels, duplex ARQ.',
    note:'342 Bd gives roughly 30% more throughput than 242 Bd with similar error resilience.',

  { id:'arq_m4_242',   cat:'HF ARQ & Protocol Modems', name:'ARQ-M4/242 (4-channel ARQ)',
    nation:'International', freq:'Various HF commercial',

    desc:'ARQ-M4/242 — 4-channel multiplexed ARQ at 242 Bd. High-throughput HF commercial data.',

    note:'4-channel multiplex allows multiple teletype circuits on a single HF channel.',
    xpBonus:14, moralBonus:8, txOk:true },

  { id:'arq_m4_342',   cat:'HF ARQ & Protocol Modems', name:'ARQ-M4/342 (4-channel ARQ)',
    nation:'International', freq:'Various HF commercial',
    modulation:'4-ch ARQ FSK 342 Bd multiplex', equip:1,
    desc:'ARQ-M4/342 — 4-channel ARQ at 342 Bd. Highest throughput of the ARQ-M family.',
    rx:'SSB + Multipsk ARQ-M4/342. Four 342 Bd FSK channels in parallel.',
    note:'M4/342 was cutting-edge HF data in the 1980s. Still heard on commercial HF circuits.',
    xpBonus:14, moralBonus:8, txOk:true },

  { id:'arq_n_scan',   cat:'HF ARQ & Protocol Modems', name:'ARQ-N (Narrow ARQ)',
    nation:'International', freq:'Various HF',
    modulation:'Narrow-shift ARQ FSK', equip:1,
    desc:'ARQ-N — narrow frequency-shift ARQ for congested HF bands. 170 Hz shift FSK with ARQ.',
    rx:'SSB + narrow FSK decoder. 170 Hz shift, ARQ retransmit on error.',
    note:'Narrow shift allows operation in congested spectrum. Used in European maritime HF nets.',
    xpBonus:10, moralBonus:6, txOk:true },

  { id:'arq6_90',      cat:'HF ARQ & Protocol Modems', name:'ARQ6-90 (6-tone ARQ)',
    nation:'International', freq:'Various HF commercial',
    modulation:'6-tone FSK ARQ 90 Bd', equip:1,
    desc:'ARQ6-90 — 6-tone MFSK ARQ at 90 baud. 6 tones provide better multipath rejection than 2-tone.',
    rx:'SSB + Multipsk ARQ6-90 decoder. 6 tones in ~900 Hz bandwidth.',
    note:'6-tone FSK trades bandwidth for multipath resistance on difficult HF paths.',
    xpBonus:12, moralBonus:7, txOk:true },

  { id:'arq6_98',      cat:'HF ARQ & Protocol Modems', name:'ARQ6-98 (6-tone ARQ 98 Bd)',
    nation:'International', freq:'Various HF commercial',
    modulation:'6-tone FSK ARQ 98 Bd', equip:1,
    desc:'ARQ6-98 — slightly faster 6-tone ARQ variant at 98 Bd. Close to 100 Bd standard.',
    rx:'SSB + Multipsk ARQ6-98 decoder. Similar to ARQ6-90, marginally higher baud rate.',
    note:'ARQ6-98 and ARQ6-90 coexist on many commercial HF circuits.',
    xpBonus:12, moralBonus:7, txOk:true },

  { id:'alis_scan',    cat:'HF ARQ & Protocol Modems', name:'ALIS (Automatic Link Set-up)',
    nation:'International/MIL', freq:'Various HF',
    modulation:'ALE/ARQ link establishment protocol', equip:1,
    desc:'Automatic Link Set-up — handshake protocol for automatic HF channel selection and link establishment.',
    rx:'SSB + ALIS decoder. Sounding and handshake bursts before data transfer.',
    note:'ALIS precursor to MIL-STD-188-141 ALE. Used in European government HF networks.',
    xpBonus:12, moralBonus:8, txOk:true },

  { id:'alis2_scan',   cat:'HF ARQ & Protocol Modems', name:'ALIS-2 (Improved ALIS)',
    nation:'International/MIL', freq:'Various HF',
    modulation:'Enhanced ALE link establishment', equip:1,
    desc:'ALIS-2 — improved version of ALIS with faster link establishment and better channel assessment.',

    xpBonus:12, moralBonus:8, txOk:true },

  { id:'autospec_scan',cat:'HF ARQ & Protocol Modems', name:'AUTOSPEC (Auto Sideband)',
    nation:'UK/Commercial', freq:'Various HF',
    modulation:'AUTOSPEC proprietary ISB FSK modem', equip:1,
    desc:'AUTOSPEC — British proprietary ISB (Independent Sideband) FSK data modem for HF.',

    nation:'International', freq:'Various HF',
    modulation:'Multi-carrier OFDM HF interactive protocol', equip:1,

  { id:'clover_i_scan',cat:'HF ARQ & Protocol Modems', name:'CLOVER-I (HAL Original)',
    nation:'USA/HAL Comm', freq:'HF amateur/commercial',
    modulation:'CLOVER-I 4-tone 62.5 Bd phase-amplitude', equip:1,

  { id:'clover2500_scan',cat:'HF ARQ & Protocol Modems', name:'CLOVER 2500',

  { id:'codan_scan',   cat:'HF ARQ & Protocol Modems', name:'Codan HF Data Modem',

  { id:'coher_cw_scan',cat:'HF ARQ & Protocol Modems', name:'Coherent CW (Phase-Lock CW)',

    modulation:'Phase-coherent OOK CW keying', equip:1,

  { id:'dup_arq_scan', cat:'HF ARQ & Protocol Modems', name:'DUP-ARQ',
    nation:'International', freq:'Various HF commercial',
    modulation:'Duplex ARQ FSK modem', equip:1,

  { id:'dup_arq2_scan',cat:'HF ARQ & Protocol Modems', name:'DUP-ARQ-2',

  { id:'dup_fec2_scan',cat:'HF ARQ & Protocol Modems', name:'DUP-FEC-2',
    nation:'International', freq:'Various HF commercial',
    modulation:'Duplex forward error correction FSK', equip:1,

    note:'FEC is preferred over ARQ for broadcast links where ACK is impossible.',
