    modulation:'OFDM digital voice 14-carrier HF', equip:0,
    desc:'FDMDV — 14-carrier OFDM digital voice for HF. Predecessor to modern FreeDV modes.',
    rx:'SSB + FDMDV decoder. 14 OFDM carriers carrying CODEC2 voice.',
    note:'FDMDV pioneered OFDM digital voice on HF. Evolved into FreeDV open standard.',
    xpBonus:12, moralBonus:9, txOk:true },

  // ══ VOICE MODES ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  { id:'cb_radio_scan',cat:'Voice Modes', name:'CB Radio (11m Citizens Band)',
    nation:'International', freq:'26.965-27.405 MHz (US) / 26.565-27.405 MHz (EU)',
    modulation:'AM/SSB voice 40 channels', equip:0,
    desc:'Citizens Band radio. 40 channels around 27 MHz. AM or SSB, 4W AM / 12W SSB legal max.',
    rx:'AM/SSB receiver on 27 MHz. Ch19 (27.185 MHz) US trucking, Ch40 SSB DX.',
    note:'CB is license-free in most countries. Ch9 emergency, Ch19 highway truckers.',
    xpBonus:6, moralBonus:5, txOk:true },

  // ══ DATA & TELEMETRY ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  { id:'hf_trading120_scan',cat:'Data & Telemetry', name:"HF Trading Link '120 Hz FMCW Idle'",
    nation:'Financial/International', freq:'Various HF',
    modulation:'120 Hz FMCW idle tone financial link', equip:1,
    desc:"Financial HF data link idle tone at 120 Hz FMCW. High-frequency trading firms use HF for speed.",
    rx:'SSB receiver + spectrum analyser. 120 Hz FMCW idle pattern on financial HF bands.',
    note:'HFT firms use HF radio for inter-market links where speed of light via fibre is too slow.',
    xpBonus:14, moralBonus:10, txOk:true },

  { id:'hf_trading350_scan',cat:'Data & Telemetry', name:"HF Trading Link '350 Hz Idle Tone'",
    nation:'Financial/International', freq:'Various HF',
    modulation:'350 Hz idle tone financial HF link', equip:1,
    desc:'Financial HF trading link idle tone at 350 Hz. Hedging and arbitrage market microstructure comms.',
    rx:'SSB receiver. Constant 350 Hz tone on financial circuit standby — watch HF spectrum analyser.',
    note:'Spread Networks, Vigilant Global use HF microwave for Chicago-NYC latency arbitrage.',
    xpBonus:14, moralBonus:10, txOk:true },

  { id:'hfdvl_scan',   cat:'Data & Telemetry', name:'HFDVL (HF Data & Voice Link)',
    nation:'International', freq:'Various HF',
    modulation:'Hybrid data/voice OFDM HF', equip:1,
    desc:'High Frequency Data and Voice Link — combined data/voice system over single HF channel.',
    rx:'SSB + HFDVL decoder. Hybrid mode combining voice and data in single 3 kHz allocation.',
    note:'HFDVL allows simultaneous voice and low-rate data on HF. Used in military comms.',
    xpBonus:12, moralBonus:8, txOk:true },

  { id:'hfdl_scan',    cat:'Aviation & Marine', name:'HFDL (HF Data Link)',
    nation:'ICAO/International', freq:'HF aviation 2-22 MHz (17 channels)',
    modulation:'HFDL DPSK 300-1800 bps ACARS', equip:0,
    desc:'HF Data Link — ICAO standard HF ACARS for long-range aircraft. 60+ ground stations globally.',
    rx:'SSB + PC-HFDL or DumpHFDL software. 17 HFDL channels. Listen for aircraft position reports.',
    note:'HFDL ground stations: Gander, Shanwick, San Francisco, Honolulu, others. Live on airframes.io.',
    xpBonus:14, moralBonus:11, txOk:true },

  { id:'hfgcs_scan',   cat:'Data & Telemetry', name:'HFGCS (HF Global Comms System)',
    nation:'USA/USAF', freq:'4724/6739/8967/11175/13200/15016 kHz',
    modulation:'USB voice + EAM digital message', equip:0,
    desc:'USAF High Frequency Global Communications System. Emergency Action Messages (EAM) to STRATCOM.',
    rx:'SSB USB receiver. Key frequencies 11175 kHz, 8992 kHz. Listen for EAM broadcasts.',
    note:"EAMs begin 'Foxtrot' or phonetic preamble, then encoded message groups. Numbers station cousin.",
    xpBonus:18, moralBonus:14, txOk:true },

  { id:'nfc_scan',     cat:'Data & Telemetry', name:'NFC (Near Field Communication)',
    nation:'International/ISO', freq:'13.56 MHz ISM',
    modulation:'ISO 14443/15693 inductive NFC', equip:1,
    desc:'Near Field Communication at 13.56 MHz. Contactless card payments, transit tickets, sharing.',
    rx:'Specialised NFC receiver/SDR at very close range (<10 cm). HF SDR at 13.56 MHz.',
    note:'NFC range is intentionally < 10 cm for security. Passive tags powered by reader field.',
    xpBonus:8, moralBonus:6, txOk:true },

  { id:'eurobalise_scan',cat:'Data & Telemetry', name:'Eurobalise Train Beacon',
    nation:'Europe/Alstom', freq:'27.095 MHz downlink / 4.234 MHz uplink',
    modulation:'Eurobalise FSK train-track datalink', equip:2,
    desc:'Eurobalise — European Train Control System track-to-train data beacon. ETCS safety critical.',
    rx:'Specialised SDR near railway track. 27 MHz downlink from transponder to train.',
    note:'Eurobalise is core of ETCS railway safety. Speed limits and track data uploaded to train.',
    xpBonus:14, moralBonus:10, txOk:true },

  { id:'chinese_30t_scan',cat:'HF ARQ & Protocol Modems', name:'Chinese 30-Tone OFDM Modem',
    nation:'China/PLA', freq:'Various HF Chinese military/diplomatic',
    modulation:'30-tone OFDM Chinese proprietary HF', equip:2,
    desc:'Chinese 30-tone OFDM HF modem. PLA military and diplomatic circuits. 30 OFDM subcarriers.',

    xpBonus:18, moralBonus:12, txOk:true },

  { id:'chinese_44_scan',cat:'HF ARQ & Protocol Modems', name:'Chinese 4+4 PSK Modem',

    modulation:'4+4 carrier PSK Chinese military HF', equip:2,

  { id:'chinese_64_scan',cat:'HF ARQ & Protocol Modems', name:'Chinese-64 MFSK',

  // ══ DATA & TELEMETRY — GENERAL ════════════════════════════════════════════
  { id:'chu_scan',     cat:'Data & Telemetry', name:"CHU (Canada Time Signal, Ottawa)",
    nation:'Canada/NRC', freq:'3330 / 7850 / 14670 kHz',
    modulation:'AM CW time ticks + AFSK data', equip:0,

    xpBonus:12, moralBonus:9, txOk:true },

  { id:'ale400_scan',  cat:'HF ARQ & Protocol Modems', name:'ALE-400 (2G ALE Burst)',
    nation:'International/Military', freq:'Various HF',
    modulation:'MIL-188-141A 8-tone ALE handshake', equip:0,
    desc:'ALE-400 — 2nd generation ALE burst per MIL-STD-188-141A. 8-tone handshake at 125 Bd.',
    rx:'SSB + PCALE or ALE decoder. Distinct 8-tone signature before HF link establishment.',
    note:'ALE-400 handshakes heard on military and government HF worldwide — channel sounding.',
    xpBonus:12, moralBonus:9, txOk:true },

  { id:'ale_2g_scan',  cat:'HF ARQ & Protocol Modems', name:'2G ALE (MIL-STD-188-141A)',
    nation:'USA/NATO', freq:'Various HF military',
    modulation:'8-tone 125 Bd ALE link establishment', equip:0,
    desc:'2G ALE — second generation automatic link establishment per MIL-STD-188-141A standard.',
    rx:'SSB + PCALE. 8-tone ALE handshakes on military HF. Sounding + calling + handshake.',
    note:'2G ALE automates HF channel selection. Thousands of stations worldwide operate ALE nets.',
    xpBonus:12, moralBonus:9, txOk:true },

  { id:'ale_3g_scan',  cat:'HF ARQ & Protocol Modems', name:'3G ALE (ARCS/MIL-STD-188-141B)',
    nation:'USA/NATO', freq:'Various HF military',
    modulation:'Wideband ALE ARCS channel scanning', equip:1,
    desc:'3G ALE — third generation ALE. MIL-STD-188-141B with wideband channel scanning (ARCS).',
    rx:'SDR + 3G ALE decoder. Wideband spectral scanning before link establishment.',
    note:'3G ALE is much faster than 2G — evaluates channels simultaneously rather than sequentially.',
    xpBonus:14, moralBonus:10, txOk:true },

  { id:'amss_scan',    cat:'Aviation & Marine', name:'AMSS (Amplitude Modulation Signalling)',
    nation:'ICAO/International', freq:'HF aviation bands',

    rx:'SSB + AMSS decoder. HF aviation frequencies 2-22 MHz. Aircraft reporting positions.',
    note:'AMSS provides oceanic ACARS when satellite link unavailable. SELCAL alert embedded.',

  { id:'8psk_scan',    cat:'HF ARQ & Protocol Modems', name:'8PSK (8-Phase Shift Keying)',
    nation:'International', freq:'Various HF',
    modulation:'8-state PSK phase modulation', equip:0,
    desc:'8PSK — 8-phase shift keying. 3 bits per symbol, high spectral efficiency for HF data.',
    rx:'SSB + SDR IQ + PSK decoder. 8-point constellation visible on IQ scope.',
    note:'8PSK used in PACTOR II/III, MIL-STD-188-110. Triple throughput vs BPSK at same bandwidth.',
    xpBonus:12, moralBonus:8, txOk:true },

  { id:'ascii_scan',   cat:'Data & Telemetry', name:'ASCII Data (HF Digital Text)',
    nation:'International', freq:'Various HF',
    modulation:'ASCII character FSK/PSK encoding', equip:0,
    desc:'ASCII-encoded digital text over HF. IRA/ASCII character set carried by FSK or PSK.',
    rx:'SSB + RTTY/ASCII decoder. 7-bit ASCII at various baud rates.',
    note:'ASCII replaced Baudot in modern HF data. IRA (International Reference Alphabet) = ASCII.',
    xpBonus:8, moralBonus:6, txOk:true },

    nation:'Netherlands/International', freq:'HF maritime',

  { id:'wmbus', cat:'ISM & Short Range', name:'WmBus (Wireless M-Bus)',
    nation:'Europe/EN13757', freq:'868.3 / 868.95 / 169 MHz',
    modulation:'FSK/GFSK Wireless M-Bus utility meter', equip:0,
    desc:'Wireless M-Bus (EN13757-4). Smart meter protocol for gas, water, heat, electricity.',
    rx:'RTL-SDR + wmbusmeters. 868 MHz FSK. Walk past buildings to decode utility data.',
    note:'wmbusmeters open-source decoder. Many EU utility meters transmit unencrypted.',
    xpBonus:12, moralBonus:8, txOk:true },

  { id:'openway_centron', cat:'ISM & Short Range', name:'OpenWay CENTRON Smart Meter',
    nation:'USA/Itron', freq:'910-920 MHz',
    modulation:'OFDM smart meter AMI', equip:1,
    desc:'Itron OpenWay CENTRON electricity smart meter. 910 MHz AMI network mesh.',
    rx:'SDR on 915 MHz + OpenWay decoder. Mesh network packets contain usage data.',
    note:'Itron meters dominate US market. FCC IDs visible on meter. RF always active.',
    xpBonus:12, moralBonus:7, txOk:true },

  { id:'wisun', cat:'ISM & Short Range', name:'Wi-SUN (Wireless Smart Utility Networks)',
    nation:'International/Wi-SUN Alliance', freq:'863-928 MHz',
    modulation:'IEEE 802.15.4g FSK/OFDM smart grid', equip:1,
    desc:'Wi-SUN Field Area Network. IEEE 802.15.4g smart grid mesh for utilities.',
    rx:'SDR on 900 MHz band. Wi-SUN mesh packets from smart meters and grid sensors.',
    note:'Wi-SUN is the protocol of choice for modern smart grid deployments globally.',
    xpBonus:12, moralBonus:8, txOk:true },

  { id:'edf_hnz', cat:'ISM & Short Range', name:'EDF HNZ (Heures Normales/Zoning)',
    nation:'France/EDF', freq:'175.000 kHz',
    modulation:'LF ripple control EDF load switching', equip:1,
    desc:'Electricite de France HNZ power-line telemetry. Load switching via LF carrier.',

    note:'HNZ controls off-peak water heaters, storage heaters across France. Linky smart meter successor.',
    xpBonus:10, moralBonus:7, txOk:true },

  { id:'lojack_sig', cat:'ISM & Short Range', name:'LoJack Vehicle Tracker',
    nation:'USA/LoJack', freq:'173.075 MHz',
    modulation:'FSK silent vehicle transponder 173 MHz', equip:1,
    desc:'LoJack stolen vehicle recovery transponder. Activates on police command via 173 MHz.',
    rx:'FM receiver on 173.075 MHz. FSK bursts only when activated by police request.',
    note:'LoJack is one-way — police activate, car transmits. 173 MHz penetrates buildings.',
    xpBonus:12, moralBonus:9, txOk:true },

  { id:'compulert', cat:'ISM & Short Range', name:'CompuLert (Paging/Alert System)',
    nation:'USA/CompuLert', freq:'VHF/UHF paging band',
    modulation:'FSK paging tone alert system', equip:0,
    desc:'CompuLert wireless alert/paging system. Used in fire stations and emergency services.',
    rx:'FM receiver on local paging frequencies. CompuLert uses tone-sequential activation.',
    note:'CompuLert competes with Plectron for fire/EMS alerting. Local frequency assignment.',
    xpBonus:8, moralBonus:6, txOk:true },

  { id:'eu_baby_monitor', cat:'ISM & Short Range', name:'EU Baby Monitor (49/863/2400 MHz)',
    nation:'Europe', freq:'49 / 863-868 / 2400 MHz',
    modulation:'FM/DECT/2.4GHz audio baby monitor', equip:0,
    desc:'European baby monitor. Older units on 49 MHz FM; modern: DECT or 2.4 GHz.',
    rx:'FM receiver on 49.830 MHz or DECT scanner. Older units wide open — audio heard.',
    note:'49 MHz baby monitors are unencrypted FM — classic scanner demo. DECT is encrypted.',
    xpBonus:6, moralBonus:5, txOk:true },

  // ══ METEOROLOGICAL & SOUNDING (additions) ════════════════════════════════
  { id:'rs41_sg', cat:'Meteorological & Sounding', name:'Vaisala RS41-SG Radiosonde',
    nation:'Finland/Vaisala', freq:'400.000-405.000 MHz',
    modulation:'4FSK 4800 baud radiosonde', equip:0,
    desc:'Current Vaisala standard radiosonde. 4FSK 4800 Bd. GPS position, PTU sensors.',
    rx:'SDR + RS decoder (rs1729 or SondeMonitor). 400-406 MHz sweep for active launches.',
    note:'Most common modern radiosonde. RS41 replaced RS92. decode with auto_rx or rp.',
    xpBonus:14, moralBonus:11, txOk:true },

  { id:'rs80_vaisala', cat:'Meteorological & Sounding', name:'Vaisala RS80 (Legacy)',
    nation:'Finland/Vaisala', freq:'1680 MHz',
    modulation:'4PSK 1680 MHz legacy radiosonde', equip:1,
    desc:'Legacy Vaisala RS80. 1680 MHz 4PSK. Still used in some stations. Pre-GPS.',
    rx:'1.6 GHz SDR + RS80 decoder. Some stations still launch on 1680 MHz.',
    note:'RS80 predates GPS — uses omega/Loran nav. Being replaced by RS41 globally.',
    xpBonus:12, moralBonus:9, txOk:true },

  { id:'rs92_kl', cat:'Meteorological & Sounding', name:'Vaisala RS92-KL Radiosonde',
    nation:'Finland/Vaisala', freq:'400.000-406.000 MHz',
    modulation:'AFSK 1200 Bd GPS radiosonde', equip:0,
    desc:'Vaisala RS92-KL — AFSK 1200 Bd with GPS. Middle-generation Vaisala radiosonde.',
    rx:'SDR + SondeMonitor or rs1729. 400-406 MHz. AFSK audio tone decode.',
    note:'RS92 used AFSK audio encoding — noticeably different sound from RS41 4FSK.',
    xpBonus:12, moralBonus:9, txOk:true },

  { id:'rs92_sgp', cat:'Meteorological & Sounding', name:'Vaisala RS92-SGP Radiosonde',
    nation:'Finland/Vaisala', freq:'400.000-406.000 MHz',
    modulation:'AFSK 2400 Bd GPS radiosonde', equip:0,
    desc:'Vaisala RS92-SGP — improved SGP variant. 2400 Bd AFSK, GPS, dual PTU.',
    rx:'SDR + SondeMonitor. Slightly higher baud than KL variant. 400-406 MHz.',
    note:'SGP = Standard GPS Package. Higher data rate than -KL. Used globally 2005-2020.',
    xpBonus:12, moralBonus:9, txOk:true },

  { id:'dfm09', cat:'Meteorological & Sounding', name:'DFM-09 Radiosonde (Graw)',
    nation:'Germany/Graw', freq:'400.000-406.000 MHz',
    modulation:'OOK 400-406 MHz radiosonde', equip:0,
    desc:'Graw DFM-09 German radiosonde. OOK 400-406 MHz. GPS, compact design.',
    rx:'SDR + DFM decoder (rs1729). 400-406 MHz. OOK modulation — distinctive pattern.',
    note:'DFM-09 uses simple OOK unlike Vaisala FSK/AFSK. German Met Service standard.',
    xpBonus:12, moralBonus:9, txOk:true },

  { id:'dfm06', cat:'Meteorological & Sounding', name:'Graw DFM-06 Radiosonde',
    nation:'Germany/Graw', freq:'400.000-406.000 MHz',
    modulation:'OOK 400-406 MHz legacy radiosonde', equip:0,
    desc:'Earlier Graw DFM-06. Predecessor to DFM-09. OOK, no GPS (Omega nav).',
    rx:'SDR + DFM-06 decoder. 400-406 MHz. Similar signature to DFM-09.',
    note:'DFM-06 used Omega navigation — increasingly rare. DFM-09 largely replaced it.',
    xpBonus:12, moralBonus:8, txOk:true },

  { id:'dfm17', cat:'Meteorological & Sounding', name:'Graw DFM-17 Radiosonde',
    nation:'Germany/Graw', freq:'400.000-406.000 MHz',
    modulation:'OOK/FSK 400-406 MHz radiosonde', equip:0,
    desc:'Current Graw DFM-17. Improved DFM-09 successor. Better GPS, more sensors.',
    rx:'SDR + rs1729 or auto_rx. DFM-17 frames decoded by SondeMonitor.',
    note:'DFM-17 adds humidity sensor improvements over DFM-09. Still OOK-based.',

  { id:'m10_meteomodem', cat:'Meteorological & Sounding', name:'Meteomodem M10 Radiosonde',
    nation:'France/Meteomodem', freq:'400.000-406.000 MHz',
    modulation:'FSK 400-406 MHz French radiosonde', equip:0,

  { id:'pilotsonde_meteomodem', cat:'Meteorological & Sounding', name:'Meteomodem PilotSonde',
    nation:'France/Meteomodem', freq:'400.000-406.000 MHz',
    modulation:'FSK pilot balloon wind-finding sonde', equip:0,
    desc:'Meteomodem PilotSonde — wind-finding radiosonde for upper-level wind profiling.',

  { id:'m20_radiosonde', cat:'Meteorological & Sounding', name:'M20 Radiosonde',

  { id:'lms6_lockheed', cat:'Meteorological & Sounding', name:'Lockheed Martin LMS-6',

  { id:'horus4fsk', cat:'Meteorological & Sounding', name:'Horus Binary 4FSK v2',

  { id:'microsonde_mk2', cat:'Meteorological & Sounding', name:'Microsonde Mark II (LORAN-C)',

  { id:'vario_adc', cat:'Data & Telemetry', name:"'Vario' Airplane Data Controller",

  { id:'berlin_160', cat:'Data & Telemetry', name:'160.282 MHz Berlin (Unknown)',

  { id:'unknown_184', cat:'Mystery & UNID Signals', name:'184.320 MHz Unknown',
    nation:'Unknown', freq:'184.320 MHz',
    modulation:'Unknown modulation 184 MHz', equip:0,

    note:'184 MHz falls between VHF LO and VHF HI aviation bands. Report to UDXF.',
    xpBonus:10, moralBonus:8, txOk:true },

  { id:'crystal_osc', cat:'Mystery & UNID Signals', name:'Crystal Oscillator Harmonic?',
    nation:'Unknown/Local', freq:'Various (integer multiples of base freq)',
    modulation:'Unmodulated CW carrier (harmonic)', equip:0,
    desc:'Suspected crystal oscillator harmonic. Strong unmodulated carrier at unexpected frequency.',

    note:'Common crystals: 10, 12, 16, 24, 27, 48 MHz. Check multiples for unintended radiation.',
    xpBonus:8, moralBonus:6, txOk:true },

  { id:'unknown_173_blip', cat:'Mystery & UNID Signals', name:'Unknown 173 MHz Signal',
    nation:'Unknown', freq:'173 MHz band',
    modulation:'Unknown short burst 173 MHz', equip:0,
    desc:'Unidentified signal in 173 MHz area. Short bursts. Could be MURS, LoJack, or unknown.',
    rx:'NFM on 173 MHz. MURS channels: 151.820, 151.880, 151.940, 154.570, 154.600 MHz.',
    note:'173 MHz region contains MURS and LoJack (173.075). Identify by timing and modulation.',

  { id:'unknown_blips_447', cat:'Mystery & UNID Signals', name:'Unknown Blips 447.325 MHz',

    modulation:'Unknown short pulsed 447 MHz', equip:0,

    modulation:'Continuous wave doppler radar module', equip:2,

  { id:'magnetron', cat:'RFI & Interference Sources', name:'Magnetron (Microwave Oven)',
    nation:'Consumer', freq:'2450 MHz ISM',
    modulation:'Broadband noise 2.4 GHz pulsed CW', equip:1,

  // ══ AVIATION & MARINE (additions) ═══════════════════════════════════════
  { id:'gk2a_lrit', cat:'Aviation & Marine', name:'GK-2A LRIT (Low-Rate Image Transmission)',
    nation:'South Korea/KMA', freq:'1690.000 MHz',
    modulation:'BPSK 630 kbps geostationary satellite', equip:2,
    desc:'GeoKompsat-2A LRIT downlink. Korean Met Agency geostationary weather satellite.',
    rx:'1.69 GHz dish + LNA + SDR. BPSK 630 kbps. Real-time full-disc infrared/visible.',
    note:'GK-2A covers Asia-Pacific. Open reception with small dish and RTL-SDR.',
    xpBonus:20, moralBonus:15, txOk:true },

  { id:'inmarsat_isatm2m', cat:'Aviation & Marine', name:'Inmarsat IsatM2M',

  { id:'inmarsat_d_downlink', cat:'Aviation & Marine', name:'Inmarsat-D/D+ Downlink',

  // ══ RAILWAY & TRANSPORT ════════════════════════════════════════════════
  { id:'ptc_signal', cat:'Data & Telemetry', name:'PTC (Positive Train Control)',
    nation:'USA/FRA', freq:'220-222 MHz',
    modulation:'TDMA 220 MHz overlay 4FSK', equip:1,
    desc:'FRA-mandated Positive Train Control. 220 MHz TDMA overlay network for US freight/passenger.',
    rx:'SDR on 220-222 MHz. 4FSK data from wayside and locomotive transponders.',
    note:'PTC federally mandated after Chatsworth collision. 220 MHz licensed nationwide.',
    xpBonus:14, moralBonus:10, txOk:true },

  { id:'i_etms', cat:'Data & Telemetry', name:'I-ETMS (Interoperable Train Management)',
    nation:'USA/Wabtec', freq:'220-222 MHz',
    modulation:'I-ETMS TDMA 220 MHz digital train control', equip:1,
    desc:'Interoperable Electronic Train Management System. Wabtec PTC implementation.',
    rx:'SDR on 220-222 MHz. I-ETMS frames from locos and track equipment.',
    note:'I-ETMS is the dominant US PTC implementation. Used by BNSF, UP, Amtrak.',
    xpBonus:14, moralBonus:10, txOk:true },

  { id:'dpu_railroad', cat:'Data & Telemetry', name:'DPU (Distributed Power Unit)',
    nation:'USA/Rail', freq:'220-222 MHz / 160-161 MHz AAR',
    modulation:'AAR FSK DPU remote locomotive link', equip:1,
    desc:'Railroad Distributed Power Unit radio link. Remote control of rear locomotive via RF.',
    rx:'SDR on 160-161 MHz or 220 MHz. DPU commands from lead unit to slave locos.',
    note:'DPU allows long coal or grain trains to push from the rear via radio control.',
    xpBonus:12, moralBonus:8, txOk:true },

  { id:'eotd_signal', cat:'Data & Telemetry', name:'EOTD (End-of-Train Device)',
    nation:'USA/Rail', freq:'457.9375 / 452.9375 MHz',
    modulation:'FSK EOT/HOT railroad telemetry', equip:0,
    desc:'End-of-Train Device — broadcasts brake pipe pressure, motion, marker light from last car.',
    rx:'NFM on 457.9375 MHz. Short FSK bursts every 60 seconds. Decode with UniTrunker.',
    note:'EOT (also called FRED) sends brake pressure data to locomotive. Safety critical.',
    xpBonus:12, moralBonus:9, txOk:true },

  { id:'stp403_bus', cat:'Data & Telemetry', name:'STP403 Bus PIT System',
    nation:'UK/Various', freq:'UHF bus telemetry band',
    modulation:'FSK bus passenger information telemetry', equip:1,
    desc:'STP403 Bus Passenger Information Telemetry. Real-time bus location and PIT data.',

    note:'Bus PIT systems feed real-time tracking displays at stops and on apps.',
    xpBonus:10, moralBonus:7, txOk:true },

  { id:'broadcast_fm_whistle', cat:'Broadcast & Video', name:'Broadcast FM Whistling',
    nation:'Various', freq:'87.5-108 MHz FM band',
    modulation:'FM intermodulation whistle artefact', equip:0,
    desc:'Heterodyne whistle from two strong FM stations intermodulating in a receiver.',

    note:'Classic receiver overload artefact. The whistle is at |f1-f2| or |2f1-f2|.',
    xpBonus:4, moralBonus:3, txOk:true },

  { id:'aist2d',        cat:'Satellites & Space', name:'AIST 2D Telemetry',

  { id:'amsat_p3d',     cat:'Satellites & Space', name:'AMSAT-P3D (AO-40) Telemetry',

  { id:'noaa_itos',     cat:'Satellites & Space', name:'NOAA ITOS HRPT (Legacy)',
    nation:'USA/NOAA', freq:'1697.5 MHz (legacy)',
    modulation:'PM legacy HRPT 1970s weather sat', equip:2,

  { id:'squelch_one',   cat:'Satellites & Space', name:'One with Squelch (Satellite Beacon)',

  { id:'orbcomm',       cat:'Satellites & Space', name:'OrbComm LEO Telemetry',
    nation:'USA/OrbComm', freq:'137.000-138.000 MHz',
    modulation:'GMSK LEO store-forward telemetry', equip:0,

  { id:'orbcomm_telephony',cat:'Satellites & Space', name:'OrbComm Mobile Telephony',

    desc:'OrbComm two-way satellite messaging service for vehicles and assets.',

  { id:'otp2',          cat:'Satellites & Space', name:'OTP-2 Musical Beacon',

    modulation:'CW musical tone amateur beacon', equip:0,
    desc:'OTP-2 CubeSat — transmits a 16-note melody in Morse on 145.980 MHz.',

  { id:'proba12',       cat:'Satellites & Space', name:'PROBA 1/2 Downlink',
    nation:'Europe/ESA', freq:'2245 / 2250 MHz S-band',

  { id:'qo100_modem',   cat:'Satellites & Space', name:'QO-100 Narrowband Transponder',

  { id:'secor4',        cat:'Satellites & Space', name:'SECOR 4 (EGRS 4) Legacy Beacon',

  { id:'siriusxm',      cat:'Satellites & Space', name:'SiriusXM Satellite Radio',

  { id:'soyuz_tel',     cat:'Satellites & Space', name:'Soyuz-MS Spacecraft Telemetry',

  { id:'transit5b5',    cat:'Satellites & Space', name:'Transit 5B-5 (OSCAR Legacy)',

    modulation:'CW legacy Navy navigation satellite', equip:0,

  { id:'the_beeps',      cat:'Mystery & UNID Signals', name:'"The Beeps" (Pirate/UNID)',

  { id:'ssb_voice_scan',  cat:'Voice Modes', name:'Single Sideband Voice (SSB)',

  { id:'thales_sys3000', cat:'Scrambling & Encryption', name:'Thales Systeme 3000 Scrambler',

    modulation:'Soviet analog voice scrambler T-219', equip:1,
    desc:'Yachta T-219 Soviet/Russian HF voice scrambler. Rolling-code analog privacy.',
    rx:'SSB receiver. Soviet-era scrambled voice with characteristic tonal signature.',
    note:'T-219 used by Soviet merchant navy and inland waterway vessels. Still heard on HF.',
    xpBonus:14, moralBonus:11, txOk:true },

  // ══ SELCALL & ALERTING (additions) ══════════════════════════════════
  { id:'ccir_selcall',   cat:'Selcall & Alerting', name:'CCIR Selcall (5-Tone)',
    nation:'International/ITU', freq:'Various HF/VHF',
    modulation:'5-tone sequential selcall (CCIR)', equip:0,
    desc:'CCIR 5-tone sequential selective calling. The original analog selcall standard.',
    rx:'SSB/FM receiver + Multipsk CCIR-5 decoder. 5 sequential tones identify called station.',
    note:'CCIR 5-tone basis for most German and European public safety selcall systems.',
    xpBonus:10, moralBonus:7, txOk:true },

  { id:'zvei_selcall',   cat:'Selcall & Alerting', name:'ZVEI Selcall',
    nation:'Germany/ZVEI', freq:'Various VHF/UHF',
    modulation:'ZVEI 5-tone sequential selcall', equip:0,
    desc:'ZVEI (German Electrical Industry Association) 5-tone selcall. German fire/EMS standard.',
    rx:'FM receiver + ZVEI decoder. 5-tone alert before voice call. Standard German paging.',
    note:'ZVEI widely used in German Feuerwehr (fire brigades). 10 different tone sequences.',

  { id:'npas_scan',      cat:'Selcall & Alerting', name:'NPAS (National Public Alerting System)',
    nation:'Canada/Pelmorex', freq:'Various (EAS-compatible broadcast)',
    modulation:'EAS/SAME digital alert header', equip:0,
    desc:'Canadian NPAS public alerting via broadcast EAS. SAME header + alert audio.',

    note:'NPAS uses EAS SAME protocol. Wireless Emergency Alerts also issued simultaneously.',
    xpBonus:10, moralBonus:8, txOk:true },

  // ══ DATA & TELEMETRY (additions) ════════════════════════════════════
  { id:'dtmf_scan',      cat:'Data & Telemetry', name:'DTMF (Dual-Tone Multi-Frequency)',
    nation:'International/Bell', freq:'VoIP / PSTN audio band / HF/VHF RF',
    modulation:'DTMF 8-tone pair telephone signalling', equip:0,
    desc:'DTMF keypad tones. 8 frequencies in 4+4 matrix. Used for radio control and telephone.',
    rx:'Any audio receiver. 16 tones (0-9, A-D, *, #). DTMF decoders in most software.',
    note:'Amateur radio uses DTMF for autopatch, remote base, and repeater control.',
    xpBonus:8, moralBonus:7, txOk:true },

  { id:'distress_beacon', cat:'Data & Telemetry', name:'Distress Radiobeacon (Analog 121.5 MHz)',
    nation:'International/COSPAS-SARSAT', freq:'121.500 MHz / 243.000 MHz',
    modulation:'FM swept tone ELT/EPIRB distress beacon', equip:0,
    desc:'Analog distress beacon on 121.5/243 MHz. Sweeping FM tone. ELT, EPIRB, PLB legacy.',
    rx:'AM/NFM receiver on 121.500 MHz. Characteristic sweeping warble tone.',
    note:'COSPAS-SARSAT stopped monitoring 121.5 MHz in 2009. 406 MHz now mandatory.',
    xpBonus:12, moralBonus:10, txOk:true },

  { id:'euro_scan',      cat:'Data & Telemetry', name:'EURO (European Paging Standard)',
    nation:'Europe/ETSI', freq:'169.425 MHz (EU primary)',
    modulation:'2FSK 512 Bd EURO paging protocol', equip:0,
    desc:'EURO paging protocol — European wide-area radio paging on 169 MHz.',
    rx:'NFM + EURO pager decoder. 169.425 MHz. Used in France, Germany, Benelux.',
    note:'EURO paging delivers voice pages and short text. Still used in hospitals.',
    xpBonus:10, moralBonus:7, txOk:true },

  { id:'uk_amds',        cat:'Data & Telemetry', name:'UK AM Data System (UK-AMDS)',
    nation:'UK/Ofcom', freq:'MW AM broadcast subcarrier',
    modulation:'AMDS data subcarrier on MW AM broadcast', equip:0,
    desc:'UK AM Data System. Digital data modulated onto subcarrier of MW AM broadcasts.',

    note:'UK-AMDS carried traffic info, emergency alerts on AM before FM RDS takeover.',
    xpBonus:10, moralBonus:7, txOk:true },

  { id:'vdew_scan',      cat:'Data & Telemetry', name:'VDEW (German Utility Teleswitch)',
    nation:'Germany/VDEW', freq:'150.000 kHz LF',
    modulation:'LF ripple control German utility', equip:1,
    desc:'VDEW Rundsteuerung — German utility LF load-switching telemetry over powerlines.',
    rx:'LF receiver on 150 kHz. VDEW commands control off-peak heating and water heating.',
    note:'VDEW (German electricity industry assn) standardised LF ripple control across Germany.',
    xpBonus:10, moralBonus:7, txOk:true },

  { id:'mfj269c',        cat:'Data & Telemetry', name:'MFJ-269C Antenna Analyzer (Sweep)',
    nation:'USA/MFJ Enterprises', freq:'0.1-230 / 415-470 MHz',
    modulation:'Swept CW antenna analyzer emission', equip:0,
    desc:'MFJ-269C antenna analyzer. Sweeps 100 kHz-230 MHz measuring SWR. Unintended radiator.',
    rx:'HF/VHF SDR. Stepped carrier from analyzer sweeping. Visible as moving CW carrier.',
    note:'Antenna analyzers are low-power intentional radiators. Legal for antenna work only.',
    xpBonus:8, moralBonus:6, txOk:true },

  { id:'primary_radar',  cat:'Scientific & Research Radar', name:'Primary Aeronautical Surveillance Radar',
    nation:'International/ICAO', freq:'1030 / 2700-2900 MHz',
    modulation:'Pulsed primary surveillance radar', equip:1,
    desc:'Airport primary radar — rotating pulsed radar detecting aircraft skin echo.',
    rx:'Wideband SDR on L-band or S-band. Radar pulse trains visible as periodic wideband bursts.',
    note:'ASR-9 L-band or S-band depending on site. Rotating 12 RPM. No transponder needed.',
    xpBonus:14, moralBonus:10, txOk:true },

  { id:'radar_altimeter', cat:'Scientific & Research Radar', name:'Radar Altimeter (RALT)',
    nation:'Aviation/Various', freq:'4200-4400 MHz C-band',
    modulation:'FMCW radar altimeter 4.2-4.4 GHz', equip:2,
    desc:'Aircraft radar altimeter. FMCW 4.2-4.4 GHz measures exact height above terrain.',
    rx:'C-band SDR near airport. FMCW chirp from landing aircraft.',
    note:'5G C-band interference with radar altimeters caused aviation safety concerns 2022.',
    xpBonus:14, moralBonus:10, txOk:true },

  { id:'s_band_jammer',  cat:'RFI & Interference Sources', name:'S-Band Jammer',
    nation:'Military/Unknown', freq:'2000-4000 MHz S-band',
    modulation:'Wideband S-band jamming emission', equip:2,
    desc:'S-band electronic warfare jammer. Targets radar, datalinks, and navigation in S-band.',
    rx:'Wideband SDR on S-band. Broadband noise or swept signal disrupting normal signals.',
    note:'S-band used for weather radar, ATC radar, and satellite uplinks. High-value jamming target.',
    xpBonus:16, moralBonus:10, txOk:true },

  { id:'meteor_trails',  cat:'RFI & Interference Sources', name:'Ionized Meteor Trails',
    nation:'Natural/Atmospheric', freq:'VHF 40-80 MHz / 144 MHz',
    modulation:'Meteor scatter brief multipath burst', equip:0,
    desc:'Ionized meteor trail reflections. Brief bursts of distant VHF signals lasting ms to seconds.',

    note:'Meteor scatter enables 2000 km VHF QSOs in milliseconds. Digital modes exploit this.',
    xpBonus:14, moralBonus:13, txOk:true },

  // ══ LEGACY CELLULAR ════════════════════════════════════════════════
  { id:'nmt_1g',         cat:'Legacy Cellular & Mobile', name:'NMT 1G (Nordic Mobile Telephone)',
    nation:'Scandinavia/Nordic', freq:'450 / 900 MHz',
    modulation:'FM analog cellular NMT-450/900', equip:0,

  { id:'amps_1g',        cat:'Legacy Cellular & Mobile', name:'AMPS 1G (Advanced Mobile Phone)',

    desc:'AMPS — first North American cellular. Analog FM, 30 kHz channels. No encryption.',

  { id:'altai_radiotelephone', cat:'Legacy Cellular & Mobile', name:'Altai Radiotelephone System',

  { id:'sonne_scan2',    cat:'HF ARQ & Protocol Modems', name:'Sonne/CONSOL Navigation Beacon',

  { id:'duga_scan', cat:'Scientific & Research Radar', name:'Duga / Russian Woodpecker OTH',
    nation:'USSR/Russia', freq:'3.26-17.54 MHz (various)',
    modulation:'Pulsed OTH skywave 10 Hz woodpecker', equip:0,
    desc:'Soviet Duga OTH radar. 10 pulse/sec "woodpecker" covering North America threat approach.',

  { id:'voronezh77ya6', cat:'Scientific & Research Radar', name:'77Ya6 Voronezh OTH Radar',
    nation:'Russia/Roscosmos', freq:'3-30 MHz HF OTH',

    modulation:'Periodic RFI pulse from monitor sleep mode', equip:0,

    rx:'HF receiver. Short broadband pulse at monitor sleep transition. One-time event.',
    note:'Documented specific case. Any LCD monitor can emit RFI during power state changes.',
    xpBonus:6, moralBonus:3, txOk:true },
  { id:'displayport_rfi', cat:'RFI & Interference Sources', name:'DisplayPort Interference',
    nation:'Consumer Electronics', freq:'Various HF/VHF',
    modulation:'TMDS differential signal RFI from DP cable', equip:0,
    desc:'DisplayPort cable radiates harmonics of pixel clock. Broad RFI up to 500+ MHz.',
    rx:'HF/VHF SDR. Comb of carriers at pixel clock multiples. Worse with long cables.',
    note:'Ferrite clamp on DP cable often fixes. Shielded cable preferred.',
    xpBonus:6, moralBonus:3, txOk:true },
  { id:'hdmi_rfi_scan', cat:'RFI & Interference Sources', name:'HDMI RFI Clock Interference',
    nation:'Consumer Electronics', freq:'Various HF/VHF',
    modulation:'TMDS clock harmonic RFI from HDMI', equip:0,
    desc:'HDMI cable radiates TMDS clock harmonics. Strong RFI source at pixel clock multiples.',
    rx:'HF SDR. Characteristic comb pattern. Identify by turning HDMI device off/on.',
    note:'HDMI a major RFI source. Ferrite on cable and HDCP-compliant devices help.',
    xpBonus:6, moralBonus:3, txOk:true },
  { id:'google_pixel_rfi', cat:'RFI & Interference Sources', name:'Google Pixel Smartphone Interference',
    nation:'Google/Android', freq:'Various HF',
    modulation:'Broadband switching RFI from smartphone', equip:0,
    desc:'Google Pixel phones documented to emit HF RFI from switching regulators and display.',
    rx:'HF receiver. Varies with screen brightness and CPU load. Turn off/on to confirm.',
    note:'All modern smartphones emit some RFI. Pixel documented on various SDR forums.',
    xpBonus:6, moralBonus:3, txOk:true },
  { id:'samsung_wm_rfi', cat:'RFI & Interference Sources', name:'Samsung Washing Machine Interference',
    nation:'Samsung/Consumer', freq:'Various HF',
    modulation:'Broadband RFI from inverter motor washing machine', equip:0,
    desc:'Samsung (and other inverter) washing machines emit broadband HF RFI during spin.',
    rx:'HF receiver. Broadband noise increasing during spin cycle. Check by timing.',
    note:'Inverter motor washing machines known HF RFI sources. Common complaint on SDR forums.',
    xpBonus:6, moralBonus:3, txOk:true },
  { id:'hp_laptop_rfi', cat:'RFI & Interference Sources', name:'HP Laptop RFI',
    nation:'HP/Consumer', freq:'Various HF',
    modulation:'SMPS and display switching noise', equip:0,
    desc:'HP laptop switching power supply and display emit broadband HF RFI.',
    rx:'HF receiver near laptop. Varies with power state. Worse on mains vs battery.',
    note:'Laptop RFI worse when charging. USB-C PD chargers often worse than barrel chargers.',
    xpBonus:6, moralBonus:3, txOk:true },
  { id:'usb3_noise', cat:'RFI & Interference Sources', name:'High-Speed USB 3.0 Noise',
    nation:'Consumer Electronics', freq:'400-500 MHz region',
    modulation:'USB 3.0 SuperSpeed differential noise', equip:0,
    desc:'USB 3.0 SuperSpeed 5 Gbps data rate emits RFI around 400-500 MHz region.',
    rx:'SDR on 400-500 MHz. Plug/unplug USB 3.0 device to confirm. Characteristic warble.',
    note:'Documented by Intel as USB 3.0 interference issue to 2.4 GHz WiFi and 433 MHz.',
    xpBonus:8, moralBonus:4, txOk:true },
  { id:'smart_tv_rfi', cat:'RFI & Interference Sources', name:'Smart TV Interference',
    nation:'Consumer Electronics', freq:'Various HF',
    modulation:'SMPS, HDMI, processing RFI from Smart TV', equip:0,
    desc:'Modern Smart TVs combine HDMI, switching PSU, and SoC RFI in one powerful package.',
    rx:'HF SDR. Multiple interference types. Turn TV on/off/standby to observe changes.',
    note:'Smart TVs are among worst domestic RFI sources. Multiple simultaneous RFI mechanisms.',
    xpBonus:6, moralBonus:3, txOk:true },
  { id:'ac_mains_rfi', cat:'RFI & Interference Sources', name:'AC Mains Interference (50/60 Hz)',
    nation:'Universal', freq:'Broadband HF + harmonics',
    modulation:'Power line conducted noise 50/60 Hz', equip:0,
    desc:'AC mains harmonics and conducted interference. 50/60 Hz and multiples on HF.',
    rx:'HF receiver. 50 or 60 Hz buzz plus harmonics. Worst near unfiltered dimmers/motors.',
    note:'Common mode filtering on power supply input reduces mains-conducted interference.',
    xpBonus:4, moralBonus:2, txOk:true },
  { id:'induction_cooker_rfi', cat:'RFI & Interference Sources', name:'Induction Cooker Interference',
    nation:'Consumer/Kitchen', freq:'30 kHz - 10 MHz',
    modulation:'25-80 kHz working frequency + harmonics', equip:0,
    desc:'Induction cooker operates at 25-80 kHz. Harmonics reach into HF amateur bands.',
    rx:'HF receiver. Buzzing noise varying with power level. Worst on LF/MF/low HF.',
    note:'Induction cookers major LF/HF RFI source. Proximity matters. Not easily filtered.',
    xpBonus:6, moralBonus:3, txOk:true },
  { id:'atsc30_scan', cat:'Broadcast & Video', name:'ATSC 3.0 NextGen TV',
    nation:'USA/International', freq:'VHF/UHF TV bands',

  { id:'dab_scan', cat:'Broadcast & Video', name:'DAB (Digital Audio Broadcasting)',
    nation:'Europe/International', freq:'174-240 MHz VHF Band III / 1452-1492 MHz L-band',

  { id:'dabplus_scan', cat:'Broadcast & Video', name:'DAB+ (Digital Audio Broadcasting Plus)',
    nation:'Europe/International', freq:'174-240 MHz VHF Band III',

  { id:'dvbc_scan', cat:'Broadcast & Video', name:'DVB-C (Digital Video Broadcasting Cable)',

    rx:'AM receiver on VHF aviation band. Repeated every ~30 minutes. Letter identifier.',

    xpBonus:10, moralBonus:8, txOk:true },
  { id:'gbas_scan', cat:'Radionavigation & Radar', name:'GBAS (Ground-Based Augmentation)',
    nation:'ICAO/International', freq:'108-118 MHz VHF navigation',
    modulation:'VHF data broadcast GBAS LAAS', equip:1,
    desc:'GBAS/LAAS — local area GPS augmentation for Cat II/III precision approach.',
    rx:'VHF navigation receiver on 108-118 MHz. GBAS correction broadcasts near airports.',
    note:'GBAS replaces ILS for Category III landings with GPS. Also called LAAS.',
    xpBonus:14, moralBonus:10, txOk:true },
  { id:'ils_marker_scan', cat:'Radionavigation & Radar', name:'ILS Marker Beacon',
    nation:'ICAO/International', freq:'75.000 MHz',
    modulation:'ILS 75 MHz AM marker beacon outer/middle/inner', equip:0,
    desc:'ILS Marker Beacon system. Outer (400 Hz), Middle (1300 Hz), Inner (3000 Hz) markers.',
    rx:'AM receiver on 75.000 MHz. Listen for tone codes during airport approach.',
    note:'Markers activate cockpit indicator lights at specific distances from runway threshold.',
    xpBonus:10, moralBonus:8, txOk:true },
  { id:'ils_scan', cat:'Radionavigation & Radar', name:'ILS (Instrument Landing System)',
    nation:'ICAO/International', freq:'108-112 MHz VHF + 329-335 MHz UHF',
    modulation:'AM ILS localizer + glide slope', equip:0,
    desc:'ILS localizer (VHF) + glide slope (UHF) + marker beacons. Standard precision approach.',

    note:'ILS provides lateral + vertical guidance to runway. Category I/II/III ratings.',
    xpBonus:12, moralBonus:9, txOk:true },
  { id:'ndb_scan2', cat:'Radionavigation & Radar', name:'NDB (Non-Directional Beacon)',
    nation:'ICAO/International', freq:'190-1750 kHz MF/LF',
    modulation:'AM CW NDB navigational beacon', equip:0,
    desc:'Non-Directional Beacon. MF/LF AM CW with 2-3 letter Morse identifier.',
    rx:'AM receiver LF/MF. CW ident every 30 seconds. Used with ADF for bearing.',
    note:'NDB being decommissioned but many still active. Marine and aviation use.',
    xpBonus:10, moralBonus:8, txOk:true },
  { id:'dgps_scan2', cat:'Radionavigation & Radar', name:'DGPS (Differential GPS)',
    nation:'International/USCG', freq:'283.5-325 kHz MF',
    modulation:'MSK 200 Bd DGPS correction beacons', equip:0,
    desc:'DGPS MF beacon corrections. Sub-metre GPS accuracy via 283-325 kHz MSK broadcasts.',
    rx:'SDR on 283-325 kHz. MSK 200 Bd signal. Free corrections for maritime GPS.',

    xpBonus:12, moralBonus:9, txOk:true },
  { id:'quadcopter_tel', cat:'Data & Telemetry', name:'Quadcopter/Drone Telemetry',
    nation:'Consumer/Various', freq:'2.400-2.483 / 5.725-5.850 GHz',
    modulation:'FHSS/DSSS drone RC and telemetry', equip:1,
    desc:'Consumer drone control and telemetry. 2.4 and 5.8 GHz FHSS/DSSS frequency hopping.',
    rx:'Wideband SDR 2.4 or 5.8 GHz. Rapid frequency hopping visible as spread energy.',

    xpBonus:10, moralBonus:8, txOk:true },
  { id:'visonic_mct201', cat:'ISM & Short Range', name:'Visonic MCT-201 Alarm Sensor',
    nation:'Israel/Visonic', freq:'433.920 MHz',
    modulation:'OOK 433 MHz wireless alarm sensor', equip:0,
    desc:'Visonic MCT-201 wireless PIR alarm sensor. OOK 433 MHz. Fixed code.',

    note:'Visonic widely used in burglar alarms. rtl_433 decodes zone and status.',
    xpBonus:8, moralBonus:5, txOk:true },
  { id:'sigfox_scan', cat:'ISM & Short Range', name:'SIGFOX LPWAN',
    nation:'France/Sigfox', freq:'868 / 915 MHz',
    modulation:'UNB BPSK 100 bps SIGFOX IoT', equip:0,
    desc:'SIGFOX ultra-narrowband LPWAN. 192 Hz bandwidth BPSK. 140 messages/day max.',

    note:'SIGFOX competes with LoRa for LPWAN IoT. Very long range, very low data rate.',

  { id:'zigbee_scan', cat:'ISM & Short Range', name:'Zigbee (IEEE 802.15.4)',
    nation:'International/Zigbee Alliance', freq:'2.4 GHz / 868 / 915 MHz',

  { id:'nb_iot_scan', cat:'ISM & Short Range', name:'NB-IoT (Narrowband IoT)',
    nation:'3GPP/International', freq:'700/800/900 MHz LTE bands',

  { id:'wimax_scan', cat:'ISM & Short Range', name:'WiMAX (IEEE 802.16)',

  { id:'cdma420_scan', cat:'Legacy Cellular & Mobile', name:'CDMA420 (450 MHz CDMA)',

  { id:'natel_scan', cat:'Legacy Cellular & Mobile', name:'NATEL (Swiss 1G Cellular)',
    nation:'Switzerland/PTT', freq:'NMT 900 MHz',

  { id:'b_netz_scan', cat:'Legacy Cellular & Mobile', name:'B-Netz (German 1G Cellular)',
    nation:'Germany/Bundespost', freq:'148.8-155.9 MHz',
    modulation:'AM B-Netz German pre-cellular mobile', equip:0,

  { id:'c_netz_scan', cat:'Legacy Cellular & Mobile', name:'C-Netz (German 2G Analog)',
    nation:'Germany/Telekom', freq:'450.075-465.750 MHz',

  { id:'is54_damps', cat:'Legacy Cellular & Mobile', name:'IS-54 D-AMPS (Digital AMPS)',
    nation:'USA', freq:'824-894 MHz',

  { id:'cdma2000_scan', cat:'Legacy Cellular & Mobile', name:'CDMA2000 (3G Physical Layer)',

  { id:'baldr_weather', cat:'ISM & Short Range', name:'BALDR Weather Station Sensor',

    modulation:'OOK FSK 433 MHz weather sensor', equip:0,

  { id:'baofeng_gmsk_scan', cat:'Voice Modes', name:'Baofeng GMSK Digital Mode',

  { id:'cbet_scan', cat:'Data & Telemetry', name:'CBET (Canada Broadcast Emergency Telemetry)',

  { id:'mdc1200_cn_scan', cat:'Data & Telemetry', name:'Chinese/Asian MDC1200 Variant',

  { id:'rds_scan', cat:'Broadcast & Video', name:'RDS (Radio Data System)',
    nation:'Europe/International', freq:'87.5-108 MHz FM subcarrier',

  { id:'kiwi_scan', cat:'Amateur HF Digital Modes', name:'Kiwi (KiwiSDR Network Mode)',

  { id:'iscat_scan', cat:'Amateur HF Digital Modes', name:'ISCAT (Ionospheric Scatter)',
    nation:'Amateur/WSJT', freq:'144 MHz meteor scatter / 50 MHz',

  { id:'jt6m_scan', cat:'Amateur HF Digital Modes', name:'JT6M (6-meter Meteor Scatter)',

  { id:'q65_scan', cat:'Amateur HF Digital Modes', name:'Q65 (Incremental Frequency Keying)',

  { id:'msk144_scan', cat:'Amateur HF Digital Modes', name:'MSK144 (Meteor Scatter)',

  { id:'fsk441_scan', cat:'Amateur HF Digital Modes', name:'FSK441 (Meteor Scatter)',
    nation:'Amateur/WSJT', freq:'144/50 MHz meteor scatter',

  { id:'pi4_scan', cat:'Amateur HF Digital Modes', name:'PI4 (EME Calling Beacon)',

  { id:'thor_scan', cat:'Amateur HF Digital Modes', name:'THOR HF Digital Mode',
    nation:'Amateur/International', freq:'Various HF amateur',
    modulation:'MFSK THOR incremental keying', equip:0,

  { id:'throb_scan', cat:'Amateur HF Digital Modes', name:'THROB HF Digital Mode',
    nation:'Amateur/G3PPT', freq:'Various HF amateur',
    modulation:'MFSK THROB 9-tone HF mode', equip:0,

  { id:'psk2k_scan', cat:'Amateur HF Digital Modes', name:'PSK2K',

  { id:'superdarn_scan', cat:'Scientific & Research Radar', name:'SuperDARN (Super Dual Auroral Radar)',

// \u2500\u2500 CATEGORY PAGE SIZES \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
const SCAN_PAGE = 8;

// \u2500\u2500 SCANNER MAIN MENU \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
function scannerMenu(catFilter, page) {
  catFilter = catFilter || null;
  page = page || 0;

  printBlank();
  printDivider();
  print('  \u2263 SCANNER & TERRESTRIAL SIGNAL MONITOR \u2263', 'header');
  printDivider();
  print('  VHF/UHF monitoring. No TX required. Receive only.', 'system');
  printBlank();

  const log = G.scanLog || [];
  const sdrOwned = G.sdrItems && G.sdrItems.length > 0;
  print(`  Signals logged: ${log.length}  |  SDR hardware: ${sdrOwned ? G.sdrItems[0] : 'None'}`, 'amber');
  printBlank();

  if (!catFilter) {
    // Category select
    print('  Select signal category:', 'white');
    printBlank();
    const cats = SCAN_CATS.map(cat => {
      const items = SCAN_SIGNALS.filter(s => s.cat === cat);
      const logged = items.filter(s => log.some(l => l.id === s.id)).length;
      return {
        label: `${cat.padEnd(30)} ${items.length} signals${logged ? '  (' + logged + ' logged)' : ''}`,
        fn: () => scannerMenu(cat, 0),
      };
    });
    cats.push({ label: 'View Signal Log', fn: viewScanLog });
    cats.push({ label: 'Back to shack', fn: mainMenu });
    printChoices(cats);
    return;
  }

  // Signal list
  const items = SCAN_SIGNALS.filter(s => s.cat === catFilter);
  const start = page * SCAN_PAGE;
  const slice = items.slice(start, start + SCAN_PAGE);

  print(`  Category: ${catFilter}`, 'header');
  printBlank();

  slice.forEach(sig => {
    const logged = log.some(l => l.id === sig.id);
    const canRx = hasSdrGear(sig.equip);
    const cls = logged ? 'success' : canRx ? 'white' : 'danger';
    const tag = logged ? ' \u2611' : canRx ? '' : ` [need ${SDR_REQ_LABELS[sig.equip]}]`;
    print(`  ${sig.name.padEnd(30)}  ${sig.modulation.substring(0,22)}${tag}`, cls);
  });
  printBlank();

  const choices = slice.map(sig => ({
    label: `${sig.name}`,
    fn: () => scannerDetail(sig, catFilter, page),
  }));
  const nav = [];
  if (page > 0) nav.push({ label: '\u25c4 Previous', fn: () => scannerMenu(catFilter, page - 1) });
  if (start + SCAN_PAGE < items.length) nav.push({ label: 'Next \u25ba', fn: () => scannerMenu(catFilter, page + 1) });
  nav.push({ label: 'Back to categories', fn: () => scannerMenu(null, 0) });
  printChoices([...choices, ...nav]);
}

// \u2500\u2500 SIGNAL DETAIL \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
function scannerDetail(sig, cat, page) {
  printBlank();
  printDivider();
  print(`  \u2263 ${sig.name} \u2263`, 'header');
  printDivider();
  printBlank();
  print(`  Frequency:  ${sig.freq}`, 'amber');
  print(`  Modulation: ${sig.modulation}`, 'system');
  print(`  Nation:     ${sig.nation}`, 'system');
  print(`  Equipment:  ${SDR_REQ_LABELS[sig.equip]}`, sig.equip > 1 ? 'danger' : 'system');
  printBlank();
  print(`  ${sig.desc}`, 'white');
  printBlank();
  print('  HOW TO RECEIVE:', 'header');
  print(`  ${sig.rx}`, 'amber');
  printBlank();
  print('  NOTES:', 'header');
  print(`  ${sig.note}`, 'system');
  printBlank();

  const canRx = hasSdrGear(sig.equip);
  const alreadyDone = (G.scanLog || []).some(l => l.id === sig.id);

  if (!canRx) {
    print(`  \u2717 Requires: ${SDR_REQ_LABELS[sig.equip]}`, 'danger');
    print('  Visit the SDR Shop to acquire the necessary equipment.', 'system');
    printBlank();
    printChoices([
      { label: 'Visit SDR Shop', fn: sdrShop },
      { label: `Back to ${cat}`, fn: () => scannerMenu(cat, page) },
    ]);
    return;
  }

  printChoices([
    {
      label: alreadyDone ? 'Scan again (already logged)' : 'Tune in and monitor',
      fn: () => doScanMonitor(sig, cat, page),
    },
    { label: `Back to ${cat}`, fn: () => scannerMenu(cat, page) },
  ]);
}

// \u2500\u2500 MONITORING ATTEMPT \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
function doScanMonitor(sig, cat, page) {
  if (!G.scanLog) G.scanLog = [];
  printBlank();
  printDivider();
  print(`  \u2263 MONITORING: ${sig.name} \u2263`, 'header');
  printDivider();
  printBlank();

  const baseChance = [0.80, 0.70, 0.58, 0.35][sig.equip] || 0.70;
  const bonus = G.antenna * 0.02 + G.radio * 0.02;
  const success = Math.random() < Math.min(0.95, baseChance + bonus);

  // Category-flavoured receive messages
  const rxFlavour = {
    'Voice Modes': [
      `Tuning to ${sig.freq}...`,
      `Signal locked. ${sig.modulation} demodulator synchronised.`,
      `Audio decoding \u2014 voice traffic audible on ${sig.name}.`,
    ],
    'Trunking Systems': [
      `Scanning for ${sig.name} control channel on ${sig.freq}...`,
      `Control channel found! Decoding talkgroup assignments...`,
      `Following talkgroup traffic. ${sig.name} system activity confirmed.`,
    ],
    'Digital Mobile Radio': [
      `Looking for ${sig.name} activity on ${sig.freq}...`,
      `Digital carrier detected. ${sig.modulation} sync achieved.`,
      `Voice frames decoding. ${sig.name} traffic confirmed.`,
    ],
    'Data & Telemetry': [
      `Monitoring ${sig.freq} for ${sig.name} data...`,
      `Data burst detected and locked. Frame sync confirmed.`,
      `Decoding ${sig.name} packets. Data stream active.`,
    ],
    'Public Safety & Dispatch': [
      `Scanning public safety frequencies for ${sig.name}...`,
      `Dispatch traffic detected. ${sig.name} system active.`,
      `Following talkgroup activity. Dispatch and tactical traffic heard.`,
    ],
    'Scrambling & Encryption': [
      `Detecting ${sig.name} on ${sig.freq}...`,
      `Encrypted signal confirmed. Signal characteristics match ${sig.name}.`,
      `Traffic identified as ${sig.name}. Content inaccessible without key.`,
    ],
    'Aviation & Marine': [
      `Monitoring aviation/marine band: ${sig.freq}...`,
      `${sig.name} signal acquired. Protocol sync established.`,
      `Data decoded. ${sig.name} traffic active.`,
    ],
  };

  const msgs = rxFlavour[sig.cat] || rxFlavour['Voice Modes'];
  setTimeout(() => print(`  ${msgs[0]}`, 'system'), 200);
  setTimeout(() => print(`  ${msgs[1]}`, 'amber'), 700);

  setTimeout(() => {
    if (success) {
      setTimeout(() => print(`  ${msgs[2]}`, 'success'), 300);
      setTimeout(() => {
        printBlank();
        print(`  \u2713 SIGNAL CONFIRMED: ${sig.name}`, 'success');
        print(`  ${sig.rx}`, 'system');

        const alreadyLogged = G.scanLog.some(l => l.id === sig.id);
        if (!alreadyLogged) {
          G.scanLog.push({
            id: sig.id, name: sig.name, freq: sig.freq,
            cat: sig.cat, nation: sig.nation,
            ts: new Date().toISOString(),
          });
          gainXP(sig.xpBonus);
          G.morale = Math.min(100, G.morale + sig.moralBonus);
          print(`  +${sig.xpBonus} XP | Morale +${sig.moralBonus} | Added to scanner log.`, 'amber');
          if (sig.txOk) {
            if (!G.scanUnlocked) G.scanUnlocked = [];
            if (!G.scanUnlocked.find(u => u.id === sig.id)) {
              G.scanUnlocked.push({ id: sig.id, name: sig.name, freq: sig.freq, mod: sig.modulation, xpMult: (sig.xpBonus/10).toFixed(1) });
              print(`  ★ TX MODE UNLOCKED: ${sig.name} — now available in Select TX Mode!`, 'success');
            }
          }
        } else {
          gainXP(Math.floor(sig.xpBonus / 4));
          print('  Already logged \u2014 signal quality confirmed again.', 'system');
        }
        autoSave();
        updateStats();
        setTimeout(() => scannerMenu(cat, page), 1600);
      }, 1000);
    } else {
      print('  No signal found on this frequency right now.', 'danger');
      print(`  Tip: ${sig.rx}`, 'system');
      updateStats();
      setTimeout(() => scannerDetail(sig, cat, page), 1200);
    }
  }, 1400);
}

// \u2500\u2500 SCAN LOG VIEWER \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500
function viewScanLog(page) {
  page = page || 0;
  const PAGE = 12;
  const entries = G.scanLog || [];
  printBlank();
  printDivider();
  print('  \u2263 SCANNER LOG \u2263', 'header');
  printDivider();
  printBlank();

  if (entries.length === 0) {
    print('  No signals logged yet. Start scanning!', 'system');
    printBlank();
    printChoices([{ label: 'Back to scanner', fn: () => scannerMenu(null, 0) }]);
    return;
  }

  const start = page * PAGE;
  const slice = entries.slice(start, start + PAGE);
  print('  SIGNAL NAME                     CATEGORY              DATE', 'header');
  print('  \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500', 'divider');

  slice.forEach((e, i) => {
    const n = (start + i + 1).toString().padStart(3);
    const nm = (e.name || '').substring(0, 30).padEnd(30);
    const ct = (e.cat || '').substring(0, 22).padEnd(22);
    const dt = e.ts ? new Date(e.ts).toLocaleDateString() : '?';
    print(`  ${n}  ${nm} ${ct} ${dt}`, 'cyan');
  });

  printBlank();
  print(`  Page ${page + 1} of ${Math.ceil(entries.length / PAGE)}  |  ${entries.length} total`, 'system');
  printBlank();
  const nav = [];
  if (page > 0) nav.push({ label: '\u25c4 Previous', fn: () => viewScanLog(page - 1) });
  if (start + PAGE < entries.length) nav.push({ label: 'Next \u25ba', fn: () => viewScanLog(page + 1) });
  nav.push({ label: 'Back to scanner', fn: () => scannerMenu(null, 0) });
  printChoices(nav);
}

// ─── ELMER SESSION ────────────────────────────────────────
const elmers = [
  { name: 'W1OLD', advice: 'Son, the best antenna is the one that\'s up! A dipole in the air beats a beam in the basement every time.' },
  { name: 'K7VET', advice: 'CW is the soul of amateur radio. Learn it well and no natural disaster can silence you.' },
  { name: 'N5EXP', advice: 'During Field Day, remember: MORE ANTENNAS. Stake that wire into the ground and work 2A.' },
  { name: 'WB2MRS', advice: 'SWR below 2:1 and you\'re golden. Above 3:1, your finals will cry for mercy.' },
  { name: 'AG3I', advice: 'Log everything. Every QSO, every frequency, every RST. Your logbook is your legacy.' },
  { name: 'KA1HAM', advice: 'Check into your local club net. Emergency comm skills are built long before the storm hits.' },
];

function elmerSession() {
  printBlank();
  printDivider();
  print('  ≋ ELMER\'S CORNER ≋', 'header');
  printDivider();
  const elmer = elmers[Math.floor(Math.random() * elmers.length)];
  print(`  ${elmer.name} lights his pipe and says:`, 'amber');
  printBlank();
  print(`  "${elmer.advice}"`, 'white');
  printBlank();
  print('  You take notes in your logbook.', 'system');
  gainXP(10);
  G.morale = Math.min(100, G.morale + 10);
  updateStats();
  setTimeout(mainMenu, 1800);
}

// ─── CHARGE BATTERY ───────────────────────────────────────
function chargeBattery() {
  printBlank();
  print('  You connect the battery to the solar panel charger.', 'system');
  print('  Charging... ▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓ 100%', 'amber');
  G.battery = 100;
  updateStats();
  print('  Battery fully charged. Ready to operate!', 'success');
  setTimeout(mainMenu, 1000);
}

// ─── WIN CONDITION ────────────────────────────────────────
function checkWinCondition() {
  if (G.qsoCount >= 50 && G.license === 3) {
    winGame();
  } else if (G.qsoCount >= 50) {
    print('', 'system');
    print('  ★ 50 QSOs achieved! Now upgrade to Amateur Extra to win!', 'amber');
  }
}

function winGame() {
  printBlank();
  printDivider();
  print('  ████████████████████████████████████████', 'success');
  print('  ██                                    ██', 'success');
  print('  ██   CONGRATULATIONS, ' + G.callsign + '!   ██', 'success');
  print('  ██                                    ██', 'success');
  print('  ██   You have achieved the pinnacle:  ██', 'success');
  print('  ██   AMATEUR EXTRA CLASS OPERATOR     ██', 'success');
  print('  ██   with 50+ QSOs logged!            ██', 'success');
  print('  ██                                    ██', 'success');
  print('  ████████████████████████████████████████', 'success');
  printBlank();
  print('  The bands are yours. The world listens.', 'amber');
  print('  73, ' + G.callsign + ' de HAM QUEST. SK SK SK', 'white');
  G.awaitingInput = false;
}

// ============================================================
// INPUT HANDLING
// ============================================================
function handleInput(val) {
  val = (val || '').trim();
  if (!val) return;
  logDisplay.textContent = `[${G.callsign}]: ${val}`;
  if (G.inputMode === 'text' && G.inputCallback) {
    const cb = G.inputCallback;
    G.inputCallback = null;
    G.awaitingInput = false;
    cb(val);
    return;
  }
  if (G.inputMode === 'choice' && G.choices.length) {
    const num = parseInt(val);
    if (num >= 1 && num <= G.choices.length) {
      const choice = G.choices[num - 1];
      G.choices = [];
      G.awaitingInput = false;
      print(`  > ${choice.label}`, 'action');
      choice.fn();
    } else {
      print('  Invalid choice. Enter a number from the list.', 'danger');
    }
  }
}

input.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => {
  if (e.key !== 'Enter') return;
  const val = input.value.trim();
  input.value = '';
  handleInput(val);
});

// Click choices
output.addEventListener('click', (e) => {
  const el = e.target;
  if (!el.classList.contains('choice')) return;
  const text = el.textContent;
  const match = text.match(/\[(\d+)\]/);
  if (!match) return;
  const num = parseInt(match[1]);
  if (num >= 1 && num <= G.choices.length) {
    const choice = G.choices[num - 1];
    G.choices = [];
    G.awaitingInput = false;
    print(`  > ${choice.label}`, 'action');
    choice.fn();
    input.value = '';
  }
});


// ── MOBILE NUMPAD ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
function npPress(digit) {
  const inp = document.getElementById('input');
  inp.value = (inp.value || '') + digit;
}
function npBackspace() {
  const inp = document.getElementById('input');
  inp.value = inp.value.slice(0, -1);
}
function npClear() {
  document.getElementById('input').value = '';
}
function npSend() {
  const inp = document.getElementById('input');
  handleInput(inp.value);
  inp.value = '';
}
// ============================================================
// BOOT
// ============================================================
updateStats();
setTimeout(() => {
  print('  INITIALIZING HAM QUEST v1.0...', 'system');
  print('  Loading band conditions... OK', 'dim');
  print('  Loading propagation model... OK', 'dim');
  print('  Warming up finals... OK', 'dim');
  print('  Checking SWR... 1:1 PERFECT', 'success');
  printBlank();
  setTimeout(startIntro, 1200);
}, 300);

input.focus();
document.getElementById('terminal').addEventListener('click', () => input.focus());
</script>
</body>
</html>
